metric.summary: Type of form. Stored as bitvector. A set ith bit implies enum FormTypeNameForLogging's ith type was detected. Multiple types are possible.

metric.name: IsForCreditCard
metric.summary: True for credit card forms, false for address/profile forms. See |FormStructure::IsCompleteCreditCardForm()|.

event.name: Autofill.EditedAutofilledFieldAtSubmission
event.summary: Records when a user edits a value that was filled by autofill.

metric.name: FieldSignature
metric.summary: The signature of the field (mod 1021). This is the hash identifier used to denote this field for query and voting purposes. See components/autofill/core/common/signatures.cc for more details.

metric.name: FormSignature
metric.summary: The signature of the form (mod 1021). This is the hash identifier used to denote this form for query and voting purposes. See components/autofill/core/common/signatures.cc for more details.

metric.name: OverallType
metric.summary: Field's overall |FieldType|. See: autofill::FieldType

event.name: Autofill.FieldFillStatus
event.summary: Recorded when Chrome validates field type predictions (on submit, on leaving a form, on receiving a form with autocomplete attributes).

metric.name: FieldSignature
metric.summary: The signature of the field (mod 1021). This is the hash identifier used to denote this field for query and voting purposes. See components/autofill/core/common/signatures.cc for more details.

metric.name: FormSignature
metric.summary: The signature of the form (mod 1021). This is the hash identifier used to denote this form for query and voting purposes. See components/autofill/core/common/signatures.cc for more details.

metric.name: IsAutofilled
metric.summary: The field is currently autofilled.

metric.name: MillisecondsSinceFormParsed
metric.summary: Time since form parse.

metric.name: PredictionSource
metric.summary: The system which generated the prediction: heuristics, server, overall.

metric.name: ValidationEvent
metric.summary: The event which triggered the validation: Submission, Left Form, Form has Autocomplete attributes.

metric.name: WasPreviouslyAutofilled
metric.summary: The field was autofilled and subsequently edited.

event.name: Autofill.FieldTypeValidation
event.summary: Recorded when Chrome validates field type predictions (on submit, on leaving a form, on receiving a form with autocomplete attributes).

metric.name: ActualType
metric.summary: The observed type of the field. See: autofill::FieldType

metric.name: FieldSignature
metric.summary: The signature of the field

metric.name: FormSignature
metric.summary: The signature of the form.

metric.name: MillisecondsSinceFormParsed
metric.summary: Time since form parse.

metric.name: PredictedType
metric.summary: The predicted type of the field. See: autofill::FieldType

metric.name: PredictionSource
metric.summary: The system which generated the prediction: heuristics, server, overall.

metric.name: ValidationEvent
metric.summary: The event which triggered the validation: Submission, Left Form, Form has Autocomplete attributes.

event.name: Autofill.FormEvent
event.summary: Recorded when user interacted with autofill.

metric.name: AutofillFormEvent
metric.summary: Logs autofill form events funnel. See |FormEvent|.

metric.name: FormTypes
metric.summary: Type of form. Stored as bitvector. A set ith bit implies enum FormTypeNameForLogging's ith type was detected. Multiple types are possible.

metric.name: MillisecondsSinceFormParsed
metric.summary: Time since form parse.

event.name: Autofill.FormFillSuccessIOS
event.summary: Records whether the form was successfully filled by Autofill on iOS. The metric is used to measure silent renderer fails when the filling request is executed successfully but the results reported in a callback are empty.

metric.name: FormFillSuccess
metric.summary: A boolean indicating success or failure.

event.name: Autofill.HiddenRepresentationalFieldSkipDecision
event.summary: Recorded while trying to fill or preview a hidden or a representational field.

metric.name: FieldOverallType
metric.summary: Field's overall |FieldType|. See |AutofillField.GetStorableType()|.

metric.name: FieldSignature
metric.summary: The signature of the field (mod 1021). This is the hash identifier used to denote this field for query and voting purposes. See components/autofill/core/common/signatures.cc for more details.

metric.name: FieldTypeGroup
metric.summary: Field's |FieldTypeGroup|. See |AutofillType.group()|.

metric.name: FormSignature
metric.summary: The signature of the form (mod 1021). This is the hash identifier used to denote this form for query and voting purposes. See components/autofill/core/common/signatures.cc for more details.

metric.name: HeuristicType
metric.summary: Field's |FieldType| based on heuristics. See |AutofillField.heuristic_type()|.

metric.name: HtmlFieldMode
metric.summary: Whether the field's autocomplete hint specified 'billing' or 'shipping'. See |AutofillField.html_mode()|.

metric.name: HtmlFieldType
metric.summary: Field's autocomplete field type hint. See |AutofillField.html_type()|.

metric.name: IsSkipped
metric.summary: True if the field was skipped while filling or previewing the form, because it was hidden or representational, but not a 'select' one.

metric.name: ServerType
metric.summary: Field's |FieldType| returned by server. See |AutofillField.server_type()|.

event.name: Autofill.InteractedWithForm
event.summary: Recorded when we parse a form to log form metadata and autofill settings that apply to all subsequent events for this form.

metric.name: FormSignature
metric.summary: FormSignature is approximately 10 bit hash of form structure.

metric.name: FormTypes
metric.summary: Type of form. Stored as bitvector. A set ith bit implies enum FormType's ith type was detected. Multiple types are possible.

metric.name: IsForCreditCard
metric.summary: True for credit card forms, false for address/profile forms.

metric.name: LocalRecordTypeCount
metric.summary: Number of local credit cards or local autofill profiles.

metric.name: ServerRecordTypeCount
metric.summary: Number of masked and full server credit cards or server autofill profiles.

event.name: Autofill.KeyMetrics
event.summary: These metrics are a clone of Autofill's UMA key metrics to measure Autofill quality. They are recorded on navigation if a form was submitted before.

metric.name: AutofillFills
metric.summary: Number of Autofill fills invoked by the user on the current form.

metric.name: FastCheckoutRunId
metric.summary: Optional, only set if a form was filled by Fast Checkout. Unique ID of a Fast Checkout run.

metric.name: FillingAcceptance
metric.summary: Has value 1 if user accepted the suggestion to fill values on a submitted form, 0 otherwise. This is counted as 1 if the user accepted such a suggestion at any time, regardless whether other suggestions were ignored or whether the accepted suggestion was reverted afterwards. Only recorded if suggestions were shown to the user.

metric.name: FillingAssistance
metric.summary:
Has value 1 if the user filled any fields via Autofill on a submitted form, 0 otherwise.
If the user reverts the filling, the form is still counted as filled.
This metric is different from FillingAcceptance in the sense that the latter is only recorded if the user has actually seen a prompt to fill data.

metric.name: FillingCorrectness
metric.summary: Has value 1 for submitted and autofilled forms if user edited at least one filled value before submitting the form, 0 otherwise. Only recorded if any suggestion was filled.

metric.name: FillingReadiness
metric.summary:
Has value 1 for submitted forms when Chromium had any address or payments data to fill, 0 otherwise.
It does not matter whether the user actually requested to fill the data. It does not matter either whether the data available for filling corresponded to the submitted data.

metric.name: FlowId
metric.summary: Hashed GUID linking together multiple emissions of this event if emitted within a 20 minutes timeframe.

metric.name: FormElementUserModifications
metric.summary: Number of times a user focused on a form element and changed its value. Consecutive value changes on the same form element are not counted, neither are JS invoked changes. Also, re-focusing a form element and changing its value is only counted if another form element's value was changed in the meantime.

metric.name: FormTypes
metric.summary: Type of form. Stored as bitvector. A set ith bit implies enum FormTypeNameForLogging's ith type was detected. Multiple types are possible.

event.name: Autofill.RepeatedServerTypePredictionRationalized
event.summary: Recorded when a field type predicted by server is rationalized because of repetition.

metric.name: FieldNewOverallType
metric.summary: Field's overall |FieldType| after rationalization. See |AutofillField.GetStorableType()|.

metric.name: FieldOldOverallType
metric.summary: Field's overall |FieldType| before rationalization. See |AutofillField.GetStorableType()|.

metric.name: FieldSignature
metric.summary: The signature of the field (mod 1021). This is the hash identifier used to denote this field for query and voting purposes. See components/autofill/core/common/signatures.cc for more details.

metric.name: FieldTypeGroup
metric.summary: Field's |FieldTypeGroup|. See |AutofillType.group()|.

metric.name: FormSignature
metric.summary: The signature of the form (mod 1021). This is the hash identifier used to denote this form for query and voting purposes. See components/autofill/core/common/signatures.cc for more details.

metric.name: HeuristicType
metric.summary: Field's |FieldType| based on heuristics. See |AutofillField.heuristic_type()|.

metric.name: HtmlFieldMode
metric.summary: Whether the field's autocomplete hint specified 'billing' or 'shipping'. See |AutofillField.html_mode()|.

metric.name: HtmlFieldType
metric.summary: Field's autocomplete field type hint. See |AutofillField.html_type()|.

metric.name: ServerType
metric.summary: Field's |FieldType| returned by server. See |AutofillField.server_type()|.

event.name: Autofill.Sectioning
event.summary: Recorded after sectioning is performed when the server classification arrived, and only when `AutofillUseParameterizedSectioning` is enabled.

metric.name: FormSignature
metric.summary: A 10-bit hash of the form's signature.

metric.name: SectioningSignature
metric.summary: A 10-bit hash of the form's computed sections.

event.name: Autofill.SuggestionFilled
event.summary: Recorded when user selects a suggestion and we fill the form with that suggestion.

metric.name: FieldSignature
metric.summary: The signature of the field

metric.name: FormSignature
metric.summary: The signature of the form.

metric.name: IsForCreditCard
metric.summary: True for credit card forms, false for address/profile forms.

metric.name: MillisecondsSinceFormParsed
metric.summary: Time since form parse.

metric.name: RecordType
metric.summary: Whether the suggestion was from a local card/autofill profile or from a server card/autofill profile.

event.name: Autofill.SuggestionsShown
event.summary: Recorded when a suggestion is shown to user.

metric.name: FieldSignature
metric.summary: The signature of the field

metric.name: FormSignature
metric.summary: The signature of the form.

metric.name: HeuristicType
metric.summary: Field's |FieldType| based on heuristics. See |AutofillField.heuristic_type()|.

metric.name: HtmlFieldType
metric.summary: Field's autocomplete field type hint. See |AutofillField.html_type()|.

metric.name: MillisecondsSinceFormParsed
metric.summary: Time since form parse.

metric.name: ServerType
metric.summary: Field's |FieldType| returned by server. See |AutofillField.server_type()|.

event.name: Autofill.TextFieldDidChange
event.summary: Recorded when user edits a text field. The text field may have been autofilled.

metric.name: FieldSignature
metric.summary: The signature of the field

metric.name: FieldTypeGroup
metric.summary: Field's |FieldTypeGroup|. See |AutofillType.group()|.

metric.name: FormSignature
metric.summary: The signature of the form.

metric.name: HeuristicType
metric.summary: Field's |FieldType| based on heuristics. See |AutofillField.heuristic_type()|.

metric.name: HtmlFieldMode
metric.summary: Whether the field's autocomplete hint specified 'billing' or 'shipping'. See |AutofillField.html_mode()|.

metric.name: HtmlFieldType
metric.summary: Field's autocomplete field type hint. See |AutofillField.html_type()|.

metric.name: IsAutofilled
metric.summary: True if the field was autofilled. See |AutofillField.is_autofilled|.

metric.name: IsEmpty
metric.summary: True if the field was empty. See |AutofillField.IsEmpty()|.

metric.summary: Time since form parse.

metric.name: ServerType
metric.summary: Field's |FieldType| returned by server. See |AutofillField.server_type()|.

event.name: Autofill2.AddressProfileImport
event.summary: Recorded after a form submission that either resulted in a save prompt for addresses or in a silent update of an already existing profile.

metric.name: AutocompleteUnrecognizedImport
metric.summary: Logs if data was imported from a field that has autocomplete=UNRECOGNIZED.

metric.name: DuplicationRank
metric.summary: Minimum number of fields whose removal makes the import candidate a duplicate of any existing profile. If the duplication rank is 1, the import candidate can be called a quasi-duplicate of an existing profile. Only recorded for new profiles and if a stored profile existed.

metric.name: ImportType
metric.summary: Logs the import type of the storage prompt. An import type can be either the import of a new profile, an update of an existing profile that results in a confirmation dialog, or a silent update that only changes the structure of an already stored address but does not affect any settings-visible stored data.

metric.name: NumberOfEditedFields
metric.summary: Logs the count for the number of fields that have been edited by the user in the storage dialog before the storage was confirmed.

metric.name: PhoneNumberStatus
metric.summary: Logs the phone number validity status based on whether the phone number was imported in the profile or not or removed beforehand.

metric.name: UserDecision
metric.summary: Logs the user decision based on the storage prompt.

metric.name: UserHasExistingProfile
metric.summary: True if the user had already saved an address profile, false otherwise.

event.name: Autofill2.FieldInfo
event.summary: All information about a specific field, recorded at form submission or form destruction time (whatever comes first).

metric.name: AutocompleteState
metric.summary: The field's autocomplete attribute value, the values are from |AutofillMetrics::AutocompleteState|.

metric.name: AutofillSkippedStatus
metric.summary: Whether filling the field was skipped during autofill, and if it was skipped, what the skipping reasons were. Recorded as a DenseSet of |FieldFillingSkipReason|.

metric.name: AutofillStatusVector
metric.summary: A bit vector of autofill statuses for a field, listed in |AutofillMetrics::AutofillStatus|. The first bit represents whether the field is focusable, the second means whether the field was focused.... These autofill statuses will not be stored in UKM individually. We create functions to read the individual autofill status from a bit vector in queries. go/zmwtc

metric.name: FieldLogEventCount
metric.summary: The total number of all the field log events created for this field.

metric.name: FieldSessionIdentifier
metric.summary: A random variable assigned to a specific instance of a form control (e.g. a HTML input element). This should be globally unique and suitable for distinguishing fields. Two different users would have different values. For the same user, the same field in the same form in two different tabs would have two different values.

metric.name: FieldSignature
metric.summary: The signature of the field, a 10 bit hash value (0-1021) of the field name and field type used to denote this field for query and voting purposes.

metric.name: FormControlType2
metric.summary: A form control type of a web form-associated element. We only list the ones that are handled by Autofill, see |autofill::FormControlType|.

metric.name: FormSessionIdentifier
metric.summary: A random variable assigned to a specific instance of a HTML form in a specific renderer. This should be globally unique and suitable for counting distinct forms. Two different users would have different values. A single user loading the same form in two different tabs would have two different values. This is used to link the fields to a form in Autofill2.FormSummary.

metric.name: HeuristicType
metric.summary: There are multiple heuristic classification strategies working in parallel. This type is predicted from the active pattern source.

metric.name: HeuristicTypeDefault
metric.summary: The type of the field predicted from the source of local heuristics on the client, which uses patterns applied for most users, the types are from |autofill::FieldType|.

metric.name: HeuristicTypeExperimental
metric.summary: The type of the field predicted from the heuristics that uses experimental patterns, the types are from |autofill::FieldType|.

metric.name: HeuristicTypeLegacy
metric.summary: The type of the field predicted from patterns whose stability is above suspicion, the types are from |autofill::FieldType|.

metric.name: HtmlFieldMode
metric.summary: The mode according to the autocomplete attribute of a field. Only set if a field has a valid autocomplete attribute. Currently this is used to specify if the field is part of the billing or shipping address. See autofill::mojom::HtmlFieldMode.

metric.name: HtmlFieldType
metric.summary: The field type specified in the autocomplete attribute of the field. Only set if a field has a valid autocomplete attribute. See autofill::mojom::HtmlFieldType.

metric.name: OverallType
metric.summary: The final field type from the list of |autofill::FieldType| that we choose after rationalization, which is used to determine the autofill suggestion when the user triggers autofilling.

metric.name: RankInFieldSignatureGroup
metric.summary: If multiple fields have the same signature, this indicates the position within this set of fields. This allows us to understand problems related to duplicated field signatures.

metric.name: SectionId
metric.summary: A single HTML form can consist of multiple sections. A section is a set of fields that are filled together. For example a single form can contain a shipping and a billing address. Each of those corresponds to one section. The sections are mapped to consecutive natural numbers starting at 1. The section IDs are unique per form.

metric.name: ServerPredictionSource1
metric.summary: The major source of predictions from the Autofill crowdsourced server, see AutofillQueryResponse::FormSuggestion::FieldSuggestion::FieldPrediction::Source.

metric.name: ServerPredictionSource2
metric.summary: The additional source of predictions from the Autofill crowdsourced server if there is an additional prediction, see AutofillQueryResponse::FormSuggestion::FieldSuggestion::FieldPrediction::Source.

metric.name: ServerType1
metric.summary: The type of the field predicted from the Autofill crowdsourced server from majority voting (and some more complex stuff). The types are from |autofill::FieldType|.

metric.name: ServerType2
metric.summary: The additional type of the field predicted from the Autofill crowdsourced server if there is an additional prediction. The types are from |autofill::FieldType|.

metric.name: ServerTypeIsOverride
metric.summary: This is an annotation for server predicted field types which indicates that a manual override defines the server type. This allows us to assess the impact of server overrides and detect possible problems with it.

metric.name: TypeChangedByRationalization
metric.summary: Whether the field's type was changed by running the rationalization.

event.name: Autofill2.FieldInfoAfterSubmission
event.summary: Information about a specific field, recorded after form submission.

metric.name: FieldSessionIdentifier
metric.summary: A random variable assigned to a specific instance of a form control (e.g. a HTML input element). This should be globally unique and suitable for distinguishing fields. Two different users would have different values. For the same user, the same field in the same form in two different tabs would have two different values. FormSessionIdentifier and FieldSessionIdentifier together are used to link to the field in Autofill2.FieldInfo.

metric.name: FormSessionIdentifier
metric.summary: A random variable assigned to a specific instance of a HTML form in a specific renderer. This should be globally unique and suitable for counting distinct forms. Two different users would have different values. A single user loading the same form in two different tabs would have two different values. FormSessionIdentifier and FieldSessionIdentifier together are used to link to the field in Autofill2.FieldInfo.

metric.name: MillisecondsFromFormParsedUntilSubmission
metric.summary: The time interval from form parse to form submission in ms in exponential buckets from |GetSemanticBucketMinForDurationTiming()|.

metric.name: SubmissionSource
metric.summary: The source of form submission. The source is from autofill::mojom::SubmissionSource.

metric.name: SubmittedType1
metric.summary: The first possible field submitted type is determined by comparing the submitted value in the field with the stored user profiles. We will have at most three possible field submitted types. The types are from |autofill::FieldType|.

metric.name: SubmittedType2
metric.summary: The second possible field submitted type is determined by comparing the submitted value in the field with the stored user profiles. We will have at most three possible field submitted types. The types are from |autofill::FieldType|.

metric.name: SubmittedType3
metric.summary: The third possible field submitted type is determined by comparing the submitted value in the field with the stored user profiles. We will have at most three possible field submitted types. The types are from |autofill::FieldType|.

event.name: Autofill2.FocusedComplexForm
event.summary: Tracks metrics of forms of type FormTypeNameForLogging::kPostalAddress and FormTypeNameForLogging::kCreditCard - if at least one field was focused whose type did not belong to FormType::kUnknownFormType. Autofocus on page load, focus-by-pressing-the-tab-key and focus-by-tapping/clicking count as focus events. The event name contains the term "complex forms" because only forms of type FormTypeNameForLogging::kPostalAddress and FormTypeNameForLogging::kCreditCard are logged (to save space and bandwidth). The event is recorded when the form object is deallocated.

metric.name: AutofillDataQueried
metric.summary: A bit-vector with bits set according to `FormType`. A set bit indicates that at least one field of the `FormType` was focused via a tap or click such that the field was eligible for an autofill if data existed.

metric.name: Autofilled
metric.summary: A bit-vector with bits set according to `FormType`. A set bit indicates that at least one field of type `FormType` was autofilled at least once (if the autofill is reverted or modified, this is ignored).

metric.name: DayInAblationWindow
metric.summary: For an ablation experiment we define a grid of 14-day windows. The ablation state is determined by a hash value of a user-seed (random number), the main frame origin, the ablation window. If, for example, an ablation window lasts 14 days, this can be a value between 0 and 13. It indicates whether the user is in the begining or at the end of an ablation window.

metric.name: EditedAfterAutofill
metric.summary: A bit-vector with bits set according to `FormType`. A set bit indicates that at least one field of type `FormType` was autofilled and later modified by the user.

metric.name: FormSessionIdentifier
metric.summary: A random variable assigned to a specific instance of a HTML form in a specific renderer. This should be globally unique and suitable for counting distinct forms. Two different users would have different values. A single user loading the same form in two different tabs would have two different values. This is used to link the fields in this form in Autofill2.FieldInfo and more details in Autofill2.FormSummary.

metric.name: FormSignature
metric.summary: The signature of the form (mod 1021). This is the hash identifier used to denote this form. See components/autofill/core/common/signatures.cc for more details.

metric.name: FormTypes
metric.summary: Bit vector with the following bits from FormTypeNameForLogging: - kUnknownFormType (1<<0), - kAddressForm (1<<1), - kCreditCardForm (1<<2) (A form with any field classified as a CC field), - kPasswordForm (1<<3), - kStandaloneCvcForm (1<<4), - kEmailOnlyForm (1<<5), - kPostalAddressForm (1<<6), Note that a form needs to be a kCreditCardForm or kPostalAddressForm to be logged.

metric.name: HadNonEmptyValueAtSubmission
metric.summary: A bit-vector with bits set according to `FormType`. A set bit indicates that at least one field of type `FormType` was non-empty at submission time.

metric.name: IsAblationStudyInDryRunMode
metric.summary: If 1, the ablation study is in dry run mode, meaning that clients are diverted into ablation and control groups, but they observe no behavioral change.

metric.name: IsInAblationGroupOfAblation
metric.summary: A bit-vector with bits set according to `FormType`. A set bit indicates that that for this form and the respective form type, the user was in the ablation group, meaning that the user did not get the autofill UI, regardless whether they had data available for filling.

metric.name: IsInAblationGroupOfConditionalAblation
metric.summary: A bit-vector with bits set according to `FormType`. A set bit indicates that that for this form and the respective form type, the user was in the conditional ablation group, meaning that the user had data that could be filled, but the filling was suppressed due to the ablation.

metric.name: IsInControlGroupOfAblation
metric.summary: A bit-vector with bits set according to `FormType`. A set bit indicates that that for this form and the respective form type, the user was in the ablation control group. This is the corresponding control group to IsInAblationGroupOfAblation. Autofill is not disabled but metrics are recorded.

metric.name: IsInControlGroupOfConditionalAblation
metric.summary: A bit-vector with bits set according to `FormType`. A set bit indicates that that for this form and the respective form type, the user was in the conditional ablation control group. This is the corresponding control group to IsInAblationGroupOfConditionalAblation. Autofill is not disabled but metrics are recorded.

metric.name: MillisecondsFromFirstInteractionUntilSubmission
metric.summary: The time interval from first interaction on the form to form submission in ms in buckets from |GetSemanticBucketMinForAutofillDurationTiming()|. An interaction is defined as modifying the value of any field in the form.

metric.name: SuggestionsAvailable
metric.summary: A bit-vector with bits set according to `FormType`. A set bit indicates that for at least one field of type `FormType` a suggestion was queried and available for the user. A bit is unset if no suggestions were queried for a form type.

metric.name: UserModified
metric.summary: A bit-vector with bits set according to `FormType`. A set bit indicates that at least one field of the `FormType` was modified by the user (typed text, pasted text or autofilled).

metric.name: WasSubmitted
metric.summary: Whether the form was submitted.

event.name: Autofill2.FormSummary
event.summary: Information recorded for a HTML form element on the HTML page, recorded at form submission or form destruction time (whatever comes first).

metric.name: AutofillFormEvents
metric.summary: A bit vector of some autofill form events that records a funnel of this autofill form. The autofill form events are from an enum list specifying the possible status of the autofill forms, such as if a dropdown with suggestions was shown, if the user interacted with a field in the form. Some of the events are triggered separately for address and credit card forms, e.g. if all the fillable fields in the credit card forms were filled. New form events can be added over time and a form can have multiple form events. This bit vector stored only events 0..63.

metric.name: AutofillFormEvents2
metric.summary: A bit vector of some autofill form events that records a funnel of this autofill form. This contains the entries 64...127 shifted right by 64 bits.

metric.name: FormSessionIdentifier
metric.summary: A random variable assigned to a specific instance of a HTML form in a specific renderer. This should be globally unique and suitable for counting distinct forms. Two different users would have different values. A single user loading the same form in two different tabs would have two different values. This is used to link the fields in this form in Autofill2.FieldInfo.

metric.name: FormSignature
metric.summary: The signature of the form, which is a 10-bit hash of the form's domain, name and all the supported form fields (including empty names).

metric.name: MillisecondsFromFirstInteratctionUntilSubmission
metric.summary: The time interval from first interaction on the form to form submission in ms in exponential buckets from |GetSemanticBucketMinForDurationTiming()|. An interaction is defined as modifying the value of any field in the form.

metric.name: MillisecondsFromFormParsedUntilSubmission
metric.summary: The time interval from form parse to form submission in ms in exponential buckets from |GetSemanticBucketMinForDurationTiming()|.

metric.name: SampleRate
metric.summary: A sampling rate is a precomputed positive integer and is applied to each form and adaptive to the popularity of forms. The most popular forms are classified into a `very popular` category and maybe apply a 1:100 sampling while the less popular forms have a sampling of 1:1. If the sampling is 1:X, this sample rate value stores the `X`.

metric.name: WasSubmitted
metric.summary: Whether the form was submitted.

event.name: Autofill2.SubmittedFormWithExperimentalFields
event.summary: Statistics on the number of non-empty experimental field-types in submitted forms, recorded at form submission time.

metric.name: FormSessionIdentifier
metric.summary: A random variable assigned to a specific instance of a HTML form in a specific renderer. This should be globally unique and suitable for counting distinct forms. Two different users would have different values. A single user loading the same form in two different tabs would have two different values. This is used to link the fields in this form in Autofill2.FieldInfo.

metric.name: FormSignature
metric.summary: The signature of the form, which is a 10-bit hash of the form's domain, name and all the supported form fields (including empty names).

metric.name: NumberOfNonEmptyExperimentalFields0
metric.summary: The total number of non-empty experimental fields in the form in group 0.

metric.name: NumberOfNonEmptyExperimentalFields1
metric.summary: The total number of non-empty experimental fields in the form in group 1.

metric.name: NumberOfNonEmptyExperimentalFields2
metric.summary: The total number of non-empty experimental fields in the form in group 2.

metric.name: NumberOfNonEmptyExperimentalFields3
metric.summary: The total number of non-empty experimental fields in the form in group 3.

metric.name: NumberOfNonEmptyExperimentalFields4
metric.summary: The total number of non-empty experimental fields in the form in group 4.

event.name: BackForwardCacheDisabledForRenderFrameHostReason
event.summary: Recorded at a history commit for each reason passed to BackForwardCache::DisableForRenderFrameHost. The source IDs match with the source IDs of HistoryNavigation events.

metric.name: Reason2
metric.summary:
For history navigations, records the reason passed to BackForwardCache::DisableForRenderFrameHost calls. The reason is specified as a 48-bit int representing the source (blink or an embedder) and a source-specific 16-bit int representing the reason specific to that source.
The metric is recorded when a history navigation commits and emits a sample for each reason. One history navigation can emit multiple reasons, and these reasons are recorded as multiple events with the same source ID based on the navigation ID.

event.name: BackForwardCacheDisallowActivationReason
event.summary: Recorded at a history commit for each reason passed to RenderFrameHost::IsInactiveAndDisallowActivation. The source IDs match with the source IDs of HistoryNavigation events.

metric.name: Reason
metric.summary:
For history navigations, records the reason passed to RenderFrameHost::IsInactiveAndDisallowActivation.
The metric is recorded when a history navigation commits and emits a sample for each reason. One history navigation can emit multiple reasons, and these reasons are recorded as multiple events with the same source ID based on the navigation ID.

event.name: BackgroundFetch
event.summary: A BackgroundFetch event is logged before a background fetch is started from a document context.

metric.name: DeniedDueToPermissions
metric.summary: Boolean for whether the background fetch was denied due to permission. This includes only the content setting permission.

metric.name: DownloadTotal
metric.summary: The value of downloadTotal provided with the background fetch. This is the number of bytes that the developer expects to be downloaded with the background fetch. This number is exponentially bucketed for privacy reasons, and uses the UKM GetExponentialBucketMin method with a value of 2.0 for spacing.

metric.name: HasTitle
metric.summary: Boolean for whether a title was provided with the background fetch.

metric.name: NumIcons
metric.summary: Count of icons provided with the background fetch.

metric.name: NumRequestsInFetch
metric.summary: Number of requests in the background fetch. This number is exponentially bucketed for privacy reasons, and uses the UKM GetExponentialBucketMin method with a value of 2.0 for spacing.

metric.name: RatioOfIdealToChosenIconSize
metric.summary: Ratio of the ideal icon to the chosen icon size, times hundred. This will be set to -1 if either ideal icon size is 0, or if none of the provided icons are suitable.

event.name: BackgroundFetchDeletingRegistration
event.summary: A BackgroundFetchDeletingRegistration event is logged when a background fetch job is being deleted.

metric.name: UserInitiatedAbort
metric.summary: Boolean for whether the background fetch job was cancelled from the UI.

event.name: BackgroundSyncCompleted
event.summary: Logged when a Background Sync one-shot registration is completed.

metric.name: MaxAttempts
metric.summary: Integer value of the maximum number of allowed retries.

metric.name: NumAttempts
metric.summary: Integer value of the number of retries / sync events dispatched.

metric.name: Status
metric.summary: The enum value of the completion status, defined in blink::ServiceWorkerStatusCode.

event.name: BackgroundSyncRegistered
event.summary: Logged when a one-shot Background Sync registration is created.

metric.name: CanFire
metric.summary: Boolean for whether a sync event can be immediately dispatched for this registration.

metric.name: IsReregistered
metric.summary: Boolean for whether this is a duplicate registration.

event.name: Badging
event.summary: Metrics that measure the use of Badging API

metric.name: UpdateAppBadge
metric.summary: Logged when navigator.setAppBadge or navigator.clearAppBadge is called.

event.name: Blink.ContentManagementSystem.Versions
event.summary: Metrics stating the detected major+minor version of known content management systems when detected. This event is recorded when the page is loaded. For interoperability with the framework detection above, this reports each content management system individually instead of a single number. The version numbers here are a bit mask: 0xMMmm (Major+minor), e.g. version 16.3 would be 0x1003

metric.name: DrupalVersion
metric.summary: A bitmask (0xMMmm) representing the Drupal version

metric.name: WordPressVersion
metric.summary: A bitmask (0xMMmm) representing the WordPress version

event.name: Blink.DeveloperMetricsRare
event.summary: Collects data for a subset of UseCounter features of interest to the Web Platform Developer Metrics team due to their recent launch or potential for spec changes. Features reported in this set should be rare, appearing in fewer than 5% of page loads.

metric.name: Feature
metric.summary: Opt-in UseCounter feature, listed in components/page_load_metrics/browser/observers/use_counter/webdev_metrics_ukm_features.cc

metric.name: IsMainFrameFeature
metric.summary: Emits True(1) or False(0) to indicate whether the Feature recorded is in the main frame or not.

event.name: Blink.FedCm
event.summary: Records performance metrics for FedCM(Federated Credential Management) API.

metric.name: AccountLabel.NumMatchingAccounts
metric.summary: Records the number of accounts that match an account label. Records at the time the accounts received by the FedCM API are filtered.

metric.name: AccountsDialogShown
metric.summary: Records a 1 each time an accounts dialog is shown.

metric.name: AccountsRequestSent
metric.summary: Records a 1 each time a request is sent to the accounts endpoint.

metric.name: AutoReauthn.BlockedByContentSettings
metric.summary: Records whether the FedCM auto re-authn call is blocked because the auto re-authn content settings permission is disabled. Records at most one sample per FedCM API with auto re-authn enabled: some failures could occur before this metric is recorded.

metric.name: AutoReauthn.BlockedByEmbargo
metric.summary: Records whether the FedCM auto re-authn call is blocked because the auto re-authn is under embargo, i.e. due to cooldown. Records at most one sample per FedCM API with auto re-authn enabled: some failures could occur before this metric is recorded.

metric.name: AutoReauthn.BlockedByPreventSilentAccess
metric.summary: Records whether the FedCM auto re-authn call is blocked because preventSilentAccess() has been called on this site. Records at most one sample per FedCM API with auto re-authn enabled: some failures could occur before this metric is recorded.

metric.name: AutoReauthn.ReturningAccounts
metric.summary: Records whether there was zero, one, or multiple returning accounts when a site calls the FedCM API with autoReauthn: true. Records at most one sample per failed API call: some failures could occur before this metric is recorded.

metric.name: AutoReauthn.Succeeded
metric.summary: Records whether auto re-authn checks succeeded or not. The count would provide roughly the number of FedCM re-authn attempts, the true count would provide the number of times the re-authn UI is shown to the user, and the false count would provide the number of attempts where auto re-authn was blocked for some non-network related reason. Records at most one sample per FedCM API with auto re-authn enabled: some failures could occur before this metric is recorded.

metric.name: AutoReauthn.TimeFromEmbargoWhenBlocked
metric.summary: Records the amount of time that has passed from the time the FedCM auto re-authn API was embargoed to the time in which the next call occurs. Only records a sample when there is an auto re-authn FedCM API call which is blocked due to embargo. Samples are exponentially bucketed, with a max bucket of 10 minutes, the embargo duration (see `kFederatedIdentityAutoReauthnEmbargoDuration`).

metric.name: Disconnect.FrameType
metric.summary: Records the type of frame that invokes disconnect(). Records once for each disconnect() call. Possible values are: main frame, same-site iframe, and cross-site iframe, where the site of the iframe is compared with the site of the main frame.

metric.name: DomainHint.NumMatchingAccounts
metric.summary: Records the number of accounts that match a domain hint. Records at the time the accounts received by the FedCM API are filtered.

metric.name: Error.ErrorDialogResult
metric.summary: Records the outcome of the error dialog. Recorded at most once per API call when the error dialog is displayed.

metric.name: Error.ErrorDialogType
metric.summary: Records the type of error dialog shown. Recorded at most once per API call when the error dialog is displayed.

metric.name: Error.TokenResponseType
metric.summary: Records the type of token response received. Recorded at most once per API call when a token response received.

metric.name: FedCmSessionID
metric.summary: Records the session ID associated to the FedCM call for which this event is recording metrics. Each FedCM call gets a random integer session id, which helps group UKM events by the session id.

metric.name: LoginHint.NumMatchingAccounts
metric.summary: Records the number of accounts that match a login hint. Records at the time the accounts received by the FedCM API are filtered.

metric.name: MismatchDialogShown
metric.summary: Records a 1 each time a mismatch dialog is shown.

metric.name: MultipleRequestsRpMode
metric.summary: Records the RpMode of two consecutive requests. Recorded every time when a token request is invoked while there is another one pending.

metric.name: NumIdpsMismatch
metric.summary: Records the number of IDPs that result in a 'mismatch' status from a single FedCM get() invocation. Recorded every time a FedCM get() call is resolved. Records some integer 0 or greater. Uses exponential count bucketing.

metric.name: NumIdpsRequested
metric.summary: Records the number of IDPs that are requested from a single FedCM get() invocation. Recorded every time a FedCM get() call is resolved. Records some integer greater than or equal to 1. Uses exponential count bucketing.

metric.name: NumRequestsPerDocument
metric.summary: Records the number of navigator.credentials.get() requests made in a document. Requests made when FedCM is disabled or when there is a pending FedCM request are not counted. Records at most one sample for every document where at least one navigator.credentials.get() request is made. No samples are recorded if the count is 0. The bucketing method used is base::UmaHistogramCounts100.

metric.name: PreventSilentAccessFrameType
metric.summary: Records the type of frame that invokes preventSilentAccess(). Records only when there is an existing FedCM sharing permission on the main frame, and some frame in the page invokes the preventSilentAccess() JavaScript call. Possible values are: main frame, same-site iframe, and cross-site iframe, where the site of the iframe is compared with the site of the main frame.

metric.name: RpMode
metric.summary: Records the RP mode of the FedCM API. Recorded once per API call together with Status.RequestIdToken when the request is fulfilled (either resolved or rejected).

metric.name: Status.Disconnect
metric.summary: Records the status of a disconnect call to the FedCM API. Recorded once per IdentityCredential.disconnect() call, once the browser knows the result.

metric.name: Status.MediationRequirement
metric.summary: Records the mediation requirement of a request token call to the FedCM API. Records a sample when the call is completed, i.e. at the same time as Status.RequestIdToken.

metric.name: Status.RequestIdToken
metric.summary: Records the status of a request id token call to the FedCM API. Recorded once per API call when the request is fulfilled (either resolved or rejected).

metric.name: Status.SignInStateMatch
metric.summary: Records whether user sign-in states between IDP and browser match after browser receives an account list from IDP prior to showing UI.

metric.name: Timing.AccountsDialogShownDuration
metric.summary: Records the duration from when an accounts dialog is shown to when dialog is destroyed e.g. user selects an account, user closes the dialog, or the developer aborts the request. This duration includes the time when the accounts dialog is inactive e.g. user switches to a different tab. Samples are at most 10 minutes. Records a sample when an accounts dialog which is not the verifying UI for auto re-authentication is shown.

metric.name: Timing.CancelOnDialog
metric.summary: Records the time (in milliseconds) from when the accounts dialog is shown to when the user closes the dialog without selecting any account.

metric.name: Timing.ContinueOnDialog
metric.summary: Records the time (in milliseconds) from when the accounts dialog is shown to when the user presses the Continue button.

metric.name: Timing.Disconnect
metric.summary: Records the time in milliseconds from when the disconnect() request is received by the browser process to the time in which it is completed, e.g. right before sending the response back to the renderer process. Records one sample for every disconnect call which sends a disconnect request.

metric.name: Timing.GetUserInfoToButtonMode
metric.summary: Records the time in milliseconds from when the accounts are received via the getUserInfo() API to the time when the Button Mode API is called. Recorded when the Button mode is invoked and there was a successful accounts fetch via the getUserInfo API call on this page. Recorded at most once per getUserInfo API call.

metric.name: Timing.IdTokenResponse
metric.summary: Records the time (in milliseconds) from when the user presses the Continue button to when the id token response is received.

metric.name: Timing.MismatchDialogShownDuration
metric.summary: Records the duration from when a mismatch dialog is shown to when user triggers IDP sign-in pop-up window or when dialog is destroyed e.g. user closes the dialog. This duration includes the time when the mismatch dialog is inactive e.g. user switches to a different tab. Samples are at most 10 minutes. Records a sample when a mismatch dialog is shown.

metric.name: Timing.ShowAccountsDialog
metric.summary: Records the time (in milliseconds) from when a call to the API was made to when the accounts dialog is shown.

metric.name: Timing.ShowAccountsDialogBreakdown.AccountsFetch
metric.summary: Records the time (in milliseconds) from when the accounts request was made to when the fetch response is received. Only recorded when the accounts are ready to show which means all fetches are done. In case of multiple identity providers, the fetch that takes the longest will be recorded.

metric.name: Timing.ShowAccountsDialogBreakdown.ClientMetadataFetch
metric.summary: Records the time (in milliseconds) from when the client metadata request was made to when the fetch response is received. Only recorded when the accounts are ready to show which means all fetches are done. In case of multiple identity providers, the fetch that takes the longest will be recorded.

metric.name: Timing.ShowAccountsDialogBreakdown.WellKnownAndConfigFetch
metric.summary: Records the time (in milliseconds) from when a call to the API was made to when the well-known and config files fetch are received. Only recorded when the accounts are ready to show which means all fetches are done. In case of multiple identity providers, the fetch that takes the longest will be recorded.

metric.name: Timing.TurnaroundTime
metric.summary: Records the overall time (in milliseconds) from when the API is called to when the id token response is received.

event.name: Blink.FedCmIdp
event.summary:
Records performance metrics for FedCM (Federated Credential Management) API. Recorded when the web developer calls the FedCM API. Individual metrics are recorded eagerly, whenever the value has been computed.
This event uses the specific WEB_IDENTITY ukm_source_id which is generated from the identity provider's origin.

metric.name: AccountsDialogShown
metric.summary: Records a 1 each time an accounts dialog is shown.

metric.name: AccountsRequestSent
metric.summary: Records a 1 each time a request is sent to the accounts endpoint.

metric.name: Disconnect.FrameType
metric.summary: Records the type of frame that invokes disconnect(). Records once for each disconnect() call. Possible values are: main frame, same-site iframe, and cross-site iframe, where the site of the iframe is compared with the site of the main frame.

metric.name: Error.ErrorDialogResult
metric.summary: Records the outcome of the error dialog. Recorded at most once per API call when the error dialog is displayed.

metric.name: Error.ErrorDialogType
metric.summary: Records the type of error dialog shown. Recorded at most once per API call when the error dialog is displayed.

metric.name: Error.ErrorUrlType
metric.summary: Records whether the error URL is same-origin, cross-origin but same-site or cross-site with the config URL. Recorded at most once per API call when a valid error URL is received.

metric.name: Error.TokenResponseType
metric.summary: Records the type of token response received. Recorded at most once per API call when a token response received.

metric.name: FedCmSessionID
metric.summary: Records the session ID associated to the FedCM call for which this event is recording metrics. Each FedCM call gets a random integer session id, which helps group UKM events by the session id.

metric.name: MismatchDialogShown
metric.summary: Records a 1 each time a mismatch dialog is shown.

metric.name: MultipleRequestsRpMode
metric.summary: Records the RpMode of two consecutive requests. Recorded every time when a token request is invoked while there is another one pending.

metric.name: NumIdpsMismatch
metric.summary: Records the number of IDPs that result in a 'mismatch' status from a single FedCM get() invocation. Recorded every time a FedCM get() call is resolved. Records some integer 0 or greater. Uses exponential count bucketing.

metric.name: NumIdpsRequested
metric.summary: Records the number of IDPs that are requested from a single FedCM get() invocation. Recorded every time a FedCM get() call is resolved. Records some integer greater than or equal to 1. Uses exponential count bucketing.

metric.name: RpMode
metric.summary: Records the RP mode of the FedCM API. Recorded once per API call together with Status.RequestIdToken when the request is fulfilled (either resolved or rejected).

metric.name: Status.Disconnect
metric.summary: Records the status of a disconnect call to the FedCM API. Recorded once per IdentityCredential.disconnect() call, once the browser knows the result.

metric.name: Status.MediationRequirement
metric.summary: Records the mediation requirement of a request token call to the FedCM API. Records a sample when the call is completed, i.e. at the same time as Status.RequestIdToken.

metric.name: Status.RequestIdToken
metric.summary: Records the status of a request id token call to the FedCM API. Recorded once per API call when the request is fulfilled (either resolved or rejected).

metric.name: Status.SignInStateMatch
metric.summary: Records whether user sign-in states between IDP and browser match after browser receives an account list from IDP prior to showing UI.

metric.name: Timing.AccountsDialogShownDuration
metric.summary: Records the duration from when an accounts dialog is shown to when dialog is destroyed e.g. user selects an account, user closes the dialog, or the developer aborts the request. This duration includes the time when the accounts dialog is inactive e.g. user switches to a different tab. Samples are at most 10 minutes. Records a sample when an accounts dialog which is not the verifying UI for auto re-authentication is shown.

metric.name: Timing.CancelOnDialog
metric.summary: Records the time (in milliseconds) from when the accounts dialog is shown to when the user closes the dialog without selecting any account.

metric.name: Timing.ContinueOnDialog
metric.summary: Records the time (in milliseconds) from when the accounts dialog is shown to when the user presses the Continue button.

metric.name: Timing.Disconnect
metric.summary: Records the time in milliseconds from when the disconnect() request is received by the browser process to the time in which it is completed, e.g. right before sending the response back to the renderer process. Records one sample for every disconnect call which sends a disconnect request.

metric.name: Timing.IdTokenResponse
metric.summary: Records the time (in milliseconds) from when the user presses the Continue button to when the id token response is received.

metric.name: Timing.MismatchDialogShownDuration
metric.summary: Records the duration from when a mismatch dialog is shown to when user triggers IDP sign-in pop-up window or when dialog is destroyed e.g. user closes the dialog. This duration includes the time when the mismatch dialog is inactive e.g. user switches to a different tab. Samples are at most 10 minutes. Records a sample when a mismatch dialog is shown.

metric.name: Timing.ShowAccountsDialog
metric.summary: Records the time (in milliseconds) from when a call to the API was made to when the accounts dialog is shown.

metric.name: Timing.TurnaroundTime
metric.summary: Records the overall time (in milliseconds) from when the API is called to when the id token response is received.

event.name: Blink.FindInPage
event.summary: Logged once per page; indicates the type of find-in-page behaviors users utilizied.

metric.name: BeforematchExpandedHiddenMatchable
metric.summary: Boolean indicating that a find-in-page match was hidden but became visible after the beforematch event was fired.

metric.name: DidHaveRenderSubtreeMatch
metric.summary: Boolean indicating whether there was an active match in content-visibility unrendered elements.

metric.name: DidSearch
metric.summary: Boolean indicating that find-in-page happened.

event.name: Blink.FrameLoader
event.summary: Timing metrics associated with the frame loader in Blink.

metric.name: CommitDocumentLoaderTime
metric.summary: Time taken to commit a new document in Blink in microseconds. Collected once per navigation when a frame is loaded. This is rounded down to the nearest exponential bucket.

event.name: Blink.HTMLParsing
event.summary:
Logged once per page load, to capture information about invocation of the HTMLDocumentParser for the initial document load. Does not include other aspects of document parsing, such as style sheet parsing or script execution during parsing. Also does not include innerText parsing.
This event is not recorded on machines with low-resolution clocks.

metric.name: ChunkCount
metric.summary: The number of chunks parsed while loading the page. Each chunk represents a piece of the HTML content that can be loaded without blocking or yielding.

metric.name: FetchQueuedPreloadsTime
metric.summary: Time taken to fetch queued subresource preloads for a document in microseconds. This is rounded down to the nearest exponential bucket.

metric.name: ParsingTimeMax
metric.summary: The maximum time used to parse a single chunk before being blocked, yielding or completing, in microseconds.

metric.name: ParsingTimeMin
metric.summary: The minimum time used to parse a single chunk before being blocked, yielding or completing, in microseconds.

metric.name: ParsingTimeTotal
metric.summary: The total amount of time spent in the HTMLDocumentParser parsing the page, in microseconds.

metric.name: PreloadTime
metric.summary: Time spent on preloading subresources for a document, including mojo operations, in microseconds. This is rounded down to the nearest exponential bucket.

metric.name: PrepareToStopParsingTime
metric.summary: Time taken to tokenize the HTML document, if necessary, and perform other tasks around parsing completion in microseconds. This is rounded down to the nearest exponential bucket.

metric.name: PumpTokenizerTime
metric.summary: Time taken to tokenize an HTML document in Blink in microseconds. This is rounded down to the nearest exponential bucket.

metric.name: ScanAndPreloadTime
metric.summary: Time taken to scan and preload subresources for a document in microseconds. This is rounded down to the nearest exponential bucket.

metric.name: ScanTime
metric.summary: Time spent on the preload scanning process for an HTML document, in microseconds. This is rounded down to the nearest exponential bucket.

metric.name: TokensParsedAverage
metric.summary: The average number of tokens parsed across all chunks.

metric.name: TokensParsedMax
metric.summary: The maximum number of tokens parsed as a chunk.

metric.name: TokensParsedMin
metric.summary: The minimum number of tokens parsed as a chunk.

metric.name: TokensParsedTotal
metric.summary: The total number of tokens parsed across all chunks.

metric.name: YieldedTimeAverage
metric.summary: The average time between parsing of two chunks, in microseconds. Will be zero if only one chunk was parsed.

metric.name: YieldedTimeMax
metric.summary: The minimum time between parsing of two chunks, in microseconds. Will be zero if only one chunk was parsed.

metric.name: YieldedTimeMin
metric.summary: The maximum time between parsing of two chunks, in microseconds. Will be zero if only one chunk was parsed.

event.name: Blink.JavaScriptFramework.Versions
event.summary: Metrics stating the detected major+minor version of known frameworks when detected. This event is recorded when the page is loaded. Note that a page can use multiple frameworks at the same time, hence why this reports each framework individually instead of a single number. The version numbers here are a bit mask: 0xMMmm (Major+minor), e.g. version 16.3 would be 0x1003

metric.name: AngularVersion
metric.summary: A bitmask (0xMMmm) representing the Angular version

metric.name: NextJSVersion
metric.summary: A bitmask (0xMMmm) representing the NextJS version

metric.name: NuxtVersion
metric.summary: A bitmask (0xMMmm) representing the Nuxt version

metric.name: VueVersion
metric.summary: A bitmask (0xMMmm) representing the Vue version

event.name: Blink.PageLoad
event.summary:
The aggregated time spent in each metric's component from creation of the local frame view to the First Contentful Paint signal. The event is reported once per page load when First Contentful Paint is signaled.
This event is not recorded on machines with low-resolution clocks.

metric.name: Accessibility
metric.summary: The time spent in accessibility tree code, between navigation and First Contentful Paint, in microseconds.

metric.name: AnchorElementMetricsIntersectionObserver
metric.summary: Time spent computing IntersectionObservations for anchor elements, in microseconds.

metric.name: Animate
metric.summary: The time spent in main frame animation, between navigation and First Contentful Paint, in microseconds.

metric.name: CompositingCommit
metric.summary: The time spent committing main thread compositing results, between navigation and First Contentful Paint, in microseconds.

metric.name: CompositingInputs
metric.summary: The time spent in main thread compositing inputs, between navigation and First Contentful Paint, in microseconds.

metric.name: ContentDocumentUpdate
metric.summary: The time spent doing forced document updates due to update sub-document content, between navigation and First Contentful Paint, in microseconds. Includes SVG, Canvas and Plugins.

metric.name: DisplayLockIntersectionObserver
metric.summary: Time spent computing IntersectionObservations for DisplayLockDocumentState, in microseconds.

metric.name: ForcedStyleAndLayout
metric.summary: The time spent in forced style recalc and layouts, between navigation and First Contentful Paint, in microseconds.

metric.name: HandleInputEvents
metric.summary: The time spent in processing rAF-aligned input for the main frame, between navigation and First Contentful Paint, in microseconds.

metric.name: HitTestDocumentUpdate
metric.summary: The time spent updating the document in preparation for a hit test for the main frame, between navigation and First Contentful Paint, in microseconds.

metric.name: ImplCompositorCommit
metric.summary: The time spent committing layer tree impl data in the compositor thread, between navigation and First Contentful Paint, in microseconds.

metric.name: IntersectionObservation
metric.summary: The time spent computing intersection observer state, between navigation and First Contentful Paint, in microseconds.

metric.name: IntersectionObservationInternalCount
metric.summary: The total number of times a blink-internal IntersectionObservation was recomputed between navigation and First Contentful Paint.

metric.name: IntersectionObservationJavascriptCount
metric.summary: The total number of times a javascript-instantiated IntersectionObservation was recomputed between navigation and First Contentful Paint.

metric.name: JavascriptDocumentUpdate
metric.summary: The time spent doing forced document updates due to generic Javascript API requests, between navigation and First Contentful Paint, in microseconds. Note this primarily captures Javascript that explicitly requires Style and Layout, but not implicit requests such as through setting focus or scroll position.

metric.name: JavascriptIntersectionObserver
metric.summary: Time spent computing IntersectionObservations for javascript observers, in microseconds.

metric.name: Layout
metric.summary: The time spent in layout document lifecycle work, between navigation and First Contentful Paint, in microseconds.

metric.name: LazyLoadIntersectionObserver
metric.summary: Time spent computing IntersectionObservations for lazy loading, in microseconds.

metric.name: MainFrame
metric.summary: The time spent in ProxyMain::BeginMainFrame, between navigation and First Contentful Paint, in microseconds.

metric.name: MediaIntersectionObserver
metric.summary: Time spent computing IntersectionObservations for media elements, in microseconds.

metric.name: Paint
metric.summary: The time spent in paint document lifecycle work, between navigation and First Contentful Paint, in microseconds.

metric.name: ParseStyleSheet
metric.summary: The time spent parsing style sheets.

metric.name: PermissionElementIntersectionObserver
metric.summary: Time spent computing IntersectionObservations for permission elements, in microseconds.

metric.name: PossibleSynchronizedScrollCount2
metric.summary:
Counts whether a possible sync-scroll update is attempted during an animation update of the outermost main frame of a page between navigation and First Contentful Paint (see sync_scroll_attempt_heuristic.h for details).
The heuristic is checked once per main frame update. The recorded value will be either 1 or 0 for a sampled frame depending on whether the heuristic determined that there was a possible sync-scroll update.

metric.name: PrePaint
metric.summary: The time spent in pre-paint document lifecycle work, between navigation and First Contentful Paint, in microseconds.

metric.name: ServiceDocumentUpdate
metric.summary: The time spent doing forced document updates due to browser requested actions, such as accessibility, intersection observer or spell checking, between navigation and First Contentful Paint, in microseconds.

metric.name: Style
metric.summary: The time spent in style document lifecycle work, between navigation and First Contentful Paint, in microseconds.

metric.name: UpdateLayers
metric.summary: The time spent in for LayerTreeHost::UpdateLayers, between navigation and First Contentful Paint, in microseconds.

metric.name: UpdateViewportIntersection
metric.summary: Time spent computing internal geometry information about iframes, between navigation and First Contentful Paint, in microseconds.

metric.name: UserDrivenDocumentUpdate
metric.summary:
The time spent doing forced document updates due to a user action such as input, find-in-page or printing, between navigation and First Contentful Paint, in microseconds.
As of 04/2023, updates for scrolling are included in this metric.

metric.name: VisualUpdateDelay
metric.summary: Time elapsed, in microseconds, between when an animation update is first scheduled and when the resulting BeginMainFrame starts.

metric.name: WaitForCommit
metric.summary: The time spent waiting for the compositor thread to begin processing a commit, between navigation and First Contentful Paint, in microseconds.

event.name: Blink.PaintTiming
event.summary: Paint timing metrics recorded in Blink, usually used as debugging metrics for PaintTiming metrics in the PageLoad event.

metric.name: LCPDebugging.HasViewportImage
metric.summary: Records whether a page has an image whose size equals the full viewport. Recorded when the LCP algorithm has stopped.

event.name: Blink.Script.AsyncScripts
event.summary: Metrics associated with the async script count associated with a document, recorded when the HTML parsing of a mainframe has completed.

event.name: Blink.ShapeText
event.summary: Metrics to measure the InlineNode::ShapeText() performance that involves font and glyphs handling while loading the outermost main frame document. This is recorded at the frequency of 0.001.

metric.name: MaxTime
metric.summary: Measures the maximum elapsed time it takes to run InlineNode::ShapeText(), in microseconds.

metric.name: TotalTime
metric.summary: Measures the total elapsed time it takes to run InlineNode::ShapeText(), in microseconds.

event.name: Blink.SVGImage
event.summary: Metrics to measure the SVGImage::DataChanged() performance per document while loading the outermost main frame document. This is recorded at the frequency of 0.001.

metric.name: Count
metric.summary: Measures the call count of SVGImage::DataChanged(). ukm::GetExponentialBucketMinForCounts1000() is applied.

metric.name: TotalTime
metric.summary: Measures the total elapsed time it takes to run SVGImage::DataChanged(), in microseconds.

event.name: Blink.UpdateTime
event.summary:
Metrics that measure various update times in Blink. This includes paint, compositing, and layout update times. This event is reported once in the first main frame update after First Contentful Paint, with data from a randomly chosen frame up to and including First Contentful Paint, and once on LocalFrameView destruction with data from a random frame between FirstContentfulPaint and shutdown.
This event is not recorded on machines with low-resolution clocks.

metric.name: Accessibility
metric.summary: The time taken for accessibility for the main frame in microseconds during the sampled frame.

metric.name: AccessibilityBeginMainFrame
metric.summary: The total main frame time used in accessibility, in microseconds.

metric.name: AnchorElementMetricsIntersectionObserver
metric.summary: Time spent computing IntersectionObservations for anchor elements, in microseconds.

metric.name: AnchorElementMetricsIntersectionObserverBeginMainFrame
metric.summary: The total main frame time used to compute IntersectionObservations for anchor elements, in microseconds.

metric.name: Animate
metric.summary: The time taken for main frame animation in microseconds during the sampled frame.

metric.name: AnimateBeginMainFrame
metric.summary: The total main frame time used by by main thread animations, in microseconds.

metric.name: CompositingCommit
metric.summary: The time taken by the compositing commit in microseconds during the sampled frame.

metric.name: CompositingCommitBeginMainFrame
metric.summary: The total main frame time used by the compositing commit, in microseconds.

metric.name: CompositingInputs
metric.summary: The time taken by the compositing inputs phase in microseconds during the sampled frame.

metric.name: CompositingInputsBeginMainFrame
metric.summary: The total main frame time used by the compositing inputs phase, in microseconds.

metric.name: ContentDocumentUpdate
metric.summary: The time spent doing forced document updates due to update sub-document content, during a sampled frame, in microseconds. Includes SVG, Canvas and Plugins.

metric.name: ContentDocumentUpdateBeginMainFrame
metric.summary: The time spent doing forced document updates due to update sub-document content, during the BeginMainFrame block of a sampled frame, in microseconds. Includes SVG, Canvas and Plugins.

metric.name: DisplayLockIntersectionObserver
metric.summary: Time spent computing IntersectionObservations for DisplayLockDocumentState, in microseconds.

metric.name: DisplayLockIntersectionObserverBeginMainFrame
metric.summary: The total main frame time used to compute IntersectionObservations for DisplayLockDocumentState, in microseconds.

metric.name: ForcedStyleAndLayout
metric.summary: The time taken by forced style recalc and layouts in microseconds between the end of the last main frame update and the current sampled frame, in microseconds.

metric.name: ForcedStyleAndLayoutBeginMainFrame
metric.summary: The total main frame time used by forced style updates and layouts, in microseconds. This metric may include time that is also included in the AnimateBeginMainFrame metric.

metric.name: HandleInputEvents
metric.summary: The time taken to process rAF-aligned input for the main frame in microseconds during the sampled frame.

metric.name: HandleInputEventsBeginMainFrame
metric.summary: The total main frame time used in processing rAF-aligned input, in microseconds.

metric.name: HitTestDocumentUpdate
metric.summary: The time taken to perform hit test document updates for the main frame in microseconds during the sampled frame. It does not compute the actual hit test time, rather the style, layout, compositing, etc time to prepare for the test.

metric.name: HitTestDocumentUpdateBeginMainFrame
metric.summary: The total main frame time used in performing hit test document updates, in microseconds.

metric.name: ImplCompositorCommit
metric.summary: The time spent committing layer tree impl data in the compositor thread, in microseconds.

metric.name: ImplCompositorCommitBeginMainFrame
metric.summary: The total main frame time spent committing layer tree impl data in the compositor thread, in microseconds. This should always match ImplCompositorCommit and serves as a check.

metric.name: IntersectionObservation
metric.summary: The time taken to compute intersection observer state for the main frame in microseconds during the sampled frame.

metric.name: IntersectionObservationBeginMainFrame
metric.summary: The total main frame time used to compute IntersectionObserver observations, in microseconds.

metric.name: IntersectionObservationInternalCount
metric.summary: The number of blink-internal IntersectionObservations that were recomputed during the sampled frame.

metric.name: IntersectionObservationInternalCountBeginMainFrame
metric.summary: The number of blink-internal IntersectionObservations that were recomputed during the sampled frame.

metric.name: IntersectionObservationJavascriptCount
metric.summary: The number of javascript-instantiated IntersectionObservations that were recomputed during the sampled frame.

metric.name: IntersectionObservationJavascriptCountBeginMainFrame
metric.summary: The number of javascript-instantiated IntersectionObservations that were recomputed during the sampled frame.

metric.name: JavascriptDocumentUpdate
metric.summary: The time spent doing forced document updates due to generic Javascript API requests, during a sampled frame, in microseconds. Note this primarily captures Javascript that explicitly requires Style and Layout, but not implicit requests such as through setting focus or scroll position.

metric.name: JavascriptDocumentUpdateBeginMainFrame
metric.summary: The time spent doing forced document updates due to generic Javascript API requests, during the BeginMainFrame block of a sampled frame, in microseconds. Note this primarily captures Javascript that explicitly requires Style and Layout, but not implicit requests such as through setting focus or scroll position.

metric.name: JavascriptIntersectionObserver
metric.summary: Time spent computing IntersectionObservations for javascript observers, in microseconds.

metric.name: JavascriptIntersectionObserverBeginMainFrame
metric.summary: The total main frame time used to compute IntersectionObservations for Javascript observers, in microseconds.

metric.name: Layout
metric.summary: The time taken for layout for the main frame in microseconds during the sampled frame.

metric.name: LayoutBeginMainFrame
metric.summary: The total main frame time used in layout, in microseconds.

metric.name: LazyLoadIntersectionObserver
metric.summary: Time spent computing IntersectionObservations for lazy loading, in microseconds.

metric.name: LazyLoadIntersectionObserverBeginMainFrame
metric.summary: The total main frame time used to compute IntersectionObservations for lazy loading, in microseconds.

metric.name: MainFrame
metric.summary: The time between a BeginMainFrame and the commit of paint results for the sampled frame, in microseconds.

metric.name: MainFrameIsBeforeFCP
metric.summary: True if the lifecycle update in this event occurred before FCP (First Contentful Paint) was registered.

metric.name: MainFrameReasons
metric.summary: A bitfield representation of the reasons for a main frame update. The fields are based on the FrameSequenceTrackerType enum, so bitwise and with the following values to determine if a reason was present: CompositorAnimation: 1, MainThreadAnimation: 2, PinchZoom: 4, RAF: 8, TouchScroll: 16, Universal: 32, Video: 64 and WheelScroll: 128.

metric.name: MediaIntersectionObserver
metric.summary: Time spent computing IntersectionObservations for media elements, in microseconds.

metric.name: MediaIntersectionObserverBeginMainFrame
metric.summary: The total main frame time used to compute IntersectionObservations for media elements, in microseconds.

metric.name: Paint
metric.summary: The time taken to paint for the main frame in microseconds during the sampled frame.

metric.name: PaintBeginMainFrame
metric.summary: The total main frame time used in paint, in microseconds.

metric.name: ParseStyleSheet
metric.summary: The time spent parsing style sheets.

metric.name: ParseStyleSheetBeginMainFrame
metric.summary: The total main frame time spent parsing style sheets.

metric.name: PermissionElementIntersectionObserver
metric.summary: Time spent computing IntersectionObservations for permission elements, in microseconds.

metric.name: PermissionElementIntersectionObserverBeginMainFrame
metric.summary: The total main frame time used to compute IntersectionObservations for permission elements, in microseconds.

metric.name: PossibleSynchronizedScrollCount2
metric.summary:
Counts whether a possible sync-scroll update is attempted during an animation update of the outermost main frame of a page (see sync_scroll_attempt_heuristic.h for details).
The heuristic is checked once per main frame update. The recorded value will be either 1 or 0 for a sampled frame depending on whether the heuristic determined that there was a possible sync-scroll update.

metric.name: PossibleSynchronizedScrollCount2BeginMainFrame
metric.summary:
Counts whether a possible sync-scroll update is attempted during an animation update of the outermost main frame of a page (see sync_scroll_attempt_heuristic.h for details).
The heuristic is checked once per main frame update. The recorded value will be either 1 or 0 for a sampled frame depending on whether the heuristic determined that there was a possible sync-scroll update.
Note that since the check happens during main frame updates, there should be no difference between this metric and PossibleSynchronizedScrollCount2 above.

metric.name: PrePaint
metric.summary: The time taken for pre-paint for the main frame in microseconds during the sampled frame.

metric.name: PrePaintBeginMainFrame
metric.summary: The total main frame time used in pre-paint, in microseconds.

metric.name: ServiceDocumentUpdate
metric.summary: The time spent doing forced document updates due to browser requested actions, such as accessibility, intersection observer or spell checking, during a sampled frame, in microseconds.

metric.name: ServiceDocumentUpdateBeginMainFrame
metric.summary: The time spent doing forced document updates due to browser requested actions, such as accessibility, intersection observer or spell checking, during the BeginMainFrame block of a sampled frame, in microseconds.

metric.name: Style
metric.summary: The time taken for style update for the main frame in microseconds during the sampled frame.

metric.name: StyleBeginMainFrame
metric.summary: The total main frame time spent updating style information and building the layout tree, in microseconds.

metric.name: StylePercentage
metric.summary: The percentage of the main frame time used in style work. An int in the range [0,100].

metric.name: UpdateLayers
metric.summary: The time taken for LayerTreeHost::UpdateLayers in microseconds during the sampled frame

metric.name: UpdateLayersBeginMainFrame
metric.summary: The total main frame time spent in LayerTreeHost::UpdateLayer, in microseconds. This should always match UpdateLayers and serves as a check.

metric.name: UpdateViewportIntersection
metric.summary: Time spent computing internal geometry information about iframes, in microseconds.

metric.name: UpdateViewportIntersectionBeginMainFrame
metric.summary: The total main frame time used to compute internal geometry information about iframes, in microseconds.

metric.name: UserDrivenDocumentUpdate
metric.summary:
The time spent doing forced document updates due to a user action such as input, find-in-page or printing, during a sampled frame, in microseconds.
As of 04/2023, updates for scrolling are included in this metric.

metric.name: UserDrivenDocumentUpdateBeginMainFrame
metric.summary:
The time spent doing forced document updates due to a user action such as input, find-in-page or printing, during the BeginMainFrame block of a sampled frame, in microseconds.
As of 04/2023, updates for scrolling are included in this metric.

metric.name: VisualUpdateDelay
metric.summary: Time elapsed between when an animation update is first scheduled and when the resulting BeginMainFrame starts.

metric.name: VisualUpdateDelayBeginMainFrame
metric.summary: Time elapsed between when an animation update is first scheduled and when the resulting BeginMainFrame starts.

metric.name: WaitForCommit
metric.summary: The time spent waiting for the compositor thread to begin processing a commit, in microseconds.

metric.name: WaitForCommitBeginMainFrame
metric.summary: The total main frame time spent waiting for the compositor thread to begin processing a commit, in microseconds. This should always match WaitForCommit and serves as a check.

event.name: Blink.UseCounter
event.summary:
Collects data for a subset of UseCounter features where UMA UseCounter data shows a behaviour that is rare but too common to blindly change. This metric should be used to reason about whether a breaking change is acceptable or not.
Note: In M87, a number of use counters were removed from UKM reporting due to being reported too frequently (high proportion across page loads).

metric.name: Feature
metric.summary: Opt-in UseCounter feature.

metric.name: IsMainFrameFeature
metric.summary: Emits True(1) or False(0) to indicate whether the Feature recorded is in the main frame or not.

event.name: BrowsingTopics.DocumentBrowsingTopicsApiResult2
event.summary: The document.browsingTopics() result and other metadata. Recorded whenever document.browsingTopics() is invoked from a context. If there are more than 3 candidate topics (e.g. due to custom configuration FeatureParam), only the first 3 candidate topics will be recorded.

metric.name: CandidateTopic0
