metric.summary: The candidate topic ID at index 0. This metric won't be recorded if no topics were derived from the candidate epochs (which can be due to insufficient epochs, failed epoch topics calculation, cleared history, or cleared/blocked individual topics).

metric.name: CandidateTopic0IsTrueTopTopic
metric.summary: Whether `CandidateTopic0` is a true top topic (as opposed to a padded or random one). This metric will be recorded iff `CandidateTopic0` is recorded.

metric.name: CandidateTopic0ModelVersion
metric.summary: The version of the model used to calculate `CandidateTopic0`. This metric will be recorded iff `CandidateTopic0` is recorded.

metric.name: CandidateTopic0ShouldBeFiltered
metric.summary: Whether `CandidateTopic0` should be filtered. This metric will be recorded iff `CandidateTopic0` is recorded.

metric.name: CandidateTopic0TaxonomyVersion
metric.summary: The version of the taxonomy applicable to `CandidateTopic0`. This metric will be recorded iff `CandidateTopic0` is recorded.

metric.name: CandidateTopic1
metric.summary: The candidate topic ID at index 1. This metric won't be recorded if less than 2 topics were derived from the candidate epochs (which can be due to insufficient epochs, failed epoch topics calculation, cleared history, or cleared/blocked individual topics).

metric.name: CandidateTopic1IsTrueTopTopic
metric.summary: Whether `CandidateTopic1` is a true top topic (as opposed to a padded or random one). This metric will be recorded iff `CandidateTopic1` is recorded.

metric.name: CandidateTopic1ModelVersion
metric.summary: The version of the model used to calculate `CandidateTopic1`. This metric will be recorded iff `CandidateTopic1` is recorded.

metric.name: CandidateTopic1ShouldBeFiltered
metric.summary: Whether `CandidateTopic1` should be filtered. This metric will be recorded iff `CandidateTopic1` is recorded.

metric.name: CandidateTopic1TaxonomyVersion
metric.summary: The version of the taxonomy applicable to `CandidateTopic1`. This metric will be recorded iff `CandidateTopic1` is recorded.

metric.name: CandidateTopic2
metric.summary: The candidate topic ID at index 2. This metric won't be recorded if less than 3 topics were derived from the candidate epochs (which can be due to insufficient epochs, failed epoch topics calculation, cleared history, or cleared/blocked individual topics).

metric.name: CandidateTopic2IsTrueTopTopic
metric.summary: Whether `CandidateTopic2` is a true top topic (as opposed to a padded or random one). This metric will be recorded iff `CandidateTopic2` is recorded.

metric.name: CandidateTopic2ModelVersion
metric.summary: The version of the model used to calculate `CandidateTopic2`. This metric will be recorded iff `CandidateTopic2` is recorded.

metric.name: CandidateTopic2ShouldBeFiltered
metric.summary: Whether `CandidateTopic2` should be filtered. This metric will be recorded iff `CandidateTopic2` is recorded.

metric.name: CandidateTopic2TaxonomyVersion
metric.summary: The version of the taxonomy applicable to `CandidateTopic2`. This metric will be recorded iff `CandidateTopic2` is recorded.

metric.name: FailureReason
metric.summary: The reason why the API access is not allowed.

event.name: BrowsingTopics.EpochTopicsCalculationResult
event.summary: The epoch topics calculation result, including the top 5 topics and other metadata. Recorded whenever the periodic (weekly) topics calculation has finished. If the result has more than 5 topics (e.g. due to custom configuration FeatureParam), only the first 5 topics will be recorded.

metric.name: ModelVersion
metric.summary: The version of the model used to calculate the result topics. This metric won't be recorded if the topics calculation failed in general.

metric.name: PaddedTopicsStartIndex
metric.summary: The start index of the randomly padded top topics. This metric won't be recorded if the topics calculation failed in general. If all topics are real, `PaddedTopicsStartIndex` will equal the feature param `kBrowsingTopicsNumberOfTopTopicsPerEpoch`.

metric.name: TaxonomyVersion
metric.summary: The version of the taxonomy applicable to the result topics. This metric won't be recorded if the topics calculation failed in general.

metric.name: TopTopic0
metric.summary: The top topic ID at index 0. This metric won't be recorded if the candidate topic was blocked, or if the topics calculation failed in general.

metric.name: TopTopic1
metric.summary: The top topic ID at index 1. This metric won't be recorded if the candidate topic was blocked, or if the topics calculation failed in general.

metric.name: TopTopic2
metric.summary: The top topic ID at index 2. This metric won't be recorded if the candidate topic was blocked, or if the topics calculation failed in general.

metric.name: TopTopic3
metric.summary: The top topic ID at index 3. This metric won't be recorded if the candidate topic was blocked, or if the topics calculation failed in general.

metric.name: TopTopic4
metric.summary: The top topic ID at index 4. This metric won't be recorded if the candidate topic was blocked, or if the topics calculation failed in general.

event.name: BrowsingTopics.PageLoad
event.summary: PageLoad metrics for BrowsingTopics. Recorded when a page has Topics API usages (that pass the user permission check), and the page is being destroyed/navigated.

metric.name: TopicsRequestingContextDomainsCount
metric.summary: The number of unique context domains have requested the topics. Rounded to the nearest exponential bucket (with a bucket ratio of 1.15).

event.name: BrowsingTopics.TopicsRedirectChainDetected
event.summary: Records the redirect state related to Topics. Recorded when a page is detected to have initiated a chain of redirects, and at least two pages within that chain invoke the Topics API, suggesting potential misuse of the API. This event can be logged multiple times for the same page if the redirect chain continues to grow.

metric.name: NumberOfPagesCallingTopics
metric.summary: The number of pages that invoked the Topics API in the redirect chain.

event.name: ChargeEventHistory
event.summary: Records history of charge events including start time and duration of charges. Can be used with other ChargeEventHistory to track the last 30 days of charging events. If a "SmartCharging" event and a "ChargeEventHistory" event has them same source ID and "EventId", it means "ChargeEventHistory" is the charge history when "SmartCharging" event is logged. ChargeEventHistory records an array. Each ChargeEventHistory event records an item in the array, specified by its index ChargeEventHistoryIndex. Items in the same array will have same source ID and EventId.

metric.name: ChargeEventHistoryDuration
metric.summary: Duration of a history charge event.

metric.name: ChargeEventHistoryIndex
metric.summary: Index of this event in the ChargeEventHistory array. Please see the event summary for more details.

metric.name: ChargeEventHistorySize
metric.summary: Number of items in ChargeEventHistory array. Please see the event summary for more details.

metric.name: ChargeEventHistoryStartTime
metric.summary: Duration (in minutes) between start time of a charge event and logging time.

metric.name: EventId
metric.summary: A unique number that represent order of an event. Used combined with "SmartCharging"'s EventId to reconstruct charge history.

event.name: ChromeOS.DeviceManagement
event.summary: Records the ChromeOS device management state. This event is recorded once per UKM report and is not associated with a source URL.

metric.name: EnrollmentStatus
metric.summary: An enum value representing the enrollment status of a ChromeOS device.

event.name: ChromeOS.WebsiteUsageTime
event.summary: Records the amount of time that the focused and activated website was accessed in last two hours on Chrome OS. This is logged each two hours, and only when the website is not installed as a PWA or Chrome app.

metric.name: Duration
metric.summary: The milliseconds that the website was accessed in the focused tab and the activated browser window. The accessing time is calculated each two hours, then the noise is applied, and sum the running time for two hours with noise to calculate the two hours accessing time. For example the running time in the past 2 hours = time1 * noise1 + time2 * noise2 + time3 * noise3....

metric.name: IsFromLastLogin
metric.summary: Whether the metrics is read from the user pref on disk, recorded for the last login.

metric.name: Promotable
metric.summary: Whether the website can be an installable web app.

metric.name: UserDeviceMatrix
metric.summary: An enum that records the user type and the device type.

event.name: ChromeOSApp.InputEvent
event.summary: Records each two hours in Chrome OS to log the input events happened on the app window. This is logged only when the user allows app syncing or allows sync of everything, and the user does not set up a sync passphrase.

metric.name: AppInputEventCount
metric.summary: The number of the input events happened in each two hours.

metric.name: AppInputEventSource
metric.summary: An enum that records the input event source.

metric.name: AppType
metric.summary: An enum that records the app type.

metric.name: UserDeviceMatrix
metric.summary: An enum that records the user type and the device type.

event.name: ChromeOSApp.InstalledApp
event.summary: Records the installed app in Chrome OS, only when the user allows app syncing or allows sync of everything, and the user does not set up a sync passphrase. Only ARC apps, Chrome apps and web apps(PWA), system web apps, buildin apps are recorded. This is logged when the user installs a new app.

metric.name: AppType
metric.summary: An enum that records the app type.

metric.name: InstallReason
metric.summary: An enum that records the reason why the app was installed.

metric.name: InstallSource2
metric.summary: An enum that records where the app was installed.

metric.name: InstallTime
metric.summary: An enum that records when the app was installed.

metric.name: UserDeviceMatrix
metric.summary: An enum that records the user type and the device type.

event.name: ChromeOSApp.Launch
event.summary: Records a launched app in Chrome OS, only when the user allows app syncing or allows sync of everything, and the user does not set up a sync passphrase. Only ARC apps, Chrome apps and web apps(PWA), system web apps, buildin apps are recorded. This is logged when an app is launched.

metric.name: AppType
metric.summary: An enum that records the app type.

metric.name: LaunchSource
metric.summary: An enum that records where the app was launched.

metric.name: UserDeviceMatrix
metric.summary: An enum that records the user type and the device type.

event.name: ChromeOSApp.UninstallApp
event.summary: Records the uninstalled app in Chrome OS, only when the user allows app syncing or allows sync of everything, and the user does not set up a sync passphrase. Only ARC apps, Chrome apps and web apps(PWA), system web apps, buildin apps are recorded. This is logged when the user uninstalls an app.

metric.name: AppType
metric.summary: An enum that records the app type.

metric.name: UninstallSource
metric.summary: An enum that records how the app was uninstalled.

metric.name: UserDeviceMatrix
metric.summary: An enum that records the user type and the device type.

event.name: ChromeOSApp.UsageTime
event.summary: Records the amount of time that the app was used in the last two hours on Chrome OS, only when the user allows app syncing or allows sync of everything, and the user does not set up a sync passphrase. Only ARC apps, Chrome apps and web apps(PWA), system web apps, builtin apps are recorded. This is logged every two hours and at shutdown.

metric.name: AppType
metric.summary: An enum that records the app type.

metric.name: Duration
metric.summary: The milliseconds that the app was used.

metric.name: UserDeviceMatrix
metric.summary: An enum that records the user type and the device type.

event.name: ChromeOSApp.UsageTimeReusedSourceId
event.summary: Records the amount of time that the app were used in the last two hours on Chrome OS, only when the user allows app syncing or allows sync of everything, and the user does not set up a sync passphrase. Only ARC apps, Chrome apps and web apps(PWA), system web apps, buildin apps are recorded. This is logged every two hours.

metric.name: AppType
metric.summary: An enum that records the app type.

metric.name: Duration
metric.summary: The milliseconds that the app was used.

metric.name: UserDeviceMatrix
metric.summary: An enum that records the user type and the device type.

event.name: ClickInput
event.summary: Metrics associated with user click input. In particular, captures when user has a burst of clicks at the same location which may be a signal of user unhappiness for an experiment if it differs from the baseline data for the site.

metric.name: Experimental.ClickInputBurst
metric.summary: The count of the largest burst of click inputs for a page load. Only recorded if it meets a minimum threshold to be considered a burst.

event.name: ClientHints.AcceptCHFrameUsage
event.summary: Record when a Client Hint is requested in an ACCEPT_CH TLS Frame.

metric.name: Type
metric.summary: Records an enum representing the specific Client Hint type that was requested.

event.name: ClientHints.AcceptCHHeaderUsage
event.summary: Record when a Client Hint is requested in a Accept-CH HTTP Response Header.

metric.name: Type
metric.summary: Records an enum representing the specific Client Hint type that was requested.

event.name: ClientHints.AcceptCHMetaUsage
event.summary: Record when a Client Hint is requested in a Accept-CH HTML Meta Tag.

metric.name: Type
metric.summary: Records an enum representing the specific Client Hint type that was requested.

event.name: ClientHints.CriticalCHHeaderUsage
event.summary: Record when a Client Hint is requested in a Critical-CH HTTP Response Header.

metric.name: Type
metric.summary: Records an enum representing the specific Client Hint type that was requested.

event.name: ClientHints.DelegateCHMetaUsage
event.summary: Record when a Client Hint is requested in a Delegate-CH HTML Meta Tag.

metric.name: Type
metric.summary: Records an enum representing the specific Client Hint type that was requested.

event.name: ClientRenderingAPI
event.summary: Metrics collecting types of rendering context created with canvas/offscreencanvas. It also collects requests of GPU device via WebGPU API.

metric.name: Canvas.RenderingContext
metric.summary: Type of rendering contexts created for HTMLCanvasElement.

metric.name: Canvas.RenderingContextDrawnTo
metric.summary: Type of rendering contexts created for HTMLCanvasElement that something has actually been drawn to. For WebGL 1 and 2 context this means there is at least one draw call issued. For other context types we leave the implementation blank at this moment.

metric.name: GPUDevice
metric.summary: Event fired when a GPUDevice from the WebGPU API is created.

metric.name: OffscreenCanvas.RenderingContext
metric.summary: Type of rendering contexts created for OffscreenCanvas.

metric.name: OffscreenCanvas.RenderingContextDrawnTo
metric.summary: Type of rendering contexts created for OffscreenCanvas that something has actually been drawn to. For WebGL 1 and 2 context this means there is at least one draw call issued. For other context types we leave the implementation blank at this moment.

event.name: Companion.PageView
event.summary: Records metrics for the companion side panel. The events correspond to a single page load in the main frame and the metrics are flushed when the page navigates away. For most of the UI surfaces, only the last event (e.g. displayed or clicked) is recorded.

metric.name: ATX.ComponentPosition
metric.summary: The position of the ATX surface in the page.

metric.name: ATX.LastEvent
metric.summary: The last event on the ATX surface.

metric.name: CQ.ClickPosition
metric.summary: The position of the CQ element that was clicked with respect to its parent list.

metric.name: CQ.ComponentPosition
metric.summary: The position of the CQ surface in the page.

metric.name: CQ.LastEvent
metric.summary: The last event on the CQ surface.

metric.name: CQ.NumEntriesAvailable
metric.summary: The number of child elements available within the CQ surface after eligibility check after text finder API. Clamped to a max value of 10.

metric.name: CQ.NumEntriesShown
metric.summary: The number of child elements actually shown within the CQ surface after eligibility check through text finder API. Clamped to a max value of 10.

metric.name: OpenTrigger
metric.summary: The UI location from which the companion page was opened.

metric.name: PageEntities.ClickPosition
metric.summary: The position of the page entities element that was clicked with respect to its parent list.

metric.name: PageEntities.ComponentPosition
metric.summary: The position of the page entities surface in the page.

metric.name: PageEntities.LastEvent
metric.summary: The last event on the page entities surface.

metric.name: PageEntities.NumEntriesAvailable
metric.summary: The number of child elements available within the page entities surface. Clamped to a max value of 10.

metric.name: PageEntities.NumEntriesShown
metric.summary: The number of child elements shown within the page entities surface. Clamped to a max value of 10.

metric.name: PH.ComponentPosition
metric.summary: The position of the PH surface in the page.

metric.name: PH.Feedback
metric.summary: The last user feedback event on the PH surface.

metric.name: PH.LastEvent
metric.summary: The last event on the PH surface.

metric.name: PHResult.ClickPosition
metric.summary: The position of the PH element that was clicked with respect to its parent list.

metric.name: PHResult.ComponentPosition
metric.summary: The position of the PH surface in the page.

metric.name: PHResult.LastEvent
metric.summary: The last event on the PH surface.

metric.name: PHResult.NumEntriesAvailable
metric.summary: The number of child elements available within the PH surface, regardless of text finder API results. Clamped to a max value of 10.

metric.name: PHResult.NumEntriesShown
metric.summary: The number of child elements within the PH surface that pass text finder API elibigility check, this is the count bullet points that have jump tags. Clamped to a max value of 10.

metric.name: PromoEvent
metric.summary: The last event on the promo surface.

metric.name: RegionSearch.ClickCount
metric.summary: The number of times region search in the multimodal box was clicked.

metric.name: RelQr.ClickPosition
metric.summary: The position of the RelQr element that was clicked with respect to its parent list.

metric.name: RelQr.ComponentPosition
metric.summary: The position of the RelQr surface in the page.

metric.name: RelQr.LastEvent
metric.summary: The last event on the RelQr surface.

metric.name: RelQr.NumEntriesAvailable
metric.summary: The number of child elements available within the RelQr surface. Clamped to a max value of 10.

metric.name: RelQr.NumEntriesShown
metric.summary: The number of child elements shown within the RelQr surface. Clamped to a max value of 10.

metric.name: RelQs.ClickPosition
metric.summary: The position of the RelQs element that was clicked with respect to its parent list.

metric.name: RelQs.ComponentPosition
metric.summary: The position of the RelQs surface in the page.

metric.name: RelQs.LastEvent
metric.summary: The last event on the RelQs surface.

metric.name: RelQs.NumEntriesAvailable
metric.summary: The number of child elements available within the RelQs surface. Clamped to a max value of 10.

metric.name: RelQs.NumEntriesShown
metric.summary: The number of child elements shown within the RelQs surface. Clamped to a max value of 10.

metric.name: TextSearchCount
metric.summary: The number of times text search box was used, clamped to a max of 10.

metric.name: VQ.ClickPosition
metric.summary: The position of the VQ element that was clicked with respect to its parent list.

metric.name: VQ.ComponentPosition
metric.summary: The position of the VQ surface in the page.

metric.name: VQ.LastEvent
metric.summary: The last event on the VQ surface.

metric.name: VQ.NumEntriesAvailable
metric.summary: The number of child elements available within the VQ surface. Clamped to a max value of 10.

metric.name: VQ.NumEntriesShown
metric.summary: The number of child elements shown within the VQ surface. Clamped to a max value of 10.

metric.name: VQS.ImageSensitiveCount
metric.summary: The number of images classified as sensitive. Uses exponential bucket of 1.3 for privacy.

metric.name: VQS.ImageShoppyCount
metric.summary: The number of images classified as shoppy. Uses exponential bucket of 1.3 for privacy.

metric.name: VQS.ImageShoppyNotSensitiveCount
metric.summary: The number of images classified as shoppy and non-sensitive. Uses exponential bucket of 1.3 for privacy.

metric.name: VQS.VisualEligibleImagesCount
metric.summary: The number of images eligible for visual classification. Uses exponential bucket of 1.3 for privacy.

metric.name: VQS.VisualSearchTriggeredCount
metric.summary: The number of images that qualify for visual suggestions. Uses exponential bucket of 1.3 for privacy.

event.name: Compose.PageEvents
event.summary: Reported at PrimaryPageChanged (or tab destruction) if any metrics have been recorded. This collects user actions in the compose feature (and some of its effects) for this page.

metric.name: ComposeTextInserted
metric.summary: The number of times the Compose result was inserted into the page. Uses exponential buckets for privacy.

metric.name: MenuItemClicked
metric.summary: The number of times the Compose context menu item was clicked for an element on the page. Uses exponential buckets for privacy.

metric.name: MenuItemShown
metric.summary: The number of times the Compose context menu item was shown for an element on the page. Uses exponential buckets for privacy.

metric.name: MissingFormData
metric.summary: The number of times the Compose context menu item was clicked but the expected dialog was not presented because Autofill could not obtain the text field's respective FormData. Uses exponential buckets for privacy.

metric.name: MissingFormFieldData
metric.summary: The number of times the Compose context menu item was clicked but the expected dialog was not presented because Autofill could not obtain the text field's respective FormFieldData. Uses exponential buckets for privacy.

metric.name: ProactiveNudgeDisabledForSite
metric.summary: True if the proactive nudge was disabled for this site during this page load. Added in M126.

metric.name: ProactiveNudgeDisabledGlobally
metric.summary: True if the proactive nudge was globally disabled this page load. Added in M126.

metric.name: ProactiveNudgeOpened
metric.summary: The number of times the Compose dialog is opened from the proactive nudge during this page load. Recorded everytime the nudge is opened. Added in M126.

metric.name: ProactiveNudgeShouldShow
metric.summary: The number of times the Compose proactive nudge should show during this page load. Recorded any time Compose is asked to show the proactive nudge even if it is eventually not shown. Added in M125.

metric.name: ProactiveNudgeShown
metric.summary: The number of times the Compose proactive nudge is shown during this page load. Recorded everytime a request to show the nudge returns true. Added in M126.

event.name: Compose.SessionProgress
event.summary: Reported at the end of every compose session. There may be more than one compose session per pageload. This collects user actions in the compose dialogs for this URL.

metric.name: Canceled
metric.summary: If the user canceled this session with the "X" button. This does not include dismissing the dialog by losing focus.

metric.name: CasualCount
metric.summary: How many times the user pressed the "shorten" button in this session. Uses exponential buckets for privacy.

metric.name: ComposeCount
metric.summary: How many times the user pressed the "generate" button in this session. Uses exponential buckets for privacy.

metric.name: DialogShownCount
metric.summary: How many times the compose dialog was shown to the user. Uses exponential buckets for privacy.

metric.name: FormalCount
metric.summary: How many times the user pressed the "formal" button in this session. Uses exponential buckets for privacy.

metric.name: InsertedResults
metric.summary: If the user inserted results back to the web page in this session, either by pressing the "insert" or "replace" button.

metric.name: LengthenCount
metric.summary: How many times the user pressed the "elaborate" button in this session. Uses exponential buckets for privacy.

metric.name: RegenerateCount
metric.summary: How many times the user pressed the "regenerate" button in this session. Uses exponential buckets for privacy.

metric.name: ShortenCount
metric.summary: How many times the user pressed the "shorten" button in this session. Uses exponential buckets for privacy.

metric.name: StartedWithProactiveNudge
metric.summary: True if the session started with the proactive nudge rather than the context menu option. Added in M126.

metric.name: UndoCount
metric.summary: How many times the user pressed the "undo" button in this session. Uses exponential buckets for privacy.

event.name: Compose.TextElementUsage
event.summary: Reported once per page load per each multiline text element that received input. Excludes potentially sensitive fields including input type=password or when autofill determines the field is sensitive.

metric.name: AutofillFormControlType
metric.summary: The control type, such as text or textarea. See |autofill::FormControlType|.

metric.name: EditingTime
metric.summary: Number of seconds the user spent editing the field. Includes time between key presses, up to 5 seconds each. Bucketed using GetExponentialBucketMinForUserTiming().

metric.name: FieldSignature
metric.summary: The signature of the field (mod 1021). This is the hash identifier used to denote this field. See components/autofill/core/common/signatures.cc for more details.

metric.name: FormSignature
metric.summary: The signature of the form (mod 1021). This is the hash identifier used to denote this form. See components/autofill/core/common/signatures.cc for more details.

metric.name: IsAutofillFieldType
metric.summary: Whether the field is detected and supported by Autofill.

metric.name: TypedCharacterCount
metric.summary: Total number of characters typed into the text element. Bucketed with an exponential factor of 2. This is reported as -1 for sensitive field types, like passwords or credit cards numbers. Note that this number attempts to count only typed changes, but is not always accurate. Additionally, there is a limit to the total possible length observed.

metric.name: TypedWordCount
metric.summary: Total number of space-delimited words typed into the text element. Bucketed with an exponential factor of 2. This is reported as -1 for sensitive field types like passwords or credit cards numbers. Note that this number attempts to count only typed changes, but is not always accurate. Additionally, there is a limit to the total possible length observed.

event.name: Compositor.Rendering
event.summary: Metrics related to rendering in the compositor.

metric.name: CheckerboardedImagesCount
metric.summary: The number of images checker-imaged and re-rasterized on this page.

event.name: Compositor.UserInteraction
event.summary: Metrics related to user interaction handled by the compositor. This includes user gestures like scrolling, pinch-zoom and page-scale animations triggered in response to a double-tap event. The length of the user interaction is defined as the time the compositor is running an animation resulting from user input.

metric.name: CheckerboardedContentArea
metric.summary: The number of visible pixels per frame checkerboarded during this interaction.

metric.name: CheckerboardedContentAreaRatio
metric.summary: The percentage of visible pixels per frame checkerboarded during this interaction. This value should be between [0, 100].

metric.name: CheckerboardedImagesCount
metric.summary: The number of images checker-imaged and re-rasterized during this interaction.

metric.name: NumMissingTiles
metric.summary: The number of visible tiles per frame checkerboarded during this interaction.

event.name: ContactsPicker.ShareStatistics
event.summary: Measures statistics about the contacts shared in the contacts picker (when the Contacts Picker dialog is dismissed.)

metric.name: PropertiesRequested
metric.summary: Measures the contact properties requested by the website (names, emails, telephone numbers). Bit 1 - telephone numbers, bit 2 - emails, bit 3 - names.

metric.name: SelectCount
metric.summary: Measures the total number of contacts selected in the contacts picker.

metric.name: SelectPercentage
metric.summary: Measures the percentage of contacts selected in the contacts picker.

event.name: ContentIndex.Added
event.summary: Collected when a new Content Index entry is registered.

metric.name: Category
metric.summary: The type of the content registered. Corresponds to blink.mojom.ContentCategory.

event.name: ContentIndex.DeletedByUser
event.summary: Collected when a Content Index entry is deleted by the user from the Downloads page.

metric.name: Deleted
metric.summary: Always true.

event.name: ContentIndex.Opened
event.summary: Collected when a Content Index entry is opened from the Downloads page.

metric.name: IsOffline
metric.summary: Whether the browser was offline when the content was opened.

event.name: ContentManagementSystemPageLoad
event.summary: Metrics stating whether the main frame of a page uses certain content management systems. This event is recorded when the page is going to be unloaded or when the app enters the background on Android. The content management system detection is done when the document is loaded (similar to when load event is fired). If a CMS is used after this then it would not be detected. For interoperability with the JavaScript framework detection above, this reports each CMS individually instead of a single enum.

metric.name: DrupalPageLoad
metric.summary: True if the page loaded in the main frame uses the Drupal CMS.

metric.name: JoomlaPageLoad
metric.summary: True if the page loaded in the main frame uses the Joomloa CMS.

metric.name: ShopifyPageLoad
metric.summary: True if the page loaded in the main frame uses the Shopify CMS.

metric.name: SquarespacePageLoad
metric.summary: True if the page loaded in the main frame uses the Squarespace CMS.

metric.name: WixPageLoad
metric.summary: True if the page loaded in the main frame uses the Wix CMS.

metric.name: WordPressPageLoad
metric.summary: True if the page loaded in the main frame uses the WordPress CMS.

metric.name: Action
metric.summary: Emits an integer id for a selection corresponding to the action enum defined in ChromeContextMenuPopulator.java (within the ContextMenuUma class).

metric.name: SearchByImage
metric.summary: Recorded when the context menu is constructed and logged if the search by image option was displayed.

metric.name: SearchWithGoogleLens
metric.summary: Recorded when the context menu is constructed and logged if the Lens option was displayed.

metric.name: ShopWithGoogleLens
metric.summary: Recorded when the context menu is constructed and logged if the Lens shopping option was displayed.

metric.name: ShopWithGoogleLensChip
metric.summary: Recorded when the context menu is constructed and logged if the Lens shopping chip was displayed.

metric.name: TranslateWithGoogleLensChip
metric.summary: Recorded when the context menu is constructed and logged if the Lens translate chip was displayed.

event.name: CookieHasNonAsciiCharacter
event.summary: A collection of metrics which are recorded for cookies which include at least one non-ascii unicode character in either their name or value fields. This metric is only interested in an event being generated, the value it records is meaningless.

metric.name: Name
metric.summary: Records events when a cookie has a non-ascii unicode character in its name field.

metric.name: Value
metric.summary: Records events when a cookie has a non-ascii unicode character in its value field.

event.name: Cookies.FirstPartyPartitionedInCrossSiteContextV3
event.summary: Recorded when a page loads that has a first-party partitioned cookie present and the cookie's name is not receive-cookie-deprecation.

metric.name: CookiePresent
metric.summary: Records true if one or more first-party partitioned cookies in a cross site context are present and the cookie's name is not receive-cookie-deprecation.

event.name: CPUUsageMeasurement
event.summary: A CPU usage measurement that corresponds to a tick in a CPU usage time-series. There is also some metadata stored to determine how faithful the CPU usage measurement is (e.g. NumberOfCoresidentTabs).

metric.name: CPUUsage
metric.summary: CPU usage measurement. This is in units of clock-cycles. For example, 1.2 seconds of 100% CPU usage on a 2.5GHz CPU corresponds to a value of 1.2 x 1.0 x 2.5e9 = 3e6.

metric.name: EventType
metric.summary: The event associated with the measurement. This is an integer value corresponding to the enum defined in resource_coordinator::RenderProcessProbe.

metric.name: IsVisible
metric.summary: Indicates if any frame hosted in this tab is visible at the moment the measurement is taken.

metric.name: NumberOfCoresidentTabs
metric.summary: Number of co-resident tabs in all of the render processes a tab is associated with when the CPU usage measurement is taken.

metric.name: ObservationWindowSizeMs
metric.summary: The size of the observation window (time since last CPU measurement) in milliseconds.

metric.name: ProcessUptimeMs
metric.summary: The uptime of the process being measured, in milliseconds.

event.name: CrossOriginSubframeWithoutEmbeddingControl
event.summary: Recorded when a subframe is embedded in a cross-origin context without having a CSP frame-ancestor header or an XFO header.

metric.name: SubframeEmbedded
metric.summary: Mesaures the number of pages with subframes embedded in cross-origin contexts without having a CSP frame-ancestor header or an XFO header.

event.name: DailyChargeSummary
event.summary: Records daily summaries of charging. DailyChargeSummary summarizes charging information (e.g. amount of time on full charge, amount of time on AC adapter, etc) in a day. It can be used in combination with other DailyChargeSummary events to get information about the last 30 days of charging. If a "SmartCharging" event and a "DailyChargeSummary" event has them same source ID and "EventId", it means "DailyChargeSummary" is the summary when "SmartCharging" event is logged. DailyChargeSummary records an array. Each event records an item in the array, specified by its index DailySummaryIndex. Items in the same array will have same source ID and EventId.

metric.name: DailySummaryHoldTimeOnAc
metric.summary: Amount of time (in minutes) the device was on AC charger and the charge was hold due to adaptive charging, at an index specified by DailySummaryIndex.

metric.name: DailySummaryIndex
metric.summary: Index of this event in the array. Please see the event summary for more details.

metric.name: DailySummaryNumDaysDistance
metric.summary: Distance in days bettwen current day and the day that the daily summary was logged.

metric.name: DailySummarySize
metric.summary: Number of items in array. Please see the event summary for more details.

metric.name: DailySummaryTimeFullOnAc
metric.summary: Amount of time (in minutes) the device was keep on AC charger with full battery (100%).

metric.name: DailySummaryTimeOnAc
metric.summary: Amount of time (in minutes) the device was keep on AC charger.

metric.name: EventId
metric.summary: A number that represent order of an event. Used combined with "SmartCharging"'s EventId to reconstruct charge history.

event.name: DataReductionProxy
event.summary: Data Reduction Proxy related metrics associated with a page load. See //components/data_reduction_proxy/.

metric.name: DataSaverPageUUID
metric.summary: A unique identifier of this page load to the proxy server. Recorded for each navigation that uses the proxy.

metric.name: EstimatedOriginalNetworkBytes
metric.summary: The estimated number of bytes that would have been used if that proxy was disabled on this page load. Recorded for each navigation that uses the proxy.

event.name: DevTools.Opened
event.summary: Records that the DevTools were opened by the user.

metric.name: HasOccurred
metric.summary: A boolean signaling that the DevTools menu was opened by the user; only records true values.

event.name: DIPS.ChainBegin
event.summary: Emitted after a redirect chain terminates, but for the page visited before the chain began -- the initial URL.

metric.name: ChainId
metric.summary: A randomly-generated 32-bit id to connect this event with the DIPS.Redirect and DIPS.ChainEnd events emitted for the same redirect chain.

metric.name: InitialAndFinalSitesSame
metric.summary: True when the site before the redirect chain is the same as the site at the end of the chain.

event.name: DIPS.ChainEnd
event.summary: Emitted for the page visited at the end of a redirect chain -- the final URL.

metric.name: ChainId
metric.summary: A randomly-generated 32-bit id to connect this event with the DIPS.ChainBegin and DIPS.Redirect events emitted for the same redirect chain.

metric.name: InitialAndFinalSitesSame
metric.summary: True when the site before the redirect chain is the same as the site at the end of the chain.

event.name: DIPS.Deletion
event.summary: Recorded when DIPS judges a site to be an incidental party and deletes its state if specific conditions are met.

metric.name: HasCookieException
metric.summary: True iff the user has a third-party cookies blocking exception for the site (as a third-party).

metric.name: IsDeletionEnabled
metric.summary: True iff site data deletion is enabled for the DIPS service.

metric.name: ShouldBlockThirdPartyCookies
metric.summary: True when third-party cookies are blocked.

event.name: DIPS.NavigationFlowNode
event.summary: When a user visits three consecutive pages A->B->C within a tab, B accesses cookies or storage, and the site (eTLD+1) for B is different from the sites of A and C, then this event is recorded.

metric.name: DidHaveSuccessfulWAA
metric.summary: True if this page had a successful WebAuthn assertion during the visit.

metric.name: DidHaveUserActivation
metric.summary: True if this page received user activation (click, etc.) during the visit.

metric.name: VisitDurationMilliseconds
metric.summary: The number of milliseconds this page was the primary page for, exponentially bucketed. The duration increases as long as the page is the primary page in its tab, even if the tab is not the active tab.

metric.name: WasEntryUserInitiated
metric.summary: True if the navigation from the prior page to this page was initiated by a user gesture (e.g., typing a URL into the omnibar, clicking a link, etc.).

metric.name: WasExitUserInitiated
metric.summary: True if the navigation from this page to the next page was initiated by a user gesture (e.g., typing a URL into the omnibar, clicking a link, etc.).

metric.name: WereEntryAndExitRendererInitiated
metric.summary: True if both the navigation from the prior page to this page, as well as the navigation from this page to the next page, were initiated from the renderer (e.g., by clicking a link in page content, etc.).

metric.name: WerePreviousAndNextSiteSame
metric.summary: True if the site (eTLD+1) for the page visited immediately prior to this one (in the same tab) is the same as the site for the page visited immediately after this one (in the same tab).

event.name: DIPS.Redirect
event.summary: Recorded on every HTTP redirect or client-side bounce, where a client-side bounce is a non-user-initiated navigation starting soon after the previous navigation committed.

metric.name: ChainId
metric.summary: A randomly-generated 32-bit id to connect this event with other DIPS.Redirect events and the DIPS.ChainBegin/ChainEnd events emitted for the same redirect chain.

metric.name: ClientBounceDelay
metric.summary: Populated for client redirects only. The time in seconds (capped at 10) between the page committing and the redirect navigation starting. An int in the range [0, 10].

metric.name: CookieAccessType
metric.summary: Whether the redirect read cookies, wrote cookies, both, or neither.

metric.name: HasStickyActivation
metric.summary: Populated for client redirects only. Whether the user interacted with the page (See the HTML spec for a definition). This info is persistem within DIPS for a duration of `dips::kInteractionTtl`.

metric.name: InitialAndFinalSitesSame
metric.summary: True when the site before the redirect chain is the same as the site at the end of the chain.

metric.name: IsPartialRedirectChain
metric.summary: True if the chain is missing the end URL. Any comparisons to the final site are invalid for this redirect.

metric.name: RedirectAndFinalSiteSame
metric.summary: True when the redirect site is the same as the site at the end of the redirect chain.

metric.name: RedirectAndInitialSiteSame
metric.summary: True when the redirect site is the same as the site before the redirect chain.

metric.name: RedirectChainIndex
metric.summary: 0-based index of this individual redirect within its chain.

metric.name: RedirectChainLength
metric.summary: The length of the overall redirect chain.

metric.name: RedirectType
metric.summary: The type of the redirect: client or server.

metric.name: SiteEngagementLevel
metric.summary: The site engagement level of the redirect URL, at the time the redirect took place.

metric.name: WebAuthnAssertionRequestSucceeded
metric.summary: Populated for client redirects only. Whether the user triggered a successful web authn assertion. This info is persistem within DIPS for a duration of `dips::kInteractionTtl`.

event.name: DIPS.TriggeringAction
event.summary:
Recorded on a repeated interval when DIPS may want to clear site storage to improve user privacy. These metrics will be used to determine which triggering action is most appropriate for our use case.
When these cases occur, the DIPS instance recording these metrics will keep track of which triggering actions have already happened to prevent logging duplicate metrics.

metric.name: DidBounce
metric.summary: True for sites that redirected the user since the last time the DIPS instance attempted to clear site storage.

metric.name: DidStatefulBounce
metric.summary: True for sites that redirected the user while accessing storage since the last time the DIPS instance attempted to clear site storage.

metric.name: UsedStorage
metric.summary: True for sites wrote to the browser's storage since the last time the DIPS instance attempted to clear site storage.

event.name: DocumentCreated
event.summary: Recorded when a Document object is created and records the source ID of the navigation and whether the document was in the main frame. This can be used to link subframe UKM events to the parent document.

metric.name: IsCrossOriginFrame
metric.summary: Whether the document was in a cross origin iframe. This can either be 0 or 1.

metric.name: IsCrossSiteFrame
metric.summary: Whether the document was in a cross site iframe. This can either be 0 or 1.

metric.name: IsMainFrame
metric.summary: Whether the document was in the main frame. This is can either be 0 or 1.

metric.name: NavigationSourceId
metric.summary: Contains the UKM source id of the navigation as an integer.

event.name: Download.Completed
event.summary: Metrics taken when a download completes. Its parent event is Download.Started.

metric.name: BytesWasted
metric.summary: The number of bytes that have been used in an excess (ie. download restarting the middle of a download).

metric.name: DownloadId
metric.summary: The id of the download that is used to associate separate events.

metric.name: ResultingFileSize
metric.summary: The size of the file that is downloaded, expressed in kilobytes with exponentially growing buckets.

metric.name: TimeSinceStart
metric.summary: The difference in the time between when the download finished and when it began, expressed in milliseconds.

event.name: Download.Interrupted
event.summary: Metrics taken when a download is interrupted. Its parent event is Download.Started.

metric.name: BytesWasted
metric.summary: The number of bytes that have been used in an excess (ie. download restarting the middle of a download).

metric.name: ChangeInFileSize
metric.summary: The difference in the size of the file that is downloaded compared to the initial reported size, expressed in kilobytes with exponentially growing buckets.

metric.name: DownloadId
metric.summary: The id of the download that is used to associate separate events.

metric.name: Reason
metric.summary: The reason the download was interrupted, expressed as an enum defined in DownloadInterruptReason.

metric.name: ResultingFileSize
metric.summary: The size of the file that is downloaded, expressed in kilobytes with exponentially growing buckets.

metric.name: TimeSinceStart
metric.summary: The difference in the time between when the download interrupted and when it began, expressed in milliseconds.

event.name: Download.Resumed
event.summary: Metrics taken when a download is resumed. Its parent event is Download.Started.

metric.name: DownloadId
metric.summary: The id of the download that is used to associate separate events.

metric.name: Mode
metric.summary: The mode by which the download was resumed, expressed as an enum defined in ResumeMode.

metric.name: TimeSinceStart
metric.summary: The difference in the time between when the download resumed and when it began, expressed in milliseconds.

event.name: Download.Started
event.summary: Metrics taken when a download begins. It has one Download.Ended and none to multiple Download.Interrupted/Download.Resumed events associated with it.

metric.name: DownloadConnectionSecurity
metric.summary: The state of the security of the final download URL and all the redirects leading to it. Expressed as an enum defined in DownloadConnectionSecurity.

metric.name: DownloadId
metric.summary: The id of the download that is used to associate separate events.

metric.name: DownloadSource
metric.summary: The source of the download, expressed as an enum defined in DownloadEntry.

metric.name: FileType
metric.summary: The type of file that is downloaded, expressed as an enum defined in DownloadContentType.

metric.name: IsSameHostDownload
metric.summary: A boolean denoting if the final download URL is the same host as the initiating frame (i.e., whether the initiating site likely controls the download itself).

event.name: Enterprise.Profile.Enrollment
event.summary: Metrics related to Profiles enrolling into Enterprise cloud management.

metric.name: IsUntrustedOidcRedirect
metric.summary: A boolean capturing that a Profile enrollment flow was blocked due to a lack of trust in the chain of navigation redirects.

event.name: Event.Scroll
event.summary: Metrics related to scroll and scroll jank,

metric.name: FrameCount
PredictorJankyFrameCount
metric.summary: The number of frames that are deemed janky to the human eye after Chrome has applied its scroll prediction algorithm..

metric.name: ScrollJank.DelayedFrameCount
metric.summary: The number of delayed frames.

metric.name: ScrollJank.MissedVsyncsMax
metric.summary: The maximum number of vsyncs missed during any and all janks.

metric.name: ScrollJank.MissedVsyncsSum
metric.summary: The total number of vsyncs missed during any and all janks that occurred.

metric.name: VsyncCount
metric.summary: The number of vsyncs that occur during the scroll. If this is less than or equal to 16, then the number of vsyncs is considered small; if this is greater than 16 and less than or equal to 64 vsyncs, then it is considered medium; if this is greater than 64 vsyncs, then is considered large.

event.name: Event.ScrollJank.PredictorJank
event.summary: Recorded for scroll update events only if the conditions for janky predictor metrics are met - if the ratio of displacement between any consecutive frames is above a particular threshold. Threshold depends on scroll speed and screen displacement in pixels.

metric.name: MaxDelta
metric.summary: The maximum scroll delta at a given update, when compared to its two neighbors. If this value is less than 7 (kScrollDeltaThreshold in PredictorJankTracker), then the scroll is considered slow. Above this threshold, the scroll is considered fast. This also helps to determine what the threshold for jank should be.

metric.name: ScrollUpdate.MissedVsync.FrameAboveJankyThreshold2
metric.summary: The total number of times that there was a larger displacement at a given frame relative to its neighboring frames in the sequence, if the previous vsync was missed.

metric.name: ScrollUpdate.NoMissedVsync.FrameAboveJankyThreshold2
metric.summary: The total number of times that there was a larger displacement at a given frame relative to its neighboring frames in the sequence, if the previous vsync was not missed.

event.name: Extensions.BackgroundContentsCreated
event.summary: Recorded each time a background contents creates its first renderer by navigating its initial WebContents.

metric.name: Seen
metric.summary: Dummy Boolean as UKM does not support events with no metrics.

event.name: Extensions.MV2ExtensionHandledInSoftDisable
event.summary: Recorded each time a user either re-enables or removes an extension affected by the Manifest V2 deprecation during the "soft disable" deprecation phase. Recorded when the user takes this action. This is only recorded for extensions that update from the Chrome Web Store.

metric.name: Action
metric.summary: The action the user took on the extension.

event.name: Extensions.OnNavigation
event.summary: Records the extension related metrics that might have negative impacts on LCP. This is recorded when navigation is finished.

metric.name: DeclarativeNetRequestFeedbackPermissionCount
metric.summary: The number of enabled extensions that have a DeclarativeNetRequestFeedback permission. The exponential bucketing is applied for privacy reasons by using ukm::GetExponentialBucketMin(value, 2).

metric.name: DeclarativeNetRequestPermissionCount
metric.summary: The number of enabled extensions that have a DeclarativeNetRequest permission. The exponential bucketing is applied for privacy reasons by using ukm::GetExponentialBucketMin(value, 2).

metric.name: DeclarativeNetRequestWithHostAccessPermissionCount
metric.summary: The number of enabled extensions that have a DeclarativeNetRequestWithHostAccess permission. The exponential bucketing is applied for privacy reasons by using ukm::GetExponentialBucketMin(value, 2).

metric.name: DeclarativeWebRequestPermissionCount
metric.summary: The number of enabled extensions that have a DeclarativeWebRequest permission. The exponential bucketing is applied for privacy reasons by using ukm::GetExponentialBucketMin(value, 2).

metric.name: EnabledExtensionCount
metric.summary: The number of enabled extensions that are not themes nor apps nor components. The exponential bucketing is applied for privacy reasons by using ukm::GetExponentialBucketMin(value, 2).

metric.name: EnabledExtensionCount.HaveHostPermissions
metric.summary: The number of enabled extensions that have host permissions for the navigation target URL. The exponential bucketing is applied for privacy reasons by using ukm::GetExponentialBucketMin(value, 2).

metric.name: EnabledExtensionCount.InjectContentScript
metric.summary: The number of enabled extensions that inject content scripts. The exponential bucketing is applied for privacy reasons by using ukm::GetExponentialBucketMin(value, 2).

metric.name: WebRequestAuthProviderPermissionCount
metric.summary: The number of enabled extensions that have a WebRequestAuthProvider permission. The exponential bucketing is applied for privacy reasons by using ukm::GetExponentialBucketMin(value, 2).

metric.name: WebRequestBlockingPermissionCount
metric.summary: The number of enabled extensions that have a WebRequestBlocking permission. The exponential bucketing is applied for privacy reasons by using ukm::GetExponentialBucketMin(value, 2).

metric.name: WebRequestPermissionCount
metric.summary: The number of enabled extensions that have a WebRequest permission. The exponential bucketing is applied for privacy reasons by using ukm::GetExponentialBucketMin(value, 2).

event.name: Extensions.WebRequest.KeepaliveRequestFinished
event.summary: Whether and how keepalive requests are blocked. Recorded when a keepalive request finishes.

metric.name: NumRedirects
metric.summary: The number of redirects the request experienced. This is up to 21, as specified at https://fetch.spec.whatwg.org/#http-redirect-fetch .

metric.name: State
metric.summary: The final state of the request.

event.name: FacilitatedPayments.Pix.Transaction
event.summary: An event that is logged when a FacilitatedPayments transaction is completed.

metric.name: Result
metric.summary: The result of the transaction.

metric.name: TriggerSource
metric.summary: The trigger source for this transaction.

event.name: FacilitatedPayments.PixCodeDetectionResult
event.summary: An event that is logged after a PIX code detection is triggered on the page.

metric.name: Attempts
metric.summary: The number of attempts at PIX code detection on a given page. Each attempt is a full scan of the document DOM tree. Repeated attempts are made if a PIX code was not found on the page, but the page URL is allowlisted. The retry attempts are limited.

metric.name: DetectionTriggeredOnDomContentLoaded
metric.summary: Whether PIX code detection was triggered on DOMContentLoaded instead of DidFinishLoad.

metric.name: LatencyInMillis
metric.summary: The duration (in milliseconds) for how long it took to scan the document for PIX code. It is the time between the browser sending a message to the renderer to trigger PIX code detection and the renderer returning the result of the scan.

metric.name: Result
metric.summary: Represents the outcome of running PIX code detection on the page. It is used to log whether or not PIX detection was run, and if a valid PIX code was found on the page.

event.name: FamilyLinkUser.BlockedContent
event.summary: Logged whenever a page load for a supervised user had some or all of its content blocked. Either MainFrameBlocked is true, or NumBlockedIframes is non-zero.

metric.name: MainFrameBlocked
metric.summary: Whether the main frame for the navigation was blocked (in which case) none of the content of the site is loaded.

metric.name: NumBlockedIframes
metric.summary: The number of Iframes that were blocked by the supervised user filtering logic in this page load. Exponentially bucketed.

event.name: FamilyLinkUser.ReauthenticationInterstitial
event.summary: These metrics are logged when an unauthenticated Family Link user tries to navigate to YouTube in the main frame, prompting a re-authentication interstitial.

metric.name: InterstitialShown
metric.summary: Recorded when the re-authentication interstitial is displayed.

metric.name: ReauthenticationCompleted
metric.summary: Recorded when users successfully re-authenticates and can continue navigation, and vice-versa for failed reauthentication.

metric.name: ReauthenticationStarted
metric.summary: Recorded when users initiate re-authentication from the interstitial.

event.name: FastCheckout.FormStatus
event.summary: Recorded at the end of a Fast Checkout run.

metric.name: Filled
metric.summary: 1 if the form was filled by Fast Checkout, 0 otherwise.

metric.name: FormSignature
metric.summary: An approximately 10-bit hash of the form's actual signature.

metric.name: FormTypes
metric.summary: Type of form. Stored as bitvector. A set i-th bit implies enum FormTypeNameForLogging's i-th type was detected. Multiple types are possible.

metric.name: RunId
metric.summary: Unique run ID for linking to other UKM events.

event.name: FastCheckout.RunOutcome
event.summary: Recorded at the end of a Fast Checkout run.

metric.name: RunId
metric.summary: Unique run ID for linking to other UKM events.

metric.name: RunOutcome
metric.summary: States if the run was successful or why it was not.

event.name: Feedback.HappinessTrackingSurvey
event.summary: Recorded when a user completes a HaTS survey.

metric.name: SurveyAnswerToQuestion1
metric.summary: The answer the user has provided to survey question number 1.

metric.name: SurveyAnswerToQuestion2
metric.summary: The answer the user has provided to survey question number 2.

metric.name: SurveyAnswerToQuestion3
metric.summary: The answer the user has provided to survey question number 3.

metric.name: SurveyAnswerToQuestion4
metric.summary: The answer the user has provided to survey question number 4.

metric.name: SurveyAnswerToQuestion5
metric.summary: The answer the user has provided to survey question number 5.

metric.name: SurveyAnswerToQuestion6
metric.summary: The answer the user has provided to survey question number 6.

metric.name: SurveyAnswerToQuestion7
metric.summary: The answer the user has provided to survey question number 7.

metric.name: SurveyAnswerToQuestion8
metric.summary: The answer the user has provided to survey question number 8.

metric.name: SurveyAnswerToQuestion9
metric.summary: The answer the user has provided to survey question number 9.

metric.name: SurveyCompleted
metric.summary: Boolean that records if a survey has been successfully completed.

metric.name: SurveyId
metric.summary: Survey ID associated with HaTS. This ID should be unique per HaTS survey and should be separate from any existing IDs.

metric.name: SurveyLoaded
metric.summary: Boolean that records if a survey has been successfully loaded.

event.name: FileSystemAPI.WebRequest
event.summary: Logged when a website requests accessing FileSystem API.

metric.name: Persistent
metric.summary: Marks a request for a Persistent FileSystem.

metric.name: Temporary
metric.summary: Marks a request for a Temporary FileSystem.

event.name: FingerprintingProtection
event.summary: Recorded for page loads where fingerprinting protection filtering policy was evaluated.

metric.name: ActivationDecision
metric.summary: Enum that records the policy decision to activate subresource filtering for a page load. 0 = Unknown, 1 = Activated, 2 = Disabled, 3 = Deprecated value, 4 = URL allowlisted, 5 = Activation conditions not met. 'Activated' indicates that subresource filtering was activated. All other reasons indicate that subresource filtering was not activated.

metric.name: AllowlistSource
metric.summary: Enum that records the source of the fingerprinting protection filtering allowlist entry. 0 = Unknown, 1 = UserBypass, 2 = Enterprise. Recorded only when `ActivationDecision` is "URL allowlisted"

metric.name: DryRun
metric.summary: Records `1` if the fingerprinting protection filtering was activated in dry-run mode. In dry-run mode, fingerprinting protection filtering policy is evaluated against resources loaded on the page, but subresource blocking is not actually applied.

event.name: FingerprintingProtectionUsage
event.summary: Recorded to track heuristics for refreshes where Fingerprinting Protection was enabled.

metric.name: RefreshCount
metric.summary: The number of times the user refreshed the current page (without navigating elsewhere).

metric.name: SiteEngagementLevel
metric.summary: The site engagement level of the page where this event was triggered.

event.name: FlocPageLoad
event.summary: Whenever the floc is computed, this event will be recorded at the commit time of the next page load. Caveat: if the browser is closed before the next page load occurs (in a new session), the event won't be recorded.

metric.name: FlocId
metric.summary: The user's floc id. This ignores any general or per-context access permissions, and records the internal int64_t number directly. This metric may still be skipped if the floc was not calculated or has been invalidated.

event.name: Fullscreen.Enter
event.summary: The browser enters the fullscreen mode.

metric.name: LockState
metric.summary: The keyboard and pointer lock states.

event.name: Fullscreen.Exit
event.summary: The browser exits the fullscreen mode.

metric.name: SessionDuration
metric.summary: Duration of the fullscreen session.

event.name: GameControls.ButtonOptionsMenuFunctionTriggered
event.summary: Logged when Game Controls' options menu button functions are triggered.

metric.name: Function
metric.summary: The functions on the button options menu.

event.name: GameControls.EditDeleteMenuFuctionTriggered
event.summary: Logged when Game Controls edit-delete menu functions are triggered.

metric.name: Function
metric.summary: The functions on the edit-delete menu.

event.name: GameControls.EditingListFunctionTriggered
event.summary: Logged when Game Controls editing list functions are triggered.

metric.name: Function
metric.summary: The functions on the editing list.

event.name: GameControls.ToggleWithMappingSource
event.summary: Logged when Game Controls feature or hint is toggled on or off.

metric.name: Function
metric.summary: Toggle the feature or mapping hint.

metric.name: MappingSource
metric.summary: Toggle the feature or mapping hint.

metric.name: ToggleOn
metric.summary: Whether the function is toggled on or off.

event.name: GameDashboard.ControlsFeatureToggleState
event.summary: Logged when Game Controls feature is toggled on or off.

metric.name: ToggleOn
metric.summary: Game Controls feature is toggled on or off.

event.name: GameDashboard.ControlsHintToggleSource
event.summary: Logged when Game Controls mapping hint is toggled on or off.

metric.name: Source
metric.summary: Where the mapping hint is toggled from.

metric.name: ToggleOn
metric.summary: Game Controls mapping hint is toggled on or off.

event.name: GameDashboard.EditControlsWithEmptyState
event.summary: Logged whether Game Controls is empty when entering Game Controls edit mode.

metric.name: Empty
metric.summary: Whether Game Controls is empty.

event.name: GameDashboard.FunctionTriggered
event.summary: Logged when the game dashboard functions are triggered.

metric.name: Function
metric.summary: Game dashboard function.

event.name: GameDashboard.RecordingStartSource
event.summary: Logged where the recording starts from.

metric.name: Source
metric.summary: Where is the recording started from, main menu or toolbar.

event.name: GameDashboard.ScreenshotTakeSource
event.summary: Logged where the screenshot is taken from.

metric.name: Source
metric.summary: Where is the screenshot taken from, main menu or toolbar.

event.name: GameDashboard.ToggleMainMenu
event.summary: Logged when the game dashboard main menu is toggled on or off and how it is toggled.

metric.name: ToggleMethod
metric.summary: How the main menu is toggled.

metric.name: ToggleOn
metric.summary: Whether the main menu is toggled on or off.

event.name: GameDashboard.ToolbarClickToExpandState
event.summary: Logged when the game dashboard toolbar is expanded or collapsed.

metric.name: Expanded
metric.summary: Whether the toolbar is expanded.

event.name: GameDashboard.ToolbarNewLocation
event.summary: Logged when the game dashboard toolbar is snapped to a new location.

metric.name: Location
metric.summary: Toolbar new snap location.

event.name: GameDashboard.ToolbarToggleState
event.summary: Logged when the game dashboard toolbar is toggled on or off.

metric.name: ToggleOn
metric.summary: Whether the toolbar is toggled on or off.

event.name: GameDashboard.WelcomeDialogNotificationToggleState
event.summary: Logged when the game dashboard welcome dialog notification is toggled on or off.

metric.name: ToggleOn
metric.summary: Welcome dialog notification is toggled on or off.

event.name: GamingInputOverlay.ActionRepositionOperationType
event.summary: An event that is logged when key mapping position is changed.

metric.name: OperationType
metric.summary: Type about how the reposition is done.

event.name: GamingInputOverlay.ActionRepositionWindowStateType
event.summary: An event that is logged when key mapping position is changed.

metric.name: WindowStateType
metric.summary: Window state type when the reposition is done.

event.name: GamingInputOverlay.ButtonGroupRepositionOperationType
event.summary: An event that is logged when button group position is changed.

metric.name: OperationType
metric.summary: Type about how the reposition is done.

event.name: GamingInputOverlay.ButtonGroupRepositionWindowStateType
event.summary: An event that is logged when menu entry position is changed.

metric.name: WindowStateType
metric.summary: Window state type when the reposition is done.

event.name: GamingInputOverlay.Customization
event.summary: An event for gaming input overlay that is logged when users enter into edit mode.

metric.name: CustomizationUsed
metric.summary: Whether the customization option was used.

event.name: GamingInputOverlay.Feature
event.summary: An event for gaming input overlay that is logged when the input overlay feature itself changes state.

metric.name: FeatureStateOn
metric.summary: Whether the gaming input overlay feature is in use.

event.name: GamingInputOverlay.MappingHint
event.summary: An event for gaming input overlay that is logged when the mapping hint state is changed.

metric.name: MappingHintStateOn
metric.summary: Whether the mapping hint for gaming input overlay is on.

event.name: GamingInputOverlay.MenuEntryRepositionOperationType
event.summary: An event that is logged when key mapping position is changed.

metric.name: OperationType
metric.summary: Type about how the reposition is done.

event.name: GamingInputOverlay.MenuEntryRepositionWindowStateType
event.summary: An event that is logged when menu entry position is changed.

metric.name: WindowStateType
metric.summary: Window state type when the reposition is done.

event.name: GeneratedNavigation
event.summary: Logged when the user navigated to their home page or their default search engine. Recorded if the first URL or the final URL matches the pattern for Default Search or matches the user's home page URL. Recorded upon commit (even if the page was not foreground).

metric.name: FinalURLIsDefaultSearchEngine
metric.summary: If the navigation committed URL matches the template URL of the user's Default Search Engine.

metric.name: FinalURLIsHomePage
metric.summary: If the navigation committed URL matches the user's home page URL. Not recorded on Android because home page is managed outside of Chrome.

metric.name: FirstURLIsDefaultSearchEngine
metric.summary: If the navigation starting URL matches the template URL of the user's Default Search Engine.

metric.name: FirstURLIsHomePage
metric.summary: If the navigation starting URL (first URL in the redirect chain) matches the user's home page URL. Not recorded on Android because home page is managed outside of Chrome.

event.name: GoogleDocsOfflineExtension
event.summary: Logged when a website requests a shared resource from Google Docs Offline Extension. Note that this is double counted for imports since it is done once in preload and once in actual load.

metric.name: ResourceRequested
metric.summary: Marks a resource request from a website.

event.name: Graphics.Smoothness.EventLatency
event.summary: Tracks the duration of stages in the rendering pipeline when processing a single frame in response to an input event. To meet UKM goals for data volume, a Poisson process with an exponential decay multiplier is used to sample events for recporting. It will sample many reandomly distributed events early during page load and initial interaction, then samples at an exponentially decreasing rate to effectively cap the number of samples. All times are in microseconds.

metric.name: Activation
metric.summary: The duration of the activation stage, in microseconds.

metric.name: BeginImplFrameToSendBeginMainFrame
metric.summary: The time from when the Impl frame is started to when BeginMainFrame is sent, in microseconds.

metric.name: Commit
metric.summary: The duration of the commit stage, in microseconds.

metric.name: EndActivateToSubmitCompositorFrame
metric.summary: The time from when an activation is complete to the next SubmitCompositorFrame, in microseconds.

metric.name: EndCommitToActivation
metric.summary: The time from when a commit is complete to the beginning of the next activation, in microseconds.

metric.name: EventType
metric.summary: Type of the input event.

metric.name: GenerationToRendererCompositor
metric.summary: The time from when the event is created until it arrives in the renderer compositor, in microseconds.

metric.name: PinchInputType
metric.summary: Type of the input device for pinch events.

metric.name: RendererCompositorFinishedToActivation
metric.summary: The time from when the event processing is finished on the renderer compositor until the beginning of the activation, in microseconds. This metric is only reported if the event is handled on the renderer compositor and the first compositor step after that is the beginning of the activation.

metric.name: RendererCompositorFinishedToBeginImplFrame
metric.summary: The time from when the event processing is finished on the renderer compositor until the compositor frame is started, in microseconds. This metric is only reported if the event is handled on the renderer compositor and compositor frame is started after that.

metric.name: RendererCompositorFinishedToCommit
metric.summary: The time from when the event processing is finished on the renderer compositor until the beginning of the commit, in microseconds. This metric is only reported if the event is handled on the renderer compositor and the first compositor step after that is the beginning of the commit.

metric.name: RendererCompositorFinishedToEndActivate
metric.summary: The time from when the event processing is finished on the renderer compositor until the end of the activation, in microseconds. This metric is only reported if the event is handled on the renderer compositor and the first compositor step after that is the end of the activation.

metric.name: RendererCompositorFinishedToEndCommit
metric.summary: The time from when the event processing is finished on the renderer compositor until the end of the commit, in microseconds. This metric is only reported if the event is handled on the renderer compositor and the first compositor step after that is the end of the commit.

metric.name: RendererCompositorFinishedToSendBeginMainFrame
metric.summary: The time from when the event processing is finished on the renderer compositor until BeginMainFrame is sent, in microseconds. This metric is only reported if the event is handled on the renderer compositor and the first compositor step after that is SendBeginMainFrame.

metric.name: RendererCompositorFinishedToSubmitCompositorFrame
metric.summary: The time from when the event processing is finished on the renderer compositor until the compositor frame is submitted, in microseconds. This metric is only reported if the event is handled on the renderer compositor and the first compositor step after that is the submission of the compositor frame.

metric.name: RendererCompositorProcessing
metric.summary: The time spent in the renderer compositor to process the event, in microseconds. This metric won't be reported if the renderer compositor does not have a dedicated thread.

metric.name: RendererCompositorQueueingDelay
metric.summary: The time from when the event arrives in the renderer compositor until the compositor starts processing it, in microseconds. This metric won't be reported if the renderer compositor does not have a dedicated thread.

metric.name: RendererCompositorToMain
metric.summary: The time from when the processing of the event on the renderer compositor is finished until the main thread starts processing the event, in microseconds. If the renderer compositor does not have a dedicated thread, the processing of the event on the renderer compositor is considered finished as soon as the event arrives in the renderer compositor. This metric won't be reported if the event is handled on the renderer compositor.

metric.name: RendererMainFinishedToActivation
metric.summary: The time from when the event processing is finished on the renderer main until the beginning of the activation, in microseconds. This metric is only reported if the event is handled on the renderer main and the first compositor step after that is the beginning of the activation.

metric.name: RendererMainFinishedToBeginImplFrame
metric.summary: The time from when the event processing is finished on the renderer main until the compositor frame is started, in microseconds. This metric is only reported if the event is handled on the renderer main and compositor frame is started after that.

metric.name: RendererMainFinishedToCommit
metric.summary: The time from when the event processing is finished on the renderer main until the beginning of the commit, in microseconds. This metric is only reported if the event is handled on the renderer main and the first compositor step after that is the beginning of the commit.

metric.name: RendererMainFinishedToEndActivate
metric.summary: The time from when the event processing is finished on the renderer main until the end of the activation, in microseconds. This metric is only reported if the event is handled on the renderer main and the first compositor step after that is the end of the activation.

metric.name: RendererMainFinishedToEndCommit
metric.summary: The time from when the event processing is finished on the renderer main until the end of the commit, in microseconds. This metric is only reported if the event is handled on the renderer main and the first compositor step after that is the end of the commit.

metric.name: RendererMainFinishedToSendBeginMainFrame
metric.summary: The time from when the event processing is finished on the renderer main until BeginMainFrame is sent, in microseconds. This metric is only reported if the event is handled on the renderer main and the first compositor step after that is SendBeginMainFrame.

metric.name: RendererMainFinishedToSubmitCompositorFrame
metric.summary: The time from when the event processing is finished on the renderer main until the compositor frame is submitted, in microseconds. This metric is only reported if the event is handled on the renderer main and the first compositor step after that is the submission of the compositor frame.

metric.name: RendererMainProcessing
metric.summary: The time spent in the main thread to process the event, in microseconds. This metric won't be reported if the renderer compositor handles the event.

metric.name: ScrollBegin.Touch
metric.summary: The total time from when the event is generated by the OS to when the frame is presented, in microseconds. Emitted for the first touch event of a scroll.

metric.name: ScrollBegin.Wheel
metric.summary: The total time from when the event is generated by the OS to when the frame is presented, in microseconds. Emitted for the first wheel event of a scroll.

metric.name: ScrollInputType
metric.summary: Type of the input device for scroll events.

metric.name: ScrollUpdate.Touch
metric.summary: The total time from when the event is generated by the OS to when the frame is presented, in microseconds. Emitted for the all touch events of a scroll after the initial one.

metric.name: ScrollUpdate.Wheel
metric.summary: The total time from when the event is generated by the OS to when the frame is presented, in microseconds. Emitted for the all wheel events of a scroll after the initial one.

metric.name: SendBeginMainFrameToCommit
metric.summary: The time from when the BeginMainFrame is sent to the beginning of the commit, in microseconds.

metric.name: SendBeginMainFrameToCommit.Accessibility
metric.summary: The time portion of SendBeginMainFrameToCommit spent on accessibility, in microseconds.

metric.name: SendBeginMainFrameToCommit.Animate
metric.summary: The time portion of SendBeginMainFrameToCommit spent on animations, in microseconds.

metric.name: SendBeginMainFrameToCommit.BeginMainSentToStarted
metric.summary: The time portion of SendBeginMainFrameToCommit spent before starting main thread work, in microseconds.

metric.name: SendBeginMainFrameToCommit.CompositeCommit
metric.summary: The time portion of SendBeginMainFrameToCommit spent on composite commit, in microseconds.

metric.name: SendBeginMainFrameToCommit.CompositingInputs
metric.summary: The time portion of SendBeginMainFrameToCommit spent on updating compositing inputs, in microseconds.

metric.name: SendBeginMainFrameToCommit.HandleInputEvents
metric.summary: The time portion of SendBeginMainFrameToCommit spent on handling input events, in microseconds.

metric.name: SendBeginMainFrameToCommit.LayoutUpdate
metric.summary: The time portion of SendBeginMainFrameToCommit spent on layout update, in microseconds.

metric.name: SendBeginMainFrameToCommit.Paint
metric.summary: The time portion of SendBeginMainFrameToCommit spent on paint, in microseconds.

metric.name: SendBeginMainFrameToCommit.Prepaint
metric.summary: The time portion of SendBeginMainFrameToCommit spent on prepaint, in microseconds.

metric.name: SendBeginMainFrameToCommit.StyleUpdate
metric.summary: The time portion of SendBeginMainFrameToCommit spent on style update, in microseconds.

metric.name: SendBeginMainFrameToCommit.UpdateLayers
metric.summary: The time portion of SendBeginMainFrameToCommit spent on updating layers, in microseconds.

metric.name: SubmitCompositorFrameToPresentationCompositorFrame
metric.summary: The time from when a compositor frame is submitted to the display compositor to when it is presented, in microseconds.

metric.name: SubmitCompositorFrameToPresentationCompositorFrame.BufferAvailableToBufferReady
metric.summary: The time from when the target framebuffer is available to when the GPU has finished drawing to it, in microseconds.

metric.name: SubmitCompositorFrameToPresentationCompositorFrame.BufferReadyToLatch
metric.summary: The time from when the target framebuffer is ready to be displayed to when the buffer is latched on for presentation, in microseconds.

metric.name: SubmitCompositorFrameToPresentationCompositorFrame.LatchToSwapEnd
metric.summary: The time from when the target framebuffer is latched on to when the swap ends, in microseconds.

metric.name: SubmitCompositorFrameToPresentationCompositorFrame.ReceivedCompositorFrameToStartDraw
metric.summary: The time from when a compositor frame is received to when it starts to draw, in microseconds.

metric.name: SubmitCompositorFrameToPresentationCompositorFrame.StartDrawToSwapStart
metric.summary: The time from when a compositor frame is started to draw to when it starts swap, in microseconds.

metric.name: SubmitCompositorFrameToPresentationCompositorFrame.SubmitToReceiveCompositorFrame
metric.summary: The time from when a compositor frame is submitted to when it is received, in microseconds.

metric.name: SubmitCompositorFrameToPresentationCompositorFrame.SwapEndToPresentationCompositorFrame
metric.summary: The time from when a compositor frame ends swap to when it is presented, in microseconds.

metric.name: SubmitCompositorFrameToPresentationCompositorFrame.SwapStartToBufferAvailable
