metric.summary: The time from when the compositor frame starts to swap to when the target framebuffer becomes available, in microseconds.

metric.name: SubmitCompositorFrameToPresentationCompositorFrame.SwapStartToSwapEnd
metric.summary: The time from when a compositor frame starts swap to when it ends swap, in microseconds.

metric.name: TotalLatency
metric.summary: The total time from when the event is created in the browser to when the frame is presented, in microseconds.

event.name: Graphics.Smoothness.Latency
event.summary: Tracks the duration of stages in the rendering pipeline while processing a single frame. All times are in microseconds.

metric.name: Activation
metric.summary: The duration of the activation stage, in microseconds.

metric.name: BeginImplFrameToSendBeginMainFrame
metric.summary: The time from when the Impl frame is started to when BeginMainFrame is sent, in microseconds.

metric.name: CanvasAnimation
metric.summary: True when a canvas animation was active this frame.

metric.name: Commit
metric.summary: The duration of the commit stage, in microseconds.

metric.name: CompositorAnimation
metric.summary: True when a compositor-driven animation was active this frame.

metric.name: EndActivateToSubmitCompositorFrame
metric.summary: The time from when an activation is complete to the next SubmitCompositorFrame, in microseconds.

metric.name: EndCommitToActivation
metric.summary: The time from when a commit is complete to the beginning of the next activation, in microseconds.

metric.name: JSAnimation
metric.summary: True when a inline style animation was active this frame.

metric.name: MainThreadAnimation
metric.summary: True when a main-thread-driven animation was active this frame.

metric.name: MissedFrame
metric.summary: True when the main frame missed its deadline for this frame.

metric.name: PinchZoom
metric.summary: True when a pinch-to-zoom interaction was active this frame.

metric.name: RAF
metric.summary: True when a rAF-driven animation was active this frame.

metric.name: ScrollbarScroll
metric.summary: True when a scrollbar driven interaction was active this frame.

metric.name: SendBeginMainFrameToCommit
metric.summary: The time from when the BeginMainFrame is sent to the beginning of the commit, in microseconds.

metric.name: SendBeginMainFrameToCommit.Accessibility
metric.summary: The time portion of SendBeginMainFrameToCommit spent on accessibility, in microseconds.

metric.name: SendBeginMainFrameToCommit.Animate
metric.summary: The time portion of SendBeginMainFrameToCommit spent on animations, in microseconds.

metric.name: SendBeginMainFrameToCommit.BeginMainSentToStarted
metric.summary: The time portion of SendBeginMainFrameToCommit spent before starting main thread work, in microseconds.

metric.name: SendBeginMainFrameToCommit.CompositeCommit
metric.summary: The time portion of SendBeginMainFrameToCommit spent on composite commit, in microseconds.

metric.name: SendBeginMainFrameToCommit.CompositingInputs
metric.summary: The time portion of SendBeginMainFrameToCommit spent on updating compositing inputs, in microseconds.

metric.name: SendBeginMainFrameToCommit.HandleInputEvents
metric.summary: The time portion of SendBeginMainFrameToCommit spent on handling input events, in microseconds.

metric.name: SendBeginMainFrameToCommit.LayoutUpdate
metric.summary: The time portion of SendBeginMainFrameToCommit spent on layout update, in microseconds.

metric.name: SendBeginMainFrameToCommit.Paint
metric.summary: The time portion of SendBeginMainFrameToCommit spent on paint, in microseconds.

metric.name: SendBeginMainFrameToCommit.Prepaint
metric.summary: The time portion of SendBeginMainFrameToCommit spent on prepaint, in microseconds.

metric.name: SendBeginMainFrameToCommit.StyleUpdate
metric.summary: The time portion of SendBeginMainFrameToCommit spent on style update, in microseconds.

metric.name: SendBeginMainFrameToCommit.UpdateLayers
metric.summary: The time portion of SendBeginMainFrameToCommit spent on updating layers, in microseconds.

metric.name: SubmitCompositorFrameToPresentationCompositorFrame
metric.summary: The time from when the a compositor frame is submitted to the display compositor to when it is presented, in microseconds.

metric.name: SubmitCompositorFrameToPresentationCompositorFrame.BufferAvailableToBufferReady
metric.summary: The time from when the target framebuffer is available to when the GPU has finished drawing to it, in microseconds.

metric.name: SubmitCompositorFrameToPresentationCompositorFrame.BufferReadyToLatch
metric.summary: The time from when the target framebuffer is ready to be displayed to when the buffer is latched on for presentation, in microseconds.

metric.name: SubmitCompositorFrameToPresentationCompositorFrame.LatchToSwapEnd
metric.summary: The time from when the target framebuffer is latched on to when the swap ends, in microseconds.

metric.name: SubmitCompositorFrameToPresentationCompositorFrame.ReceivedCompositorFrameToStartDraw
metric.summary: The time from when a compositor frame is received to when it starts to draw, in microseconds.

metric.name: SubmitCompositorFrameToPresentationCompositorFrame.StartDrawToSwapStart
metric.summary: The time from when a compositor frame is started to draw to when it starts swap, in microseconds.

metric.name: SubmitCompositorFrameToPresentationCompositorFrame.SubmitToReceiveCompositorFrame
metric.summary: The time from when a compositor frame is submitted to when it is received, in microseconds.

metric.name: SubmitCompositorFrameToPresentationCompositorFrame.SwapEndToPresentationCompositorFrame
metric.summary: The time from when the a compositor frame ends swap to when it is presented, in microseconds.

metric.name: SubmitCompositorFrameToPresentationCompositorFrame.SwapStartToBufferAvailable
metric.summary: The time from when the compositor frame starts to swap to when the target framebuffer becomes available, in microseconds.

metric.name: SubmitCompositorFrameToPresentationCompositorFrame.SwapStartToSwapEnd
metric.summary: The time from when a compositor frame starts swap to when it ends swap, in microseconds.

metric.name: TotalLatency
metric.summary: The total time starting from BeginImplFrame to when CompositorFramePresentation is done with updates from the MainThread (i.e. the time it takes for BeginImplFrame, BeginMainFrame, Commit, Activate, SubmitCompositorFrame and PresentCompositorFrame), in microseconds.

metric.name: TouchScroll
metric.summary: True when a touchscreen driven interaction was active this frame.

metric.name: Unknown
metric.summary: The time within a stage not attributed to any system, in microseconds.

metric.name: Video
metric.summary: True when video playback was active this frame.

metric.name: WheelScroll
metric.summary: True when a mouse driven scroll was active this frame.

event.name: Graphics.Smoothness.NormalizedPercentDroppedFrames
event.summary:
Measures various normalizations for the smoothness metric. The metric is measured by counting dropped frames, and various normalization strategies are used.
The metric is reported once per page-load, and when the page closes (e.g. the user closes the tab, or navigates away to a different page).

metric.name: AboveThreshold
metric.summary: The number of fixed-duration sliding windows (as a percentage) where the number of dropped frames are above a threshold. The specific threshold is yet to be determined.

metric.name: Average
metric.summary: This is trivially computable by counting the total number of dropped frames and the total number of vsyncs the page has been visible for, exposed as a percentage.

metric.name: CompositorFocusedMedian
metric.summary: CompositorFocused metrics report the throughput of frames that include compositor thread updates. The median dropped frames within a fixed-duration sliding window (as a percentage). So out of all PerecentDroppedFrames of sliding windows the median will be reported here.

metric.name: CompositorFocusedPercentile95
metric.summary: CompositorFocused metrics report the throughput of frames that include compositor thread updates. The 95-th percentile dropped frames within a fixed-duration sliding window (as a percentage). So out of all PerecentDroppedFrames of sliding windows the 95th percentile will be reported here.

metric.name: CompositorFocusedVariance
metric.summary: CompositorFocused metrics report the throughput of frames that include compositor thread updates. The variance of percent dropped frames within a fixed-duration sliding window. So out of all PerecentDroppedFrames of sliding windows the variance will be reported here.

metric.name: MainFocusedMedian
metric.summary: MainFocused metrics report the throughput of frames that include main thread updates. The median dropped frames within a fixed-duration sliding window (as a percentage). So out of all PerecentDroppedFrames of sliding windows the median will be reported here.

metric.name: MainFocusedPercentile95
metric.summary: MainFocused metrics report the throughput of frames that include main thread updates. The 95-th percentile dropped frames within a fixed-duration sliding window (as a percentage). So out of all PerecentDroppedFrames of sliding windows the 95th percentile will be reported here.

metric.name: MainFocusedVariance
metric.summary: MainFocused metrics report the throughput of frames that include main thread updates. The variance of percent dropped frames within a fixed-duration sliding window. So out of all PerecentDroppedFrames of sliding windows the variance will be reported here.

metric.name: Median
metric.summary: The median dropped frames within a fixed-duration sliding window (as a percentage). So out of all PerecentDroppedFrames of sliding windows the median will be reported here.

metric.name: Percentile95
metric.summary: The 95-th percentile dropped frames within a fixed-duration sliding window (as a percentage). So out of all PerecentDroppedFrames of sliding windows the 95th percentile will be reported here.

metric.name: ScrollFocusedMedian
metric.summary: ScrollFocused metrics reports the throughput with highest priority on scroll interactions. The median dropped frames within a fixed-duration sliding window (as a percentage) with highest priority given to scroll throughput. So out of all PerecentDroppedFrames of sliding windows the median will be reported here.

metric.name: ScrollFocusedPercentile95
metric.summary: ScrollFocused metrics reports the throughput with highest priority on scroll interactions. The 95-th percentile dropped frames within a fixed-duration sliding window (as a percentage). So out of all PerecentDroppedFrames of sliding windows the 95th percentile will be reported here.

metric.name: ScrollFocusedVariance
metric.summary: ScrollFocused metrics reports the throughput with highest priority on scroll interactions. The variance of percent dropped frames within a fixed-duration sliding window. So out of all PerecentDroppedFrames of sliding windows the variance will be reported here.

metric.name: SmoothnessBad
metric.summary: The percentage of sliding windows with bad smoothness, or a PercentDroppedFrames of 12% to 25%.

metric.name: SmoothnessGood
metric.summary: The percentage of sliding windows with good smoothness, or a PercentDroppedFrames of 3% to 6%.

metric.name: SmoothnessOkay
metric.summary: The percentage of sliding windows with okay smoothness, or a PercentDroppedFrames of 6% to 12%.

metric.name: SmoothnessVeryBad25to50
metric.summary: The percentage of sliding windows with a subset of very bad smoothness, or a PercentDroppedFrames of 25% to 50%.

metric.name: SmoothnessVeryBad50to75
metric.summary: The percentage of sliding windows with a subset of very bad smoothness, or a PercentDroppedFrames of 50% to 75%.

metric.name: SmoothnessVeryBad75to100
metric.summary: The percentage of sliding windows with a subset of very bad smoothness, or a PercentDroppedFrames of 75% to 100%.

metric.name: SmoothnessVeryGood
metric.summary: The percentage of sliding windows with very good smoothness, or a PercentDroppedFrames of 0% to 3%.

metric.name: Variance
metric.summary: The variance of percent dropped frames within a fixed-duration sliding window. So out of all PerecentDroppedFrames of sliding windows the variance will be reported here.

metric.name: WorstCase
metric.summary: The maximum number of dropped frames (as a percentage) within a fixed-duration sliding window. So out of all PerecentDroppedFrames of sliding windows the max percentile will be reported here.

metric.name: WorstCaseAfter1Sec
metric.summary: The same as WorstCase metric, but only the sliding windows which starts 1 second after first contentful paint will be taken into account.

metric.name: WorstCaseAfter2Sec
metric.summary: The same as WorstCase metric, but only the sliding windows which starts 2 second after first contentful paint will be taken into account.

metric.name: WorstCaseAfter5Sec
metric.summary: The same as WorstCase metric, but only the sliding windows which starts 5 second after first contentful paint will be taken into account.

event.name: HistoryClusters
event.summary: Metrics that capture the iteractions with HistoryClusters, or Journeys, This will be recorded once per visit to the HistoryClusters UI and logged when the UI is closed or navigated away from.

metric.name: FinalState
metric.summary: The final state, or outcome, of an interaction on the HistoryClusters UI.

metric.name: InitialState
metric.summary: The initial state that describes how an interaction with the HistoryClusters UI was started.

metric.name: NumQueries
metric.summary: The number of times the user performed a query on the HistoryClusters UI during this interaction. This can include partial queries that occur due to the UI's query timing.

metric.name: NumTogglesToBasicHistory
metric.summary: The number of times the user toggled to the basic history page during this interaction with the HistoryClusters UI.

event.name: HistoryManipulationIntervention
event.summary: Logged when an entry in the back-forward list is marked to be skipped on subsequent back/forward button clicks as part of the history manipulation intervention. This is logged when the entry is navigated away from.

event.name: HistoryNavigation
event.summary: Metrics recorded each time we commit a history navigation, which are needed to estimate benefits of back-forward cache. Except as otherwise noted, the metrics are recorded for history navigations, but not for same-document navigations.

metric.name: BackForwardCache.BlocklistedFeatures
metric.summary: When navigating back to a page in the session history, this records what blocklisted features were used when the page was loaded and prevented this page from being stored in the back-forward cache. Multiple blocklisted features can be recorded for the same page as a binary mask. This entry records the features numbered from 0 to 63.

metric.name: BackForwardCache.BlocklistedFeatures2
metric.summary: When navigating back to a page in the session history, this records what blocklisted features were used when the page was loaded and prevented this page from being stored in the back-forward cache. Multiple blocklisted features can be recorded for the same page as a binary mask. This entry records the features numbered from 64 to 127.

metric.name: BackForwardCache.BrowsingInstanceNotSwappedReason
metric.summary: When navigating back to a page in the session history and the BackForwardCache wasn't used because of the related browsing instances, this records the reason why the browsing instance wasn't swapped.

metric.name: BackForwardCache.DisabledForRenderFrameHostReasonCount
metric.summary: The number of the reasons passed to BackForwardCache::DisableForRenderFrameHost calls.

metric.name: BackForwardCache.IsAmpPage
metric.summary: Boolean whether the now-restored page uses AMP (see https://amp.dev). If the page wasn't restored from the back-forward cache, this metric will not be populated. This metric will never be true when BackForwardCache.IsServedFromBackForwardCache is false.

metric.name: BackForwardCache.IsServedFromBackForwardCache
metric.summary: Boolean whether the page was restored from the back-forward cache or not.

metric.name: BackForwardCache.NotRestoredReasons
metric.summary: Bitmask of the reasons why the page was not restored from the back-forward cache. See contents::BackForwardCacheMetrics::NotRestoredReason for the meaning of the individual bits. This entry records the features numbered from 0 to 63.

metric.name: BackForwardCache.NotRestoredReasons2
metric.summary: Bitmask of the reasons why the page was not restored from the back-forward cache. See contents::BackForwardCacheMetrics::NotRestoredReason for the meaning of the individual bits. This entry records the features numbered from 64 to 127.

metric.name: CrossOriginSubframesFeatures
metric.summary: Bitmask of features numbered from 0 to 63 used by the cross origin subframes of the previous page load. See blink::SchedulingPolicy::Feature for the meaning for the individual features.

metric.name: CrossOriginSubframesFeatures2
metric.summary: Bitmask of features numbered from 64 to 127 used by the cross origin subframes of the previous page load. See blink::SchedulingPolicy::Feature for the meaning for the individual features.

metric.name: CumulativeShiftScoreAfterBackForwardCacheRestore
metric.summary:
Deprecated as of 04/23. It is still reported, but most users should use PageLoad.LayoutInstability.MaxCumulativeShiftScore.AfterBackForwardCacheRestore.SessionWindow.Gap1000ms.Max5000ms.
Measures the cumulative layout shift (bit.ly/3fQz29y) that has occurred during the session, after the page is restored from the back-forward cache. This metric's integral value is 100x the fractional cumulative layout shift score described in the explainer.

metric.name: FirstInputDelayAfterBackForwardCacheRestore
metric.summary: Measures First Input Delay, the duration between the hardware timestamp and the start of event processing on the main thread for the first meaningful input per navigation, after the page is restored from the back-forward cache.

metric.name: FirstRequestAnimationFrameAfterBackForwardCacheRestore
metric.summary: Measures the time duration between the page restore from back-forward cache and the first requestAnimationFrame time in milliseconds.

metric.name: ForegroundDurationAfterBackForwardCacheRestore
metric.summary: For page loads that start in the foreground, measures the duration of time in milliseconds until one of the following events occurs: the load of the main resource fails, the page load is stopped, the tab hosting the page is closed, the render process hosting the page goes away, a new navigation which later commits is initiated in the same tab, or the tab hosting the page is backgrounded. This metric has a very long tail, for pages that spend a long period of time in the foreground. As such, use of the mean or tail values are not recommended.

metric.name: LastCommittedCrossDocumentNavigationSourceIdForTheSameDocument
metric.summary: For history navigations and reloads, the source id of the previous navigation which loaded the page we're trying to navigate back to.

metric.name: MainFrameFeatures
metric.summary: Bitmask of features numbered from 0 to 63 used by the main frames of the previous page load. See blink::SchedulingPolicy::Feature for the meaning for the individual features.

metric.name: MainFrameFeatures2
metric.summary: Bitmask of features numbered from 64 to 127 used by the main frames of the previous page load. See blink::SchedulingPolicy::Feature for the meaning for the individual features.

metric.name: MaxCumulativeShiftScoreAfterBackForwardCacheRestore.SessionWindow.Gap1000ms.Max5000ms
metric.summary: Measures the maximum cumulative layout shift (bit.ly/cls-explainer) that has occurred in a session window, after the page is restored from the back-forward. The gap between two consecutive shifts in a window is not bigger than 1000ms and the maximum window size is 5000ms. This metric's integral value is 100x the fractional cumulative layout shift score described in the explainer.

metric.name: NavigatedToTheMostRecentEntryForDocument
metric.summary:
Boolean for whether we navigated to the same navigation entry as the one which was last visible.
It can be false when subframes or same-document navigations are present. For example, after navigating from http://foo to http://foo#bar and then http://bar and then going back by 2 entries will mean we went to http://foo while the last committed entry from this document was http://foo#bar.
It's expected to be rare, but might be problematic for back-forward cache.

metric.name: NavigationToFirstPaintAfterBackForwardCacheRestore
metric.summary: Measures the time in milliseconds from navigation timing's navigation start to the time the first paint is performed, after the page is restored from the back-forward cache.

metric.name: NumInteractionsAfterBackForwardCacheRestore
PageEndReasonAfterBackForwardCacheRestore
metric.summary: The reason (as a |page_load_metrics::PageEndReason|) why the user navigated away from the page after it was restored from the back-forward cache. This includes navigations after which the page is stored in the back-forward cache again, as well as other page-ending events.

metric.name: SameOriginSubframesFeatures
metric.summary: Bitmask of features numbered from 0 to 63 used by the same origin subframes of the previous page load. See blink::SchedulingPolicy::Feature for the meaning for the individual features.

metric.name: SameOriginSubframesFeatures2
metric.summary: Bitmask of features numbered from 64 to 127 used by the same origin subframes of the previous page load. See blink::SchedulingPolicy::Feature for the meaning for the individual features.

metric.name: SecondRequestAnimationFrameAfterBackForwardCacheRestore
metric.summary: Measures the time duration between the page restore from back-forward cache and the second requestAnimationFrame time in milliseconds.

metric.name: ThirdRequestAnimationFrameAfterBackForwardCacheRestore
metric.summary: Measures the time duration between the page restore from back-forward cache and the third requestAnimationFrame time in milliseconds.

metric.name: TimeSinceNavigatedAwayFromDocument
metric.summary:
Time in milliseconds from the moment the current navigation stopped being active to the start of the current navigation.
This is clamped to hours for values greater than 3 hours, to minutes for values greater than 3 minutes, to seconds for values greater than 5 seconds.

metric.name: UserInteractionLatencyAfterBackForwardCacheRestore.HighPercentile2.MaxEventDuration
metric.summary: An approximation of a high percentile of user interaction latency of a page after it is restored from the back-forward cache, in ms. For this metric, we measure the latency of user interactions by Responsiveness.UserInteraction.MaxEventDuration.

metric.name: WorstUserInteractionLatencyAfterBackForwardCacheRestore.MaxEventDuration2
metric.summary: The maximum value of user interaction latency of a page after it is restored from the back-forward, in ms. For this metric, we measure the latency of user interactions by Responsiveness.UserInteraction.MaxEventDuration.

event.name: Identifiability
event.summary:
Identifiability metrics are used to measure the diversity of web clients as observed by individual web sites.
Browser or device fingerprinting uses differences in behaviors of web APIs as identifiers. The values collected under this event indicate the level of identifiability of these differences.
Metrics recorded under this event are keyed based on parameters determined at runtime. Hence the metrics described in this file are not exhaustive.
See //third_party/blink/public/common/privacy_budget/identifiability/identifiability_metric_builder.h for details on how the metrics hash is determined.

metric.name: GeneratorVersion.926
metric.summary:
An integer identifying the "generator" version for this record. Some identifiability study samples are derived from multiple factors which are run though a digest function to derive the int64_t value. If the set of factors change or the method of combining them changes, then the resulting statistics cannot be aggregated with those from a prior version.
The name suffix (.926) was chosen so that the resulting metric hash as determined by base::HashMetricName() has all 8 LSBs set to 0. Such a metric hash does not collide with any other metric hash generated by blink::IdentifiabilityMetricBuilder().

metric.name: StudyGeneration.626
metric.summary:
An integer identifying the study "generation." In case the study parameters need to be tweaked to the point where results cannot be aggregated across study parameter sets, this value can be changed to partition the results.
The name suffix (.626) was chosen so that the resulting metric hash as determined by base::HashMetricName() has all 8 LSBs set to 0. Such a metric hash does not collide with any other metric hash generated by blink::IdentifiabilityMetricBuilder().

event.name: InputMethod.Assistive.AutocorrectV2
event.summary: Metrics recording a summary of user interactions with Autocorrect feature. The event is recorded whenever an autocorrect suggestion is shown or a user interacted with a suggestion to accept or reject it.

metric.name: CompatibilitySummary.PK
metric.summary: A summary of interactions with Physical Keyboard Autocorrect suggestions for the purpose of identifying App Compatibility issues.

metric.name: CompatibilitySummary.VK
metric.summary: A summary of interactions with Virtual Keyboard Autocorrect suggestions for the purpose of identifying App Compatibility issues.

event.name: InputMethod.Assistive.Match
event.summary: Recorded when an assistive action could be triggered according to the surrounding text.

metric.name: Type
metric.summary: The type of the assistive suggestion which is triggered.

event.name: InputMethod.LongpressDiacritics
event.summary: Recorded when a user uses the long press diacritics feature to insert diacritics via long press with a physical keyboard.

metric.name: Actions
metric.summary: Action taken by the user to interact with this feature.

event.name: InputMethod.Manta.Orca
event.summary: Metrics recording a summary of critical user interactions with the Editor feature. The event is recorded whenever a user triggers a critical event in the Editor funnel (specifically; the UI is shown, a request is triggered, or a user inserts some suggested text).

metric.name: EditorCriticalStates
metric.summary: A summary of interactions with the Editor feature for the purpose of identifying common contexts where the feature is used.

event.name: InputMethod.NonCompliantApi
event.summary: Metrics indicating what Input Method Editor (IME) operations are behaving incorrectly on an input field. This event is recorded whenever an IME operation is executed on the input field but the result does not match the IME's expectations (e.g. IME wants to insert 'a' but the input field inserted 'b' instead).

metric.name: NonCompliantOperation
metric.summary: The operation that behaved incorrectly.

event.name: InstalledRelatedApps
event.summary: Recorded before resolving a call to navigator.getInstalledRelatedApps().

metric.name: Called
metric.summary: Always true.

event.name: IOS.FindInPageSearchMatches
event.summary: Logged when the FindInPage returns a user search request result. Starting from M111, this is only logged at the end of the Find session i.e. when the Find UI is dismissed.

metric.name: HasMatches
metric.summary: True if there were matches.

event.name: IOS.FullscreenActions
event.summary: Logged when a user exited the fullscreen mode when browsing a web page in iOS.

metric.name: HasExitedManually
metric.summary: True if the user tapped on the top toolbar to exit and false if they exited it in any other way.

event.name: IOS.IsDefaultBrowser
event.summary: As of iOS14, users will be able to set a default browser other than Safari. When Chrome is the default browser, it will open all URL links, which is likely to change certain stability metrics. Thus, it will be good to filter those metrics by default browser status. This metrics records whether the user was deemed to have set Chrome as the device's default browser. This metric will be logged once per metrics log upload, and if the metric changes mid-report, this just records the state at the very end of the report. Note: this is tied to a source_id that is not going to be emitted.

metric.name: IsDefaultBrowser
metric.summary: True if Chrome is set as default browser.

event.name: IOS.PageAddedToReadingList
event.summary: Logged when the user adds a page to the Reading List.

metric.name: AddedFromMessages
metric.summary: True if the page was added by the user tapping on the Messages prompt to save to Reading List.

event.name: IOS.PageReadability
event.summary: Logged when the Distilibility score of the current page is returned and the Reading List Message is shown. Muliplied by 10X to get tenth digit granularity. 0.1 granularity will be recorded for scores between 0.5 and 1.5. Otherwise, the granularity will be 0.5.

metric.name: DidAccept
metric.summary: True if the user tapped on the Messages prompt to save the current page to Reading List.

metric.name: DistilibilityLongScore
metric.summary: Distilibility score value indicating the "long readability" of the page.

metric.name: DistilibilityScore
metric.summary: Distilibility score value indicating the "readability" of the page.

event.name: IOS.PageZoomChanged
event.summary: Logged when the user changes their zoom level on iOS.

metric.name: ContentSizeCategory
metric.summary: The content size category the user is currently using.

metric.name: OverallZoomLevel
metric.summary: The overall zoom level. This is based on a combination of the user zoom level and a multiplier from the user's content size category.

metric.name: UserZoomLevel
metric.summary: The current zoom multiplier (percentage-based, with 100 -> 100%) the user has chosen.

event.name: IOS.ParcelTrackingNumberDetected
event.summary: Logged when the current page has a valid parcel tracking number in the HTML of the initial page load.

metric.name: ParcelCarrier
metric.summary: The parcel carrier for the parcel number detected. As a privacy measure, 80% of the time when the event occurs, a random enum value is assigned.

event.name: IOS.RendererGone
event.summary: Logged when WKWebView process is reported gone. Metrics will give information of the context in which some website present sad tabs. It will help debug some renderer issues either in Chrome or in WebKit.

metric.name: AliveRecentlyRendererCount
metric.summary: The number of WKWebView alive in the last 2 seconds.

metric.name: AliveRendererCount
metric.summary: The number of WKWebView alive at the moment this renderer is gone.

metric.name: InForeground
metric.summary: Whether the app/tab was in foregound or background. 0: tab in foreground, app in foreground, 1: tab in background, app in foreground, 2: tab in foreground, app in background, 3: tab in background, app in background.

metric.name: SawMemoryWarning
metric.summary: Whether a memory warning was received less than 5 seconds before the renderer process is gone.

event.name: JavascriptFrameworkPageLoad
event.summary: Metrics stating whether the main frame of a page uses certain JavaScript frameworks. This event is recorded when the page is going to be unloaded or when the app enters the background on Android. The framework detection is done when the document is loaded (similar to when load event is fired). If a framework is used after this then it would not be detected. Note that a page can use multiple frameworks at the same time, hence why this reports each framework individually instead of a single enum.

metric.name: AngularPageLoad
metric.summary: True if the page loaded in the main frame uses the Angular JavaScript framework.

metric.name: GatsbyPageLoad
metric.summary: True if the page loaded in the main frame uses the Gatsby JavaScript framework.

metric.name: NextJSPageLoad
metric.summary: True if the page loaded in the main frame uses the Next.js JavaScript framework.

metric.name: NuxtJSPageLoad
metric.summary: True if the page loaded in the main frame uses the Nuxt.js JavaScript framework.

metric.name: PreactPageLoad
metric.summary: True if the page loaded in the main frame uses the Preact JavaScript framework.

metric.name: ReactPageLoad
metric.summary: True if the page loaded in the main frame uses the React JavaScript framework.

metric.name: SapperPageLoad
metric.summary: True if the page loaded in the main frame uses the Sapper JavaScript framework.

metric.name: SveltePageLoad
metric.summary: True if the page loaded in the main frame uses the Svelte JavaScript framework.

metric.name: VuePageLoad
metric.summary: True if the page loaded in the main frame uses the Vue JavaScript framework.

metric.name: VuePressPageLoad
metric.summary: True if the page loaded in the main frame uses the VuePress JavaScript framework.

event.name: Lens.Overlay.Overlay.UserAction
event.summary: Recorded each time the user interactions with the Lens Overlay, enumerated by those actions.

metric.name: UserAction
metric.summary: The user interaction with the Lens Overlay.

event.name: Lens.Overlay.SessionEnd
event.summary: Recorded once when the Lens Overlay feature is dismissed. Measures the invocation source of the session, the duration, and whether a search performed in the session.

metric.name: GeneratedTabCount
metric.summary: The number of tabs opened by the user during a Lens Overlay session. On iOS the Lens Overlay opens all non-lens URLs in new tabs. New unimodal searches are also opened in a new tab. Additionally the user can open tabs in the background through the web context menu.

metric.name: InvocationResultedInSearch
metric.summary: Indicates whether or not a search was performed at any point since the overlay was invoked.

metric.name: InvocationSource
metric.summary: The UI entry point used to invoke the Lens Overlay feature.

metric.name: SessionDuration
metric.summary: Measures the elapsed time between when the overlay was invoked and when it was dismissed.

metric.name: SessionForegroundDuration
metric.summary: Measures the total time the Lens Overlay is in the foreground, between invocation and dismissal. Sum of all times that the overlay tab is returned to the foreground.

event.name: Lens.Overlay.TimeToFirstInteraction
event.summary: Recorded once when the user first interacts with the overlay. Measures the elapsed time (ms) between when the overlay was invoked and when the user first interacts with the overlay. Nothing is logged when the user enters through the image context menu.

metric.name: AllEntryPoints
metric.summary: Time to first interaction for all entry points.

metric.name: AppMenu
metric.summary: Time to first interaction when overlay invoked through the app menu.

metric.name: ContentAreaContextMenuPage
metric.summary: Time to first interaction when overlay invoked through the page content area context menu.

metric.name: FindInPage
metric.summary: Time to first interaction when overlay invoked through the find in page button.

metric.name: FirstInteractionType
metric.summary: The first interaction event type.

metric.name: Omnibox
metric.summary: Time to first interaction when overlay invoked through the omnibox.

metric.name: Toolbar
metric.summary: Time to first interaction when overlay invoked through the toolbar.

event.name: LoadCountsPerTopLevelDocument
event.summary:
Counts loads of each type that occurred on a top-level document. Recorded when a top-level document is unloaded. This can be used to diagnose changes to the "Total pageloads" shown on stability dashboard.
Exponential bucketing is applied to all counts. The bucketing function preserves exact count up until 20.

metric.name: NumMainFrameSameDocumentLoads_Hidden
metric.summary: Number of main frame same-document loads that occurred while the page was hidden.

metric.name: NumMainFrameSameDocumentLoads_Visible
metric.summary: Number of main frame same-document loads that occurred while the page was visible.

metric.name: NumSubFrameDifferentDocumentLoads_Hidden
metric.summary: Number of sub frame different-document loads that occurred while the page was hidden.

metric.name: NumSubFrameDifferentDocumentLoads_Visible
metric.summary: Number of sub frame different-document loads that occurred while the page was visible.

metric.name: NumSubFrameSameDocumentLoads_Hidden
metric.summary: Number of sub frame same-document loads that occurred while the page was hidden.

metric.name: NumSubFrameSameDocumentLoads_Visible
metric.summary: Number of sub frame same-document loads that occurred while the page was visible.

event.name: LoadingPredictor
event.summary: Metrics that reflect the accuracy of the predictions used by the Loading Predictor. This event will be recorded once per page load on DocumentOnLoadCompletedInPrimaryMainFrame().

metric.name: CorrectSubresourceOriginPreconnectsInitiated
metric.summary: The number of subresource origin preconnects that were initiated by the Loading Predictor for the navigation and were actually used by the page load, excluding the main frame URL's origin. If more than 100 subresource origins were preconnected to and were actually used by the page load, this will be capped at 100.

metric.name: CorrectSubresourcePrefetchesInitiated
metric.summary: The number of subresource prefetches that were initiated by the Loading Predictor for the navigation and were actually used by the page load. If more than 100 subresources were prefetched and were actually used by the page load, this will be capped at 100.

metric.name: LocalPredictionCorrectlyPredictedOrigins
metric.summary: The number of subresource origins that were correctly predicted by the local prediction database. This will be recorded if there is a prediction available in the local prediction database and does not necessarily mean that the prediction was used. If more than 100 subresource origins were correctly predicted, this will be capped at 100.

metric.name: LocalPredictionOrigins
metric.summary: The number of subresource origins that were predicted locally. This will be recorded if there is a prediction available in the local prediction database and does not necessarily mean that the prediction was used. If more than 100 subresource origins were predicted, this will be capped at 100.

metric.name: NavigationStartToFirstSubresourcePrefetchInitiated
metric.summary: The time (in milliseconds) that elapsed between navigation start and the first subresource prefetch that was initiated based on these predictions.

metric.name: NavigationStartToNavigationCommit
metric.summary: The time (in milliseconds) that elapsed between navigation start and the commit finished.

metric.name: NavigationStartToOptimizationGuidePredictionArrived
metric.summary: The time (in milliseconds) that elapsed between navigation start and the optimization guide prediction arriving for the navigation.

metric.name: OptimizationGuidePredictionCorrectlyPredictedLowPriorityOrigins
metric.summary: The number of predicted subresource origins that were only used by the page for low-priority subresource loads before it unloaded. This will be recorded if the Optimization Guide was consulted for the page load and the Optimization Guide had a prediction for it. If more than 100 subresource origins were correctly predicted, this will be capped at 100.

metric.name: OptimizationGuidePredictionCorrectlyPredictedLowPrioritySubresources
metric.summary: The number of predicted subresources that were only used by the page for low-priority subresource loads before it unloaded. This will be recorded if the Optimization Guide was consulted for the page load and the Optimization Guide had a prediction for it. If more than 100 subresources were predicted, this will be capped at 100.

metric.name: OptimizationGuidePredictionCorrectlyPredictedLowPrioritySubresources.CrossOrigin
metric.summary: The number of predicted cross-origin subresources that were only used by the page for low-priority subresource loads before it unloaded. This will be recorded if the Optimization Guide was consulted for the page load and the Optimization Guide had a prediction for it. If more than 100 subresources were predicted, this will be capped at 100.

metric.name: OptimizationGuidePredictionCorrectlyPredictedOrigins
metric.summary: The number of subresource origins that were correctly predicted by the Optimization Guide. This only counts origins of high-priority subresources that are used before the page finishes loading. This will be recorded if the Optimization Guide was consulted for the page load and the Optimization Guide had a prediction for it. If more than 100 subresource origins were correctly predicted, this will be capped at 100.

metric.name: OptimizationGuidePredictionCorrectlyPredictedSubresources
metric.summary: The number of subresources that were correctly predicted by the Optimization Guide. This only counts high-priority subresources used before the page finishes loading. This will be recorded if the Optimization Guide was consulted for the page load and the Optimization Guide had a prediction for it. If more than 100 subresources were predicted, this will be capped at 100.

metric.name: OptimizationGuidePredictionCorrectlyPredictedSubresources.CrossOrigin
metric.summary: The number of cross-origin subresources that were correctly predicted by the Optimization Guide. This only counts high-priority subresources used before the page finishes loading. This will be recorded if the Optimization Guide was consulted for the page load and the Optimization Guide had a prediction for it. If more than 100 subresources were predicted, this will be capped at 100.

metric.name: OptimizationGuidePredictionDecision
metric.summary: The decision coming from the Optimization Guide for whether there was a prediction available for the Page Load. This will be recorded if the Optimization Guide was consulted for the page load.

metric.name: OptimizationGuidePredictionOrigins
metric.summary: The number of subresource origins that were predicted by the Optimization Guide. This will be recorded if the Optimization Guide was consulted for the page load and the Optimization Guide had a prediction for it. If more than 100 subresource origins were predicted, this will be capped at 100.

metric.name: OptimizationGuidePredictionSubresources
metric.summary: The number of subresources that were predicted by the Optimization Guide. This will be recorded if the Optimization Guide was consulted for the page load and the Optimization Guide had a prediction for it. If more than 100 subresources were predicted, this will be capped at 100.

metric.name: OptimizationGuidePredictionSubresources.CrossOrigin
metric.summary: The number of cross-origin subresources that were predicted by the Optimization Guide. This will be recorded if the Optimization Guide was consulted for the page load and the Optimization Guide had a prediction for it. If more than 100 subresources were predicted, this will be capped at 100.

metric.name: SubresourceOriginPreconnectsInitiated
metric.summary: The number of subresource origins that were initiated by the Loading Predictor and were preconnected to, excluding the main frame URL's origin. If more than 100 subresource origins were preconnected to, this will be capped at 100.

metric.name: SubresourcePrefetchesInitiated
metric.summary: The number of subresource prefetches that were initiated by the Loading Predictor for the navigation. If more than 100 subresources were prefetched, this will be capped at 100.

event.name: LocalNetworkRequests
event.summary: Metrics that describe the resource request behavior of pages for which navigation successfully commits. A separate entry is generated for every unique IP address or localhost port number to which the loaded page makes a resource request.

metric.name: Count.Failed
metric.summary: The count of requests made by the page to the given resource for which a response is not received or a network error occurs (i.e. |net_error| != |net::OK|) between the time navigation to the page commits and the time it completes.

metric.name: Count.Successful
metric.summary: The count of requests made by the page to the given resource for which a successful response is received (i.e. |net_error| == |net::OK|) between the time navigation to the page commits and the time it completes.

metric.name: PortType
metric.summary: An enum value representing the type of port for requests to localhost. The enum is defined in |LocalNetworkRequestsPageLoadMetricsObserver|. Possible values are 1 for common web server ports, 2 for common database server ports, 4 for common print server ports, 8 for common development server ports, and 0 for all other ports.

metric.name: ResourceType
metric.summary: An enum value representing the type of resource requested. The enum is defined in |LocalNetworkRequestsPageLoadMetricsObserver|. Possible values are 0 for public resources requested by private pages, 1 for private resources requested by public pages, 2 for private resources within the same reserved IP space as the loaded private page, 4 for private resources within a different reserved IP space than the loaded private page, 8 for resources requested by public pages that are suspected to be routers, and 16 for localhost resources.

event.name: LookalikeUrl.NavigationSuggestion
event.summary: Metrics recorded when a navigated URL's domain name is visually similar to a popular domain or a domain that the user engaged with.

metric.name: MatchType
metric.summary: An enum value representing the type of the match (popular domain, engaged domain, etc.). The enum is defined in |LookalikeUrlBlockingPage|.

metric.name: TriggeredByInitialUrl
metric.summary: A boolean representing whether or not the warning was triggered by the first URL in the chain, or whether the warning was triggered by the final (committed) URL.

metric.name: UserAction
metric.summary: An enum value representing the decision the user made when shown an interstitial (ignored, suggestion_accepted, etc.). The enum is defined in |LookalikeUrlBlockingPage|.

event.name: MainFrameNavigation
event.summary: Metric associated with a main frame navigation. Currently implemented only on iOS.

metric.name: DidCommit
metric.summary: Set to 1 if the navigation successfully committed.

event.name: MainFrameNavigation.ZstdContentEncoding
event.summary: Event recorded on navigation commit when a page load is content-encoded with zstd and is a main frame navigation.

metric.name: UsedZstd
metric.summary: Set to 1 if the main frame served zstd-compressed content.

event.name: Media.Autoplay.Attempt
event.summary: Event recorded when there is an attempt to autoplay (ie. no user gesture). It will be recorded regardless of the result of this attempt.

metric.name: AudioTrack
metric.summary: Whether the element had an audio track when autoplay was attempted.

metric.name: HighMediaEngagement
metric.summary: Whether the document has a high media engagement.

metric.name: Muted
metric.summary: Whether the element was muted when autoplay was attempted.

metric.name: Source
metric.summary: Source of the autoplay attempt: 0 for attribute; 1 for play().

metric.name: UserGestureRequired
metric.summary: Whether a user gesture was required per autoplay rules at the time of attempt. By definition there is no user gesture on the stack when the attempt is registered.

metric.name: UserGestureStatus
metric.summary: Reflects the current status of user gesture/activation. This is a bit field with the following values: - 0b0001 if there is a user gesture on the stack; - 0b0010 if there was a user gesture on the page (ie. was activated); - 0b0100 if there was a user gesture propagated after a navigation.

metric.name: VideoTrack
metric.summary: Whether the element had a video track when autoplay was attempted.

event.name: Media.Autoplay.AudioContext
event.summary: Records the AudioContext autoplay information.

metric.name: SourceNodeStarted
metric.summary: A boolean indicating if start() was called for any source node associated to the AudioContext.

metric.name: Status
metric.summary: Status of this AudioContext when the autoplay policy applies. It will match the values from AudioContext::AutoplayStatus.

metric.name: UnlockType
metric.summary: How the AudioContext was unlocked if it was. It will match the values from AudioContext::AutoplayUnlockType unless the AudioContext was never unlocked in which case it will be equal to -1.

event.name: Media.Autoplay.Muted.UnmuteAction
event.summary: Event recorded when there is an attempt to unmute an media that was autoplaying following the rules of autoplay muted.

metric.name: Result
metric.summary: 0 means that the unmute failed because it happened without a user gesture. 1 means that it succeeded because it had a user gesture.

metric.name: Source
metric.summary: Similar to "Source" in "Media.Autoplay.Attempt" with the addition of a value for both sources being used: 0 for attribute; 1 for play(); 2 for both attempted.

event.name: Media.BasicPlayback
event.summary: Core metrics associated with a media playback through WebMediaPlayerImpl. Reported at the time of WatchTimeRecorder destruction or render process termination; whichever comes first. There will be multiple entries for a given playback; one for every PlaybackProperties change as well as one for foreground and background. Records may be aggregated based on PlayerID and correlated with the Media.WebMediaPlayerState event.

metric.name: AudioCodec
metric.summary: media::AudioCodec enum value. Can be kUnknownAudioCodec even when HasAudio is true because we don't always know the codec.

metric.name: AudioCodecProfile
metric.summary: media::AudioCodecProfile enum value. Can be AudioCodecProfile::kUnknown even when HasAudio is true because we don't always know the codec profile.

metric.name: AudioDecoderName
metric.summary: Enumeration of audio decoder implementations, zero if none or unknown (Cast, HLS, etc). See AudioDecoderType (media/base/decoder.h) for expected values.

metric.name: AudioEncryptionScheme
metric.summary: media::EncryptionScheme enum value (media/base/encryption_scheme.h). Can be kUnencrypted if IsEME is false or if the audio track is unencrypted.

metric.name: AutoplayInitiated
metric.summary: Boolean value indicating whether the initial playback was initiated via autoplay. Multilpe chunk records of the same player should have the same value. Autoplay is defined as a playback starting before there was a user activation on the page or without a user initiated same-domain navigation.

metric.name: CompletedRebuffersCount
metric.summary: Integer count of the number of times a previous rebuffer succeeded and playback continued.

metric.name: CompletedRebuffersDuration
metric.summary: Sum in milliseconds of all completed rebuffering events. Only reported if a rebuffering completion occurred.

metric.name: Duration
metric.summary: Duration in milliseconds, rounded to the most significant digit, of the media being played. May be missing if the duration is unknown or infinite.

metric.name: HasAudio
metric.summary: Boolean value indicating the presence of audio.

metric.name: HasVideo
metric.summary: Boolean value indicating the presence of video.

metric.name: IsBackground
metric.summary: Boolean value indicating if this event is for a background playback.

metric.name: IsEME
metric.summary: Boolean value indicating if this event is for an EME playback.

metric.name: IsMSE
metric.summary: Boolean value indicating if this event is for an MSE playback. If false it means this was a SRC playback.

metric.name: IsMuted
metric.summary: Boolean value indicating if this event is for a muted playback.

metric.name: IsTopFrame
metric.summary: Flag indicating whether the report comes from the top frame or some inner frame. For privacy, metrics from inner frames are recorded with the top frame's origin, so this flag helps separate top frame vs. embedded playbacks.

metric.name: LastPipelineStatus
metric.summary: media::PipelineStatusCodes enum value (media/base/pipeline_status.h). Always 0 if the playback succeeded; all other values indicate the playback ended in an error.

metric.name: MeanTimeBetweenRebuffers
metric.summary: Average number of milliseconds between rebuffering events. Only reported if a rebuffering event occurred. Computed by dividing total watch time by the number of rebuffering events.

metric.name: MediaStreamType
metric.summary: Enum value indicating the type of MediaStream if the playback is from a MediaStream, or kNonMediaStream otherwise.

metric.name: PlayerID
metric.summary: ID which corresponds to a given WebMediaPlayerImpl instance. May be linked with Media.WebMediaPlayerState events to understand playback more deeply.

metric.name: RebuffersCount
metric.summary: Integer count of the number of times playback experienced rebuffering.

metric.name: VideoCodec
metric.summary: media::VideoCodec enum value. Can be kUnknownVideoCodec even when HasVideo is true because we don't always know the codec.

metric.name: VideoCodecProfile
metric.summary: media::VideoCodecProfile enum value. Can be VIDEO_CODEC_PROFILE_UNKNOWN even when HasVideo is true because we don't always know the codec profile.

metric.name: VideoDecoderName
metric.summary: Enumeration of video decoder implementations, zero if none or unknown (Cast, HLS, etc). See VideoDecoderType (media/base/decoder.h) for expected values.

metric.name: VideoEncryptionScheme
metric.summary: media::EncryptionScheme enum value (media/base/encryption_scheme.h). Can be kUnencrypted if IsEME is false or if the video track is unencrypted.

metric.name: VideoFramesDecoded
metric.summary: Integer count of the video frames decoded in this record.

metric.name: VideoFramesDropped
metric.summary: Integer count of the video frames dropped in this record. Should not exceed VideoFramesDecoded.

metric.name: VideoNaturalHeight
metric.summary: Integer value indicating the natural height of the playback.

metric.name: VideoNaturalWidth
metric.summary: Integer value indicating the natural width of the playback.

metric.name: WatchTime
metric.summary: Watch time in milliseconds for this playback. See Media.WatchTime UMA metrics.

metric.name: WatchTime.AC
metric.summary: Watch time on AC power in milliseconds for this playback. See Media.WatchTime UMA metrics.

metric.name: WatchTime.Battery
metric.summary: Watch time on battery power in milliseconds for this playback. See Media.WatchTime UMA metrics.

metric.name: WatchTime.DisplayFullscreen
metric.summary: Watch time for fullscreen in milliseconds for this playback. See Media.WatchTime UMA metrics. Only reported for foreground playbacks.

metric.name: WatchTime.DisplayInline
metric.summary: Watch time for inline display in milliseconds for this playback. See Media.WatchTime UMA metrics. Only reported for foreground playbacks.

metric.name: WatchTime.DisplayPictureInPicture
metric.summary: Watch time for picture in picture display in milliseconds for this playback. See Media.WatchTime UMA metrics. Only reported for foreground playbacks.

metric.name: WatchTime.NativeControlsOff
metric.summary: Watch time with no controls in milliseconds for this playback. See Media.WatchTime UMA metrics. Only reported for foreground playbacks.

metric.name: WatchTime.NativeControlsOn
metric.summary: Watch time with native controls in milliseconds for this playback. See Media.WatchTime UMA metrics. Only reported for foreground playbacks.

event.name: Media.EME.ApiPromiseRejection
event.summary: Event recorded when an Encrypted Media Extensions (EME) API promise is rejected. In most cases this indicates a failure.

metric.name: Api
metric.summary: The EME API for which the promise is rejected.

metric.name: KeySystem
metric.summary: The key system associated with the current CDM.

metric.name: SystemCode
metric.summary: A CDM specific code providing more details about the cause of rejection.

metric.name: UseHardwareSecureCodecs
metric.summary: Whether hardware secure codecs are required for the current CDM.

event.name: Media.EME.CreateMediaKeys
event.summary: Event recorded when createMediaKeys() is called as part of Encrypted Media Extensions (EME) API.

metric.name: IsAdFrame
metric.summary: Whether the frame is an ad frame.

metric.name: IsCrossOrigin
metric.summary: Whether the frame is Cross-Origin to the main frame.

metric.name: IsTopFrame
metric.summary: Whether the frame is the main frame.

metric.name: KeySystem
metric.summary: The key system associated with this call.

event.name: Media.EME.GetStatusForPolicy
event.summary: Event recorded when the EME API GetStatusForPolicy promise is resolved.

metric.name: IsAdFrame
metric.summary: Whether the frame is an ad frame. This is not set if the frame is null.

metric.name: KeySystem
metric.summary: The key system associated with the current CDM.

metric.name: MinHdcpVersion
metric.summary: The minimum HDCP version requested for EME API GetStatusForPolicy.

metric.name: UseHardwareSecureCodecs
metric.summary: Whether hardware secure codecs are required for the current CDM.

event.name: Media.EME.RequestMediaKeySystemAccess
event.summary: Event recorded when RequestMediaKeySystemAccess() is called as part of Encrypted Media Extensions (EME) API.

metric.name: IsAdFrame
metric.summary: Whether the frame is an ad frame.

metric.name: IsCrossOrigin
metric.summary: Whether the frame is Cross-Origin to the main frame.

metric.name: IsFromMediaCapabilities
metric.summary: Whether called by MediaCapabilities or not.

metric.name: IsTopFrame
metric.summary: Whether the frame is the main frame.

metric.name: KeySystem
metric.summary: The key system passed in requestMediaKeySystemAccess() call.

metric.name: VideoCapabilities
metric.summary: Whether there are any "videoCapabilities".

metric.name: VideoCapabilities.HasEmptyRobustness
metric.summary: Whether there are any "videoCapabilities" with empty robustness.

metric.name: VideoCapabilities.HasHwSecureAllRobustness
metric.summary: Whether there are any "videoCapabilities" with robustness being "HW_SECURE_ALL".

event.name: Media.EME.Usage
event.summary: Event recorded when an Encrypted Media Extensions (EME) API is called.

metric.name: Api
metric.summary: The EME API that is called.

metric.name: IsPersistentSession
metric.summary: For EME API calls done on MediaKeySession, whether the session is persistent or not.

metric.name: KeySystem
metric.summary: The key system associated with the current CDM.

metric.name: UseHardwareSecureCodecs
metric.summary: Whether hardware secure codecs are required for the current CDM.

event.name: Media.Engagement.SessionFinished
event.summary:
The Media Engagement index stores the number of significant media playbacks per origin and the number of visits. From that we calculate a Media Engagement Score.
To tweak the scoring function we are logging the total number of significant media playbacks, the total number of visits, the calculated engagement score and the new number of significant media playbacks that occurred this visit.

metric.name: Engagement.IsHigh
metric.summary: Whether the Media Engagement Service considers the score to be high (we are using a two threshold approach so there is one threshold to be considered high and another one to lose that status to reduce jitter).

metric.name: Engagement.Score
metric.summary: The calculated Media Engagement score for the current origin. The score is calculated by dividing the number of significant media playbacks by the number of visits. If the number of visits is below 5 then the score will be zero. This score is taken from MediaEngagementService.

metric.name: Playbacks.Total
metric.summary: The total number of significant media playbacks on this origin.

metric.name: Player.Audible.Delta
metric.summary: The number of unique audio/video players on a page that was audible (made sound) during a visit.

metric.name: Player.Significant.Delta
metric.summary: The number of unique audio/video players on a page that was audible (made sound) and considered significant (played for at least 7 seconds) during a visit.

metric.name: Visits.Total
metric.summary: The total number of visits to this origin.

event.name: Media.Engagement.ShortPlaybackIgnored
event.summary:
The Media Engagement index stores the number of significant media playbacks per origin and the number of audible players. From that we calculate a Media Engagement Score.
Media with a short playback length is ignored so we are logging any time the player is ignored with the length in msec. This will allow us to identify whether sites are being penalized or there is abuse and allow us to tweak the length considered "short".

event.name: Media.Feed.Discover
event.summary: Media Feeds are out of band feeds of media recommendations that are fetched by the browser. This records when we discover a feed that has been placed in a meta tag on a website.

metric.name: HasMediaFeed
metric.summary: Whether the site has a media feed.

event.name: Media.GlobalMediaControls.ActionButtonPressed
event.summary: Records when a user presses a button in the Global Media Controls. Records no more than 100 actions per source per session. Records which button was pressed (e.g. play, pause, next track, picture-in-picture).

metric.name: MediaSessionAction
metric.summary: The action of the button that was pressed (e.g. play, pause, next track, picture-in-picture).

event.name: Media.Learning.PredictionRecord
event.summary:
A record of some predicted value vs. an observation. If the prediction is for a regression-style task (e.g., "predict a number" rather than "predict an enum value"), then the predicted and observed values are the numeric values, scaled by default to be in the integer range 0-100.
The exact scaling used depends on the particular regression problem. Please see the LearningTask structure for the task of interest to find out how it maps values into the [0, 100] output range.
A record is created for every prediction separately.
For example, in the MediaCapabilities experiments, each playback generates a record. The predicted / observed values are the ratio of dropped to decoded frames, expressed as a percentage scaled to 0-100.

metric.name: LearningTask
metric.summary: The learning task to which this record applies. This is a persistent hash of the |name| field in the LearningTask. Presumably, you'll want to include only records of a particular task when analyzing results.

metric.name: ObservedValue
metric.summary:
Observed value for this sample, scaled into the range [0, 100]. The exact scaling factor depends on the particular task.
MediaCapabilities, for example, reports 100 * percentage_of_dropped_frames in this field.

metric.name: PredictedValue
metric.summary:
Predicted value for this sample, scaled into the range [0, 100]. The exact scaling factor depends on the particular task.
MediaCapabilities, for example, reports 100 * percentage_of_dropped_frames in this field.

metric.name: TrainingDataSize
metric.summary: Number of training examples that were used to train this model.

metric.name: TrainingDataTotalWeight
metric.summary: Total weight of all examples that were used to train this model.

event.name: Media.SiteMuted
event.summary: Event recorded when a website tries to play audio but is muted by the sound content setting.

metric.name: MuteReason
metric.summary: Enum value giving the reason the site was muted. Defined as |SoundContentSettingObserver::MuteReason|.

event.name: Media.VideoDecodePerfRecord
event.summary: A record of decoding performance metrics from a video playback with the given stream characteristics (profile, resolution, fps). Also includes what Media Capabilities API would claim for such a stream as a means of assessing the API's accuracy.

metric.name: Perf.ApiWouldClaimIsPowerEfficient
metric.summary: Boolean signaling whether MediaCapabilities would classify streams with these characteristics as power efficient prior to considering this latest record. MediaCapabilities prediction is accurate when this value matches RecordIsPowerEfficient.

metric.name: Perf.ApiWouldClaimIsSmooth
metric.summary: Boolean signaling whether MediaCapabilities would classify streams with these characteristics as smooth prior to considering this latest record. MediaCapabilities prediction is accurate when this value matches RecordIsSmooth.

metric.name: Perf.PastVideoFramesDecoded
metric.summary: Integer count of past video frames decoded for the given video type.

metric.name: Perf.PastVideoFramesDropped
metric.summary: Integer count of past video frames dropped for the given video type.

metric.name: Perf.PastVideoFramesPowerEfficient
metric.summary: Integer count of past video frames power efficient for the given video type.

metric.name: Perf.RecordIsPowerEfficient
metric.summary: Boolean signaling whether MediaCapabilities would classify this isolated playback record as power efficient.

metric.name: Perf.RecordIsSmooth
metric.summary: Boolean signaling whether MediaCapabilities would classify this isolated playback record as smooth.

metric.name: Perf.VideoFramesDecoded
metric.summary: Integer count of the video frames decoded in this record.

metric.name: Perf.VideoFramesDropped
metric.summary: Integer count of the video frames dropped in this record. Should not exceed VideoFramesDecoded.

metric.name: Perf.VideoFramesPowerEfficient
metric.summary: Integer count of the video frames decoded via power efficient means in this record. Should not exceed VideoFramesDecoded.

metric.name: Video.CodecProfile
metric.summary: media::VideoCodecProfile enum value. Can be VIDEO_CODEC_PROFILE_UNKNOWN if we don't know the video codec.

metric.name: Video.EME.KeySystem
metric.summary: Name of the KeySystem used during for an encrypted (EME) playback. Will be unset when EME is not used.

metric.name: Video.EME.UseHwSecureCodecs
metric.summary: Boolean indicating whether content decryption module (CDM) was configured to use hardware secure decoding. May be true or false for EME playbacks. Will be unset for non-EME playbacks.

metric.name: Video.FramesPerSecond
metric.summary: Integer representing video frames per second. This is the cadence of video frames as described by stream timestamps multiplied by HTMLMediaELement.playbackRate.

metric.name: Video.InTopFrame
metric.summary: Flag indicating whether the record comes from the top frame or some inner frame. For privacy, metrics from inner frames are recorded with the top frame's origin, so this flag helps separate top frame vs. embedded playbacks.

metric.name: Video.NaturalHeight
metric.summary: Integer representing height of video natural size.

metric.name: Video.NaturalWidth
metric.summary: Integer representing width of video natural size.

metric.name: Video.PlayerID
metric.summary: ID which corresponds to a given WebMediaPlayerImpl instance. May be linked with Media.WebMediaPlayerState events to understand playback more deeply.

event.name: Media.VideoEncoderMetrics
event.summary: A record of encoding usage metrics in chrome, for example, for cast mirroring and webcodecs. This is recorded in the end of each video encoding session.

metric.name: Height
metric.summary: Integer value indicating the height of the encoded stream. This is bucketed per 100 and capped by 8200.

metric.name: IsHardware
metric.summary: Boolean value indicating whether the encoder implementation is hardware accelerated one or not.

metric.name: NumEncodedFrames
metric.summary: Integer value indicating the number of the successfully encoded frames. This is bucketed per 100.

metric.name: Profile
metric.summary: media::VideoCodecProfile enum value. The codec profile configured to encode the video.

metric.name: Status
metric.summary: media::EncoderStatus::Codes enum value. The first error status code on error, otherwise media::EncoderStatus::Codes::kOk.

metric.name: SVCMode
metric.summary: Integer value indicating the SVC configuration of the encoding. The value corresponds to media::SVCScalabilityMode.

metric.name: UseCase
metric.summary: Integer value indicating the video encoder use case. The value corresponds to media:::mojom::VideoEncoderUseCase.

metric.name: Width
metric.summary: Integer value indicating the width of the encoded stream. This is bucketed per 100 and capped by 8200.

event.name: Media.WebAudio.AudioContext.AudibleTime
event.summary: Records the AudioContext audible time information.

metric.name: AudibleTime
metric.summary: Audible time in milliseconds for this event.

metric.name: IsMainFrame
metric.summary: Indicates whether the event is fired from main frame.

event.name: Media.WebMediaPlayerState
event.summary: Final state of WebMediaPlayerImpl instance. Records only immutable playback properties. Contains a PlaybackID which links to Media.BasicPlayback events.

metric.name: AudioEncryptionType
metric.summary: Encryption type of the audio stream such as whether audio is encrypted and if so whether it has clear lead.

metric.name: ContainerName
metric.summary: media::container_names::MediaContainerName enum value. Only recorded for !IsMSE src=URL playbacks, this is the container of the media being played back; E.g., mp4, avi, mp3, etc.

metric.name: FinalPipelineStatus
metric.summary: media::PipelineStatus enum value. Always 0 if the playback succeeded; all other values indicate the playback ended in an error.

metric.name: HasWaitingForKey
metric.summary: Boolean value indicating whether an EME playback has been blocked waiting for the CDM to be set or waiting for decryption key. For an non-EME playback, this is always reported as false.

metric.name: IsEME
metric.summary: Boolean value indicating if this event is for an EME playback. Note: EME can be attached anytime during the lifecycle of a WebMediaPlayerImpl, but once attached can't be removed.

metric.name: IsHardwareSecure
metric.summary: Boolean value indicating whether the EME playback is using hardware secure pipeline. For clear playback, this will always be false.

metric.name: IsMSE
metric.summary: Boolean value indicating if this event is for an MSE playback. If false it means this was a SRC playback.

metric.name: IsTopFrame
metric.summary: Flag indicating whether the report comes from the top frame or some inner frame. For privacy, metrics from inner frames are recorded with the top frame's origin, so this flag helps separate top frame vs. embedded playbacks.

metric.name: KeySystem
metric.summary: For EME playback, this is the key system used. For clear playback, this will always be the default value 0 (kUnknownKeySystemForUkm).

metric.name: PlayerID
metric.summary: ID which corresponds to a given WebMediaPlayerImpl instance. May be linked with Media.BasicPlayback events to understand a playback more deeply.

metric.name: RendererType
metric.summary: Type of media::Renderer used for the playback. In most cases we do not support dynamically changing RendererType during one playback.

metric.name: TimeToFirstFrame
metric.summary: Time in milliseconds from when WebMediaPlayerImpl starts loading until the first video frame has been shown.

metric.name: TimeToMetadata
metric.summary: Time in milliseconds from when WebMediaPlayerImpl starts loading until metadata is known.

metric.name: TimeToPlayReady
metric.summary: Time in milliseconds from when WebMediaPlayerImpl starts loading until it has buffered enough to start playback.

metric.name: URLScheme
metric.summary: media::mojom::MediaURLScheme enum value. Only recorded for !IsMSE src=URL playbacks, this is the scheme of that URL; E.g., http, https, filesystem, etc.

metric.name: VideoEncryptionType
metric.summary: Encryption type of the video stream such as whether video is encrypted and if so whether it has clear lead.

event.name: MediaRouter.CastWebSenderExtensionLoadUrl
event.summary: Records that the a resource has been loaded from the Media Router Component Extension.

metric.name: HasOccurred
metric.summary: A boolean signaling that the event has occurred; only records true values.

event.name: MediaRouter.SiteInitiatedMirroringStarted
event.summary: Recorded whenever user starts a site-initiated mirroring session via Media Router.

metric.name: AllowAudioCapture
metric.summary: Whether audio capture is allowed in the cast session. If allowed, audio playback will happen on the receiver side. If not allowed, audio playback will happen on the sender side.

event.name: MediaRouter.TabMirroringStarted
event.summary: Recorded whenever user starts a tab mirroring session via Media Router.

metric.name: AudioState
metric.summary: The audio playback state of the WebContents at the start of the tab mirroring session.

event.name: MediaStream.Device
event.summary: Events recorded when a page requests access device media, i.e. by starting screen capture or opening a webcam or microphone, and the result of that request.

metric.name: VideoCaptureGenerateStreamResult
metric.summary: The result of a page's request to open a video capture device (e.g., webcam).

event.name: Memory.Experimental
event.summary: Metrics associated with memory consumption, in MB.

metric.name: ArrayBuffer
metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by Array Buffer.

metric.name: BlinkGC
metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by Oilpan.

metric.name: BlinkGC.AllocatedObjects
metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by live allocations made from Oilpan.

metric.name: CommandBuffer
metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by GL command buffer.

metric.name: Discardable
metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by Discardable memory service.

metric.name: DownloadService
metric.summary: Measure of memory used by download service.

metric.name: Extensions.ValueStore
metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by Key Value Store databases of extensions.

metric.name: FontCaches
metric.summary: Measure of memory used by font platform and shape caches in renderer process.

metric.name: GpuMemory
metric.summary: Measure of GPU memory used by Chrome.

metric.name: History
metric.summary: Approximate measure of memory consumed by History service.

metric.name: IsVisible
metric.summary: Indicates whether the tab is visible or not at the time of metric collection.

metric.name: JavaHeap
metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by java heap on Android.

metric.name: LevelDatabase
metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by unaccounted level databases.

metric.name: Malloc
metric.summary: Measure of memory allocated by malloc, that is not classified by other metrics.

metric.name: Malloc.AllocatedObjects
metric.summary: Measure of total memory used by objects allocated using malloc.

metric.name: NumberOfAdSubframes
metric.summary: The number of ad subframes that the associated renderer owns.

metric.name: NumberOfArrayBufferContents
metric.summary: The number of arraybuffer contents that the associated renderer owns.

metric.name: NumberOfDetachedScriptStates
metric.summary: The number of detached ScriptState that the associated renderer owns.

metric.name: NumberOfDocuments
metric.summary: The number of documents that the associated renderer owns.

metric.name: NumberOfExtensions
metric.summary: The number of extensions that are served from the associated renderer process.

metric.name: NumberOfFrames
metric.summary: The number of frames that the associated renderer owns.

metric.name: NumberOfLayoutObjects
metric.summary: The number of layout objects that the associated renderer owns.

metric.name: NumberOfMojoHandles
metric.summary: The number of mojo handles stored in HandleTable.

metric.name: NumberOfNodes
metric.summary: The number of nodes that the associated renderer owns.

metric.name: NumberOfWebMediaPlayers
metric.summary: The number of web media players in the associated renderer.

metric.name: OmniboxSuggestions
metric.summary: Measure of memory used due to URL indexing and autocomplete suggestions.

metric.name: PartitionAlloc
metric.summary: Measure of memory allocated by PartitionAlloc allocator.

metric.name: PartitionAlloc.AllocatedObjects
metric.summary: Measure of total memory used by objects allocated using PartitionAlloc.

metric.name: PartitionAlloc.Partitions.ArrayBuffer
metric.summary: Measure of memory used by Array Buffer partition in PartitionAlloc.

metric.name: PartitionAlloc.Partitions.Buffer
metric.summary: Measure of memory used by Buffer partition in PartitionAlloc.

metric.name: PartitionAlloc.Partitions.FastMalloc
metric.summary: Measure of memory used by Fast Malloc partition in PartitionAlloc.

metric.name: PartitionAlloc.Partitions.Layout
metric.summary: Measure of memory used by Layout partition in PartitionAlloc.

metric.name: PrivateMemoryFootprint
metric.summary: Measure of total private memory consumed by process.

metric.name: PrivateSwapFootprint
metric.summary: Measure of private swap memory consumed by a process. Available on Linux and Android.

metric.name: ProcessType
metric.summary: Type of process (e.g. browser, renderer, GPU --- see services/resource_coordinator/public/mojom/memory_instrumentation/memory_instrumentation.mojom) of associated metrics.

metric.name: Resident
metric.summary: Size of process' working set.

metric.name: SharedMemoryFootprint
metric.summary: Measure of total shared memory consumed by process.

metric.name: SiteStorage
metric.summary: Measure of memory used due to web storage APIs in browser process.

metric.name: SiteStorage.BlobStorage
metric.summary: Measure of memory used by in-memory blob file API in browser process.

metric.name: SiteStorage.IndexDB
metric.summary: Measure of memory used due to IndexedDB API in browser process.

metric.name: SiteStorage.LocalStorage
metric.summary: Measure of memory used due to Local Storage API in browser process.

metric.name: SiteStorage.SessionStorage
metric.summary: Measure of memory used due to Session Storage API in browser process.

metric.name: Skia
metric.summary: Measure of memory used by Skia.

metric.name: Skia.SkGlyphCache
metric.summary: Measure of memory used by Skia Glyph Cache.

metric.name: Skia.SkResourceCache
metric.summary: Measure of memory used by Skia Resource Cache.

metric.name: Sqlite
metric.summary: Measure of memory used by all sqlite databases.

metric.name: Sync
metric.summary: Measure of memory used by Sync storage.

metric.name: TabRestore
metric.summary: Approximate measure of memory used by Tab restore service.

metric.name: TimeSinceLastNavigation
metric.summary: The time in seconds since the Tab navigated. Only emitted for tabs that are not sharing a process.

metric.name: TimeSinceLastVisibilityChange
metric.summary: The time in seconds since the Tab changed visibility. Only emitted for tabs that are not sharing a process.

metric.name: Total2.PrivateMemoryFootprint
metric.summary: Measure of total private memory consumed by all processes.

metric.name: Total2.SharedMemoryFootprint
metric.summary: Measure of total shared memory consumed by all processes.

metric.name: UI
metric.summary: Measure of memory used by Android UI bitmaps.

metric.name: Uptime
metric.summary: Process uptime.

metric.name: V8
metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by V8.

metric.name: V8.AllocatedObjects
metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by live objects in V8.

metric.name: V8.Main
metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by the main isolate of V8.

metric.name: V8.Main.AllocatedObjects
metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by live objects in the main isolate of V8.

metric.name: V8.Main.GlobalHandles
