metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by global handles in V8.

metric.name: V8.Main.GlobalHandles.AllocatedObjects
metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by used global handles in V8.

metric.name: V8.Main.Heap
metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by the main heap of V8.

metric.name: V8.Main.Heap.AllocatedObjects
metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by live objects in the main heap of V8.

metric.name: V8.Main.Heap.CodeLargeObjectSpace
metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by the code large object space of the main heap of V8.

metric.name: V8.Main.Heap.CodeLargeObjectSpace.AllocatedObjects
metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by live objects in the code large object space of the main heap of V8.

metric.name: V8.Main.Heap.CodeSpace
metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by the code space of the main heap of V8.

metric.name: V8.Main.Heap.CodeSpace.AllocatedObjects
metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by live objects in the code space of the main heap of V8.

metric.name: V8.Main.Heap.LargeObjectSpace
metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by the large object space of the main heap of V8.

metric.name: V8.Main.Heap.LargeObjectSpace.AllocatedObjects
metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by live objects in the large object space of the main heap of V8.

metric.name: V8.Main.Heap.MapSpace
metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by the map space of the main heap of V8.

metric.name: V8.Main.Heap.MapSpace.AllocatedObjects
metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by live objects in the map space of the main heap of V8.

metric.name: V8.Main.Heap.NewLargeObjectSpace
metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by the new large object space of the main heap of V8.

metric.name: V8.Main.Heap.NewLargeObjectSpace.AllocatedObjects
metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by live objects in the new large object space of the main heap of V8.

metric.name: V8.Main.Heap.NewSpace
metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by the new space of the main heap of V8.

metric.name: V8.Main.Heap.NewSpace.AllocatedObjects
metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by live objects in the new space of the main heap of V8.

metric.name: V8.Main.Heap.OldSpace
metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by the old space of the main heap of V8.

metric.name: V8.Main.Heap.OldSpace.AllocatedObjects
metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by live objects in the old space of the main heap of V8.

metric.name: V8.Main.Heap.ReadOnlySpace
metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by the read-only space of the main heap of V8.

metric.name: V8.Main.Heap.ReadOnlySpace.AllocatedObjects
metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by live objects in the read-only space of the main heap of V8.

metric.name: V8.Main.Heap.SharedLargeObjectSpace
metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by the shared large object space of the main heap of V8. Measured in bytes.

metric.name: V8.Main.Heap.SharedLargeObjectSpace.AllocatedObjects
metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by live objects in the shared large object space of the main heap of V8. Measured in bytes.

metric.name: V8.Main.Heap.SharedSpace
metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by the shared space of the main heap of V8. Measured in bytes.

metric.name: V8.Main.Heap.SharedSpace.AllocatedObjects
metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by live objects in the shared space of the main heap of V8. Measured in bytes.

metric.name: V8.Main.Malloc
metric.summary: Measure of memory allocated by malloc in the main isolate of V8.

metric.name: V8.Workers
metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by worker isolates of V8.

metric.name: V8.Workers.AllocatedObjects
metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by live objects in worker isolates of V8.

metric.name: WebCache
metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by all resources in Blink Web Cache.

metric.name: WebCache.CSSStylesheetResources
metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by CSS Stylesheet resources in Blink Web Cache.

metric.name: WebCache.EncodedSizeDuplicatedInDataUrls
metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by data URLs of Image resources in Blink Web Cache.

metric.name: WebCache.FontResources
metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by Font resources in Blink Web Cache.

metric.name: WebCache.ImageResources
metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by Image resources in Blink Web Cache.

metric.name: WebCache.OtherResources
metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by other resources in Blink Web Cache.

metric.name: WebCache.ScriptResources
metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by Script resources in Blink Web Cache.

metric.name: WebCache.V8CodeCache
metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by V8 code cache held by Script resources in Blink Web Cache.

metric.name: WebCache.XSLStylesheetResources
metric.summary: Measure of memory consumed by XSL Stylesheet resources in Blink Web Cache.

metric.name: WebMediaPlayer.Audio
metric.summary: Measure of total memory used by media/webmediaplayer/audio.

metric.name: WebMediaPlayer.DataSource
metric.summary: Measure of total memory used by media/webmediaplayer/data_source.

metric.name: WebMediaPlayer.Demuxer
metric.summary: Measure of total memory used by media/webmediaplayer/demuxer.

metric.name: WebMediaPlayer.Video
metric.summary: Measure of total memory used by media/webmediaplayer/video.

event.name: Memory.TabFootprint
event.summary: Measure of memory used by the processes that host an instance of a tab. Intended as a high-level metric for analyzing effective memory use of individual sites.

metric.name: MainFrameProcessPMF
metric.summary: Measure of private memory, in MB, consumed by the render process dedicated to hosting the main frame. Undefined if the main frame is hosted by a render process used by other tabs. If this is undefined, TabPMF will be undefined.

metric.name: SubFrameProcessPMF.Excluded
metric.summary: Number of render processes that were blocked from contributing to SubFrameProcessPMF.Total. A process can have its contribution blocked if the process has responsibilities outside the scope of the relevant tab. If this is non-zero, TabPMF will be undefined.

metric.name: SubFrameProcessPMF.Included
metric.summary: Number of render processes that contributed to SubFrameProcessPMF.Total.

metric.name: SubFrameProcessPMF.Total
metric.summary: Measure of total private memory, in MB, consumed by the render processes dedicated to hosting sub-frames of the tab. Note that, if a render process hosts frames of other tabs, it isn't considered to be 'dedicated' and so it doesn't contribute to this total.

metric.name: TabPMF
metric.summary: Measure of private memory, in MB, consumed by all render processes dedicated to hosting some part of a tab. Undefined if there are processes responsible for hosting parts of this tab while hosting parts of other tabs.

event.name: MixedContentAutoupgrade.ResourceRequest
event.summary: Status and Network error or HTTP response code for a resource request that was autoupgraded to HTTPS as part of the mixed content autoupgrade experiment.

metric.name: Code
metric.summary: The HTTP response or network error code for an autoupgraded request.

metric.name: Status
metric.summary: An enum with 0 representing started, 1 failed, and 2 response received.

metric.name: AllowUserZoom
metric.summary: Whether the page allows the user to zoom in/out.

metric.name: SmallTextRatio
metric.summary: Percentage of small font text area in total text area.

metric.name: TextContentOutsideViewportPercentage
metric.summary: Percentage of pixels of text and images horizontally outside the viewport, relative to the frame width.

metric.name: ViewportDeviceWidth
metric.summary: Whether the width of the viewport specified as device-width or not.

metric.name: ViewportHardcodedWidth
metric.summary: Specified hardcoded viewport width in CSS pixels.

metric.name: ViewportInitialScaleX10
metric.summary: Specified initial viewport scaling multiplied by 10. 1 means 0.1, 100 means 10. [1-100].

metric.name: CloseDisplayEdge
metric.summary: The distance from the center position of tapped target to display's edge is less than 5mm.

metric.name: HighlyZoomed
metric.summary: Whether the page is manually 100%+ zoomed when tapped.

metric.name: TooClose
metric.summary: Whether the center of the tapped tap target is close less than 5mm to other tap target's edge.

metric.name: TooSmall
metric.summary: Whether the tapped tap target is small as both width and height is less than 7mm.

metric.name: Zoomed
metric.summary: Whether the page is manually 20%+ zoomed when tapped.

event.name: MobileMenu.DirectShare
event.summary: User pressed 'Share' in the app menu. The intent most recently used to share was invoked directly. See also MobileMenu.Share.

metric.name: HasOccurred
metric.summary: A boolean signaling that the event has occurred; only records true values.

event.name: MobileMenu.FindInPage
event.summary: User pressed 'Find in page' in the app menu.

metric.name: HasOccurred
metric.summary: A boolean signaling that the event has occurred (typically only records true values).

event.name: MobileMenu.Share
event.summary: User pressed 'Share' in the app menu. A picker was shown. See also MobileMenu.DirectShare.

metric.name: HasOccurred
metric.summary: A boolean signaling that the event has occurred; only records true values.

event.name: Navigation.IDNA2008Transition
event.summary: Recorded upon main frame navigations to hostnames that contain characters that are treated differently between IDNA 2003 and IDNA 2008, aka deviation characters. Ignores reload navigations.

metric.name: Character
metric.summary: The deviation character in the URLs eTLD+1. Chrome's URL parsing code normalizes deviation characters so the characters don't appear in the recorded URL. This is recorded to make the deviation character identification simpler.

event.name: Navigation.ReceivedResponse
event.summary: Recorded when the navigation receives a response from the networking layer. Only recorded for main frames (i.e. top-level frames).

metric.name: HasAcceptCHFrame
metric.summary: A boolean value representing whether the navigation included the processing of an ACCEPT_CH ALPS frame.

metric.name: NavigationFirstResponseLatency
metric.summary: The duration (in milliseconds) for how long it took from the time the navigation loader is created to the time the navigation loader receives the response from the networking layer.

event.name: NavigationPredictorAnchorElementMetrics
event.summary: Metrics that describe an anchor element's data. This event is emitted at most 10 times per page load, once for each of the top n URLs on a page, and the order of the events is randomized. If there is no nth anchor element, no value is returned.

metric.name: AnchorIndex
metric.summary: The index of the clicked-on anchor element in the tracked list.

metric.name: BucketedPathHash
metric.summary: The anchor element's href URL path is hashed, then bucketed using 10 different buckets. The bucket ({0, 1, ..., 9}) the hash falls into is logged in this field.

metric.name: ContainsImage
metric.summary: 1 if the anchor element contains an image.

metric.name: FontSize
metric.summary: The bucketed font size of the anchor element, according to its computed style. 1 corresponds to a pixel font size in [0, 10), 2 corresponds to a fonts size in [10, 18) and 3 is logged when the font size is 18 pixels or larger.

metric.name: HasTextSibling
metric.summary: 1 if the anchor element has an immediate text sibling, 0 otherwise.

metric.name: IsBold
metric.summary: 1 if the anchor's computed style's font-weight is greater than 500, 0 otherwise.

metric.name: IsInIframe
metric.summary: 1 if the anchor element is in an iframe.

metric.name: IsURLIncrementedByOne
metric.summary: 1 if the anchor element's target URL and the document's source URL only differ by one number, and the target URL increments that number in the source URL by 1.

metric.name: NavigationStartToLinkLoggedMs
metric.summary: The time in ms between navigation start and the moment this entry was logged. Exponentially bucketed using GetExponentialBucketMin a value of 1.3.

metric.name: PathDepth
metric.summary: The number of slashes in the anchor element's href URL path. Truncated at 5.

metric.name: PathLength
metric.summary: The number of characters in the anchor element's href URL path. Buckets of size 10, truncated at 100.

metric.name: PercentClickableArea
metric.summary: The percent of the total document area that this anchor element's clickable area occupies.

metric.name: PercentVerticalDistance
metric.summary: How far down the document the top of the anchor element is, expressed as a ratio with the total document height.

metric.name: SameOrigin
metric.summary: 1 if the anchor element's target URL and the document's source URL are the same origin.

event.name: NavigationPredictorPageLinkClick
event.summary: Sent on an in-page link click.

metric.name: AnchorElementIndex
metric.summary: An integer (0-indexed), corresponding to the index of that anchor element in the list of top ten anchor elements on that page, sorted by their navigation scores. If an anchor element is clicked that is not in NavigationPredictorAnchorElementMetrics, -1 is returned.

metric.name: HrefUnchanged
metric.summary: 1 if the href of the anchor element at the time the click is handled is the same as the href when the element was created. 0 if the href changed.

metric.name: NavigationStartToLinkClickedMs
metric.summary: The time in milliseconds between the moment an entry for the anchor element was logged to UKM and the moment the user clicked on it. Exponentially bucketed, 1.3 factor.

event.name: NavigationPredictorPageLinkMetrics
event.summary: Metrics that describe aggregate information about the links within a page. Some anchor elements present on a page may not be represented in these metrics, if they are outside the viewport or if their placement in the page is after the 40th anchor element. This event is emitted at most once per page load. Unless otherwise specified, each of the aggregate metrics is bucketted with exponential buckets of size 1.3.

metric.name: MedianLinkLocation
metric.summary: The median vertical link location in the viewport, as a ratio of the distance from the top of the link to the visible top of the page. Bucketted with a linear bucket size of 10.

metric.name: NumberOfAnchors.ContainsImage
metric.summary: The total number of links in the page that are images.

metric.name: NumberOfAnchors.InIframe
metric.summary: The total number of links in the page in any iframe.

metric.name: NumberOfAnchors.SameHost
metric.summary: The total number of links in the page that point to the same origin as the current page's origin.

metric.name: NumberOfAnchors.Total
metric.summary: The total number of links within a page.

metric.name: NumberOfAnchors.URLIncremented
metric.summary: The total number of links on the page, where each link only differs from the current page's URL by a number, and that number is incremented by 1 in the link.

metric.name: TotalClickableSpace
metric.summary: The total clickable space in the entire page, expressed as a ratio of the visible clickable region to the entire viewport region.

metric.name: Viewport.Height
metric.summary: The height of the viewport, in pixels.

metric.name: Viewport.Width
metric.summary: The width of the viewport, in pixels.

event.name: NavigationPredictorPreloadOnHover
event.summary: This event records required data to assess and fine-tune the preloading on-hover and on pointer down heuristics, and is submitted once per mouse over, mouse out, mouse down and mouse up events for randomly selected anchor elements.

metric.name: HoverNotTakenMs
metric.summary: Duration that the user was hovering over the anchor element and it did not result in a navigation to that link. Exponentially bucketed using GetExponentialBucketMin with a spacing of 1.3.

metric.name: HoverTakenMs
metric.summary: Duration that the user was hovering over the anchor element and it resulted in a navigation to that link. Exponentially bucketed using GetExponentialBucketMin with a spacing of 1.3.

metric.name: MouseDownNotTakenMs
metric.summary: Time since the user pressed the left/middle mouse button over the anchor element and that did not result in a navigation to that link. Exponentially bucketed using GetExponentialBucketMin with a spacing of 1.3.

metric.name: MouseDownTakenMs
metric.summary: Time since the user presses the left/middle mouse button over the anchor element and that resulted in a navigation to that link. Exponentially bucketed using GetExponentialBucketMin with a spacing of 1.3.

event.name: NavigationPredictorUserInteractions
event.summary: Metrics that describe user interaction with an anchor element. This event is emitted once per randomly selected anchor elements.

metric.name: AnchorIndex
metric.summary: The index of the anchor element in NavigationPredictor's |top_urls_|.

metric.name: DistanceFromLastPointerDown
metric.summary: Vertical distance from the center of the anchor element to the most recent pointerdown that started a scroll, as a percentage of screen height. This value is between -100 and 100 (inclusive), and is negative when the pointerdown was below the anchor's center (positive otherwise). This will only be set for anchor elements that are in the viewport. Linearly bucketed with a bucket size of 10.

metric.name: EnteredViewportCount
metric.summary: How many times the anchor element entered the viewport. Exponentially bucketed using GetExponentialBucketMin a value of 1.3.

metric.name: IsInViewport
metric.summary: 1 if anchor element is in viewport, otherwise 0.

metric.name: IsPointerHoveringOver
metric.summary: 1 if the pointer is hovering over the anchor element, otherwise 0.

metric.name: MaxEnteredViewportToLeftViewportMs
metric.summary: Maximum time between the moment the anchor element entered the viewport and the moment it left the viewport in milliseconds. Exponentially bucketed using GetExponentialBucketMin a value of 1.3.

metric.name: MaxHoverDwellTimeMs
metric.summary: Maximum dwelltime of pointer hovering over the anchor element in milliseconds. Exponentially bucketed using GetExponentialBucketMin a value of 1.3.

metric.name: MouseAcceleration
metric.summary: Mouse acceleration when the OnHover event triggered. Exponentially bucketed using GetExponentialBucketMin a value of 1.3.

metric.name: MouseVelocity
metric.summary: Mouse velocity when the OnHover event triggered. Exponentially bucketed using GetExponentialBucketMin a value of 1.3.

metric.name: PointerHoveringOverCount
metric.summary: How many times the pointer was hovering over the anchor element. Exponentially bucketed using GetExponentialBucketMin a value of 1.3.

metric.name: VerticalPositionInViewport
metric.summary: Vertical position of the center of the anchor element (y-coordinate of the center), as a percentage of the viewport's height. This value is between 0 and 100 (inclusive). This will only be set for anchor elements that are in the viewport. Linearly bucketed with a bucket size of 10.

event.name: NavigationRequestBreakDown
event.summary: Metrics to measure the main frame navigation timings by tracking NavigationRequest in the browser process. This is recorded at the frequency of 0.001.

metric.name: BeginNavigationToLoaderStart
metric.summary: Measures the duration from BeginNavigation to LoaderStart, in milliseconds.

metric.name: LoaderStartToReceiveResponse
metric.summary: Measures the duration from LoaderStart to ReceiveResponse, in milliseconds.

metric.name: NavigationStartToBeginNavigation
metric.summary: Measures the duration from NavigationStart to BeginNavigation, in milliseconds.

metric.name: ReceiveResponseToCommitNavigation
metric.summary: Measures the duration from ReceiveResponse to CommitNavigation, in milliseconds.

event.name: NavigationThrottleDeferredTime
event.summary: Information recorded about a navigation throttle when a navigation is deferred at any stage of the navigation. Only recorded for main frame navigations that were deferred and later resumed.

metric.name: DurationOfNavigationDeferralMs
metric.summary: The duration from the start of the navigation deferral until the throttle resumes the navigation recorded in milliseconds.

metric.name: NavigationThrottleEventType
metric.summary: An enum representing each event that can cause deferral method (WillFailRequest, WillProcessResponse, WillRedirectRequest, WillStartRequest). See NavigationThrottleRunner::Event for enum details.

metric.name: NavigationThrottleNameHash
metric.summary: A (MD5) hash of the logging name string which represents a unique navigation throttle class (subclass of NavigationThrottle). See NavigationThrottle::GetNameForLogging().

event.name: NavigationTiming
event.summary: Metrics associated with main frame navigation.

metric.name: FinalLoaderCallback
metric.summary: The time relative to navigation start that a callback for the navigation loader is last invoked for the main resource of a main frame navigation.

metric.name: FinalRequestStart
metric.summary: The time relative to navigation start that the final HTTP request is sent for the main resource of a main frame navigation.

metric.name: FinalResponseStart
metric.summary: The time relative to navigation start that the headers of the final HTTP response are received for the main resource of a main frame navigation.

metric.name: FirstLoaderCallback
metric.summary: The time relative to navigation start that a callback for the navigation loader is first invoked for the main resource of a main frame navigation.

metric.name: FirstRequestStart
metric.summary: The time relative to navigation start that the first HTTP request is sent for the main resource of a main frame navigation.

metric.name: FirstResponseStart
metric.summary: The time relative to navigation start that the headers of the first HTTP response are received for the main resource of a main frame navigation.

metric.name: NavigationCommitSent
metric.summary: The time relative to navigation start that navigation commit message for a main frame navigation is sent to a renderer process.

event.name: Network.DataUrls
event.summary: Recorded per resource request when parsing for a data URL is completed.

metric.name: IsImage
metric.summary: If the data URL is for an image resource.

metric.name: ParseTime
metric.summary: The time taken (ms) to parse the data URL. This includes base64 decoding and building the response.

metric.name: UrlLength
metric.summary: The length of the data URL, indicating the size of the resource.

event.name: NewTabPage.HistoryClusters
event.summary: Metrics related to a History Clusters module shown on the New Tab Page. Recorded for each History Clusters module shown on a 1P New Tab Page load, which can be multiple for a page load.

metric.name: BelongsToBoostedCategory
metric.summary: Cluster is of a boosted category.

metric.name: BelongsToMostSeenCategory
metric.summary: Cluster is of the most frequently seen category.

metric.name: BelongsToMostUsedCategory
metric.summary: Cluster is of the most frequently used category.

metric.name: DidDisableModule
metric.summary: Whether the user disabled the module.

metric.name: DidDismissModule
metric.summary: Whether the user dismissed the specific journey shown in this module.

metric.name: DidEngageWithModule
metric.summary: Whether the user engaged with the module.

metric.name: DidMarkAsDone
metric.summary: Whether the user dismissed with positive intent the cluster shown in this module.

metric.name: LayoutTypeShown
metric.summary: The layout type that was shown to the user for this module.

metric.name: MinutesSinceMostRecentVisit
metric.summary: Minutes since the shown cluster's most recent visit, exponentially bucketed using GetExponentialBucketMin with a spacing of 1.3.

metric.name: MostFrequentSeenCategoryCount
metric.summary: The number of times the most frequently seen category by a user occurred in a fixed time period.

metric.name: MostFrequentUsedCategoryCount
metric.summary: The number of times the most frequently used category by a user occurred in a fixed time period.

metric.name: NumAbandonedCarts
metric.summary: Number of abandoned carts associated with the cluster.

metric.name: NumAssociatedCategories
metric.summary: Number of categories associated with the cluster.

metric.name: NumTimesSeenLast24h
metric.summary: Number of times the cluster was seen by the user in the last 24 hours.

metric.name: NumTimesUsedLast24h
metric.summary: Number of times the cluster was used by the user in the last 24 hours.

metric.name: NumTotalVisits
metric.summary: Number of total visits in the same cluster including ones that are not necessarily shown in the module.

metric.name: NumUniqueHosts
metric.summary: Number of unique hosts represented in the cluster.

metric.name: NumVisitsWithImage
metric.summary: Number of visits with an image to show.

event.name: NoStatePrefetch
event.summary: Metrics related to NoStatePrefetch that are recorded using the same UKM IDs as PageLoad.

metric.name: PrefetchedRecently.FinalStatus
metric.summary: Final status of the nostate prefetch if one was recently attempted for either the committed URL of this navigation or for any URL in the redirect chain for the main frame resource for this navigation. page. Recorded as enum PrerenderFinalStatus in //tools/metrics/histograms/enums.xml.

metric.name: PrefetchedRecently.Origin
metric.summary: Triggering origin of the nostate prefetch if one was recently attempted for either the committed URL of this navigation or for any URL in the redirect chain for the main frame resource for this navigation. Recorded as enum PrerenderOrigin in //tools/metrics/histograms/enums.xml.

metric.name: PrefetchedRecently.PrefetchAge
metric.summary: Records the time (in milliseconds) from the start of the nostate prefetch to the time of commit of this navigation. Metric is recorded down to the nearest power of 2. Recorded only if a nostate prefetch was recently attempted for either the committed URL of this navigation or for any URL in the redirect chain for the main frame resource for this navigation.

event.name: Notification
event.summary: A notification event corresponds with the entire lifespan of a single notification, and will be logged to UKM when the notification is deleted from the notification database.

metric.name: ClosedReason
metric.summary: Enum for how the notification was closed. {user, developer, unknown}

metric.name: DidReplaceAnotherNotification
metric.summary: Boolean value representing whether a tag is present and a previously shown notification has been replaced. For UKM logging, we treat a replacement notification as a new notification.

metric.name: DidUserOpenSettings
metric.summary: Boolean value indicating whether the user accessed the Chrome provided settings from the notification.

metric.name: HasBadge
metric.summary: Boolean value representing if a badge URL was included.

metric.name: HasIcon
metric.summary: Boolean value representing if an icon URL was included.

metric.name: HasImage
metric.summary: Boolean value representing if an image URL was included.

metric.name: HasRenotify
metric.summary: Boolean value representing whether default notification indicators (sound, vibration, light) should be played again if the notification is replacing an older notification.

metric.name: HasTag
metric.summary: Boolean value representing if a tag is present.

metric.name: IsSilent
metric.summary: Boolean value representing whether default notification indicators (sound, vibration, light) should be suppressed.

metric.name: NumActionButtonClicks
metric.summary: Number of clicks on developer provided action buttons.

metric.name: NumActions
metric.summary: Number of developer specified actions that can be taken.

metric.name: NumClicks
metric.summary: Number of clicks, excluding action buttons. I.e. clicks on the notification itself.

metric.name: RequireInteraction
metric.summary: Boolean value representing whether the notification should remain onscreen indefinitely, rather than being auto-minimized to the notification center (if allowed by platform).

metric.name: TimeUntilClose
metric.summary: The length of time, in ms, between when the notification was triggered and when it was closed.

metric.name: TimeUntilFirstClick
metric.summary: Time, in ms, between when the notification is shown and the first click.

metric.name: TimeUntilLastClick
metric.summary: Time, in ms, between when the notification is shown and the last click.

event.name: Omnibox.EditUrlSuggestion.Share
event.summary: User pressed 'Share' while viewing edit URL suggestions which shares the current URL.

metric.name: HasOccurred
metric.summary: A boolean signaling that the event has occurred; only records true values.

event.name: Omnibox.SuggestionUsed
event.summary: Recorded when the user uses the omnibox to search or navigate.

metric.name: ResultType
metric.summary: A value that indicates if the user searched or navigated from the omnibox. Used to understand which URLs most commonly lead to searches or navigations from the omnibox.

event.name: OmniboxSecurityIndicator.FormSubmission
event.summary: Recorded when a form is submitted on an HTTPS page. Used to understand any regression introduced by updated omnibox security indicators.

metric.name: Submitted
metric.summary: Placeholder metric, always true.

event.name: OpenerHeuristic.PopupInteraction
event.summary: Recorded when the user first interacts in a pop-up with opener access.

metric.name: OpenerHasSameSiteIframe
metric.summary: "True" iff the opener page contains an iframe whose URL belongs to the same registrable domain as this pop-up. "Unknown" if the opener page is closed or the user navigates its frame to another page before interacting with this pop-up.

metric.name: PopupId
metric.summary: A random integer to identify this pop-up and associate this event with other OpenerHeuristic.* events relating to the same pop-up.

metric.name: SecondsSinceCommitted
metric.summary: The number of seconds since the initial pop-up navigation committed, until the user interacted with the pop-up. Clamped to three minutes at most, and rounded down to the nearest exponential bucket (out of 50 buckets).

metric.name: UrlIndex
metric.summary: The number of URLs visited (including redirects) in the pop-up before the user interacted with it. For example, the initial URL has index 1. If the page redirected once before the user interacted, the index would be 2.

event.name: OpenerHeuristic.PopupPastInteraction
event.summary: Recorded when a pop-up with opener access is initially opened, and the user has previously interacted on the site of the URL passed to window.open().

metric.name: HoursSinceLastInteraction
metric.summary: The number of hours since the user last interacted with the site, until the pop-up was opened. Clamped to 720 hours at most, and rounded down to the nearest exponential bucket (out of 50 buckets).

metric.name: OpenerHasSameSiteIframe
metric.summary: "True" iff the opener page contains an iframe whose URL belongs to the same registrable domain as this pop-up. "Unknown" if the opener page is closed or the user navigates its frame to another page before interacting with this pop-up.

metric.name: PopupId
metric.summary: A random integer to identify this pop-up and associate this event with other OpenerHeuristic.* events relating to the same pop-up.

event.name: OpenerHeuristic.PostPopupCookieAccess
event.summary: Recorded by a top-level page that opens a pop-up with opener access, when the user interacts with that pop-up. Recorded by a top-level page that opens a pop-up with opener access, when the pop-up site later tries to access cookies on the opener site.

metric.name: AccessId
metric.summary: A random integer to identify the top-level site and associate this event with the OpenerHeuristic.TopLevel event that preceded it when the pop-up was opened.

metric.name: AccessSucceeded
metric.summary: Whether the cookie access attempt is allowed, or blocked by a third-party cookie block.

metric.name: HoursSincePopupOpened
metric.summary: The number of hours before the cookie access that the initial OpenerHeuristic.TopLevel event was recorded. Bucketized evenly with 50 buckets up to 60 days.

metric.name: IsAdTagged
metric.summary: Whether the cookie access attempt was tagged as an ad cookie, and thus exempt from mitigations including heuristics.

event.name: OpenerHeuristic.TopLevel
event.summary: Recorded by a top-level page that opens a pop-up with opener access, when the user interacts with that pop-up.

metric.name: AccessId
metric.summary: A random integer to identify the top-level site and associate this event with any OpenerHeuristic.PostPopupCookieAccess events that follow when the pop-up site attempts to access cookies on the top-level site.

metric.name: HasSameSiteIframe
metric.summary: "True" iff the top-level page contains an iframe whose URL belongs to the same registrable domain as the pop-up. "Unknown" if the top-level page is closed or the user navigates the main frame to another page before interacting with the pop-up.

metric.name: IsAdTaggedPopupClick
metric.summary: Whether the popup frame was opened by a click on an ad-tagged frame.

metric.name: PopupId
metric.summary: A random integer to identify the pop-up and associate this event with other OpenerHeuristic.* events relating to the same pop-up.

metric.name: PopupProvider
metric.summary: An enum identifying the JavaScript framework used to open the pop-up, when it can be recognized. Typically an SSO library such as Google Sign-In. "Unknown" when no framework is recognized.

event.name: OptimizationGuide
event.summary: Optimization guide metrics associated with a page load. It is recorded when the optimization guide was consulted in making a decision for the page load. See //components/optimization_guide/.

metric.name: NavigationHintsFetchAttemptStatus
metric.summary: An enum value representing the status of a fetch attempt for the page load and what it contains (i.e. hosts or URLs). Captured at the start of the navigation.

metric.name: NavigationHintsFetchRequestLatency
metric.summary: The duration (in milliseconds) for how long it took from the request to go out to when it was usable on the client. Only recorded if a fetch was initiated for the navigation. Will be recorded as INT64_MAX if the request did not complete successfully.

metric.name: RegisteredOptimizationTargets
metric.summary: A bitmask of the optimization targets that were registered at the start of the page load where each flipped bit corresponds to its int value in |optimization_guide::proto::OptimizationTarget|.

metric.name: RegisteredOptimizationTypes
metric.summary: A bitmask of the optimization types that were registered at the start of the page load where each flipped bit corresponds to its int value in |optimization_guide::proto::OptimizationTarget|.

event.name: PageContentAnnotations2
event.summary: Metrics on the content annotations of the page, one record per page load commit and has an annotation calculated on it. The metrics are recorded in a RAPPOR-style with low-entropy so only aggregate analysis can be performed.

metric.name: VisibilityScore
metric.summary: Records a low entropy respresentation of the visibility score output by a content annotations model. The low entropy value is calculated using a RAPPOR-style calculation to enable analysis only on aggregated results. The raw score is between 0 and 1, scaled to between 0 and 100. The score is then placed into one of 16 linearly spaced buckets by dividing the score by the bucket size and taking the floor. The resulting bucket value has noise added with a probability of .5 where the noise is randomly applied to each individual bit.

event.name: PageDomainInfo
event.summary: Metrics that describe the domain of pages that successfully commit. One event is generated per page load that commits. Currently associated with LocalNetworkRequests UKM metric collection.

metric.name: DomainType
metric.summary: An enum value representing the type of domain of the loaded page. The enum is defined in |LocalNetworkRequestsPageLoadMetricsObserver|. Possible values are 0 for unknown (should never be logged), 1 for pages with public domains, 2 for pages with private domains (IP addresses that are part of a reserved IP space not including localhost), or 4 for localhost.

event.name: PageForegroundSession
event.summary: Total time in foreground in milliseconds, recorded each time the page is backgrounded. May be recorded multiple times for a single page visit.

metric.name: ForegroundDuration
metric.summary: Total time in foreground in milliseconds, recorded each time the page is backgrounded.

metric.name: ForegroundTotalInputDelay
metric.summary: sum(Input Delay), recorded each time the page is backgrounded. The Input Delay refers to the duration between the hardware timestamp and the start of event processing on the main thread for the meaningful input. In ms.

event.name: PageInfoBubble
event.summary: Recorded when an action on the Page Info Bubble (aka Origin Info Bubble or WebsiteSettings Bubble) is taken. May be recorded multiple times for a single page visit.

metric.name: ActionTaken
metric.summary: Records an enum value representing which action on the Page Info Bubble was taken.

event.name: PageLoad
event.summary: Core metrics associated with web page loads.

metric.name: CpuTime
metric.summary: Amount of CPU wall time over the duration of a page load, while the page is in the foreground. Measured in milliseconds.

metric.name: DayOfWeek
metric.summary: The day of the week when the page load was initiated. The time is measured in the local time zone according to the system clock. Only collected for page loads that start while on foreground.

metric.name: DocumentTiming.NavigationToDOMContentLoadedEventFired
metric.summary: Measures the time in milliseconds from navigation timing's navigation start to the time the DOMContentLoaded event is fired, for main frame documents.

metric.name: DocumentTiming.NavigationToLoadEventFired
metric.summary: Measures the time in milliseconds from navigation timing's navigation start to the time the load event is fired, for main frame documents.

metric.name: DurationSinceLastVisitSeconds
metric.summary: The duration since the last visit to this URL in seconds, if the user has visited the URL before. Recorded as -1 if the user has not visited the URL before, or if the History service is unavailable or slow to respond. Any duration that exceeds 30 days will be recorded as 30 days, so in practice, if this duration indicates 30 days, it can be anything from 30 to the maximum duration that local history is stored.

metric.name: Experimental.InputToNavigationStart
metric.summary: Measures the time in milliseconds from when the OS level input event occurred which triggered a navigation, until the navigation was started. Only set if the code can trace back to the original input event. Some known cases where it is missing: crbug.com/889223 crbug.com/893162 crbug.com/897837 crbug.com/897840

metric.name: Experimental.LayoutInstability.CumulativeShiftScoreAtFirstOnHidden
metric.summary: Measures the cumulative layout shift (bit.ly/lsm-explainer) that has occurred on the page (including all subframes) until the first time the page moves from the foreground to the background. This metric's integral value is 100x the fractional cumulative layout shift score described in the explainer. Stable since M98. Log of major changes: http://bit.ly/chrome-speed-metrics-changelog. This metric is trying to record the layout shift values which are not able to be recorded in LayoutInstability.CumulativeShiftScore for some reasons.

metric.name: Experimental.LayoutInstability.MaxCumulativeShiftScoreAtFirstOnHidden.SessionWindow.Gap1000ms.Max5000ms
metric.summary: Measures the maximum cumulative layout shift (bit.ly/cls-explainer) that has occurred in a session window until the first time the page moves from the foreground to the background. The gap between two consecutive shifts in a window is not bigger than 1000ms and the maximum window size is 5000ms. This metric's integral value is 100x the fractional cumulative layout shift score described in the explainer. This metric is trying to record the layout shift values which are not able to be recorded in LayoutInstability.MaxCumulativeShiftScore.SessionWindow.Gap1000ms.Max5000ms for some reasons.

metric.name: Experimental.Navigation.UserInitiated
metric.summary: True if this navigation was initiated by user input. For renderer navigations, only true if the code can trace back to the original input event. Some known cases where it is missing: crbug.com/889223 crbug.com/897837 crbug.com/897840

metric.name: Experimental.TotalForegroundDuration
metric.summary: Re-adding experimental version of TotalForegroundDuration to allow for an analysis of the now obsolete field. This will help inform a discrepency found when switching to the PageTiming.TotalForegroundDuration field.

metric.name: HourOfDay
metric.summary: The hour of the day when the page load was initiated. The time is measured in the local time zone according to the system clock. Only collected for page loads that start while on foreground.

metric.name: InteractiveTiming.FirstInputDelay4
metric.summary: Measures First Input Delay, the duration between the hardware timestamp and the start of event processing on the main thread for the first meaningful input per navigation. See https://goo.gl/tr1oTZ for a detailed explanation. In ms. Log of major changes: http://bit.ly/chrome-speed-metrics-changelog

metric.name: InteractiveTiming.FirstInputTimestamp4
metric.summary: The duration between navigation start and the hardware timestamp of the first meaningful input per navigation. See https://goo.gl/tr1oTZ for a detailed explanation. In ms.

metric.name: InteractiveTiming.FirstScrollDelay
metric.summary: Measures the time, in milliseconds, from when a user starts scrolling until the result shows up on screen if the scroll was the first scroll since navigation.

metric.name: InteractiveTiming.FirstScrollTimestamp
metric.summary: The duration between navigation start and the timestamp of the first scroll per navigation. In ms. This is rounded down to the nearest exponential bucket.

metric.name: InteractiveTiming.INPOffset
metric.summary: The one-based offset of the interaction in the list of all interactions in this page load which was the INP (https://web.dev/inp).

metric.name: InteractiveTiming.INPTime
metric.summary: The time (in milliseconds from navigation start) of the interaction which was the INP (https://web.dev/inp).

metric.name: InteractiveTiming.NumInteractions
metric.summary: The number of distinct user interactions. See definition of user interaction: https://web.dev/better-responsiveness-metric/#group-events-into-interactions

metric.name: InteractiveTiming.UserInteractionLatency.HighPercentile2.MaxEventDuration
metric.summary: An approximation of a high percentile of user interaction latency of a page load, in ms. For this metric, we measure the latency of user interactions by Responsiveness.UserInteraction.MaxEventDuration.

metric.name: InteractiveTiming.UserInteractionLatencyAtFirstOnHidden.HighPercentile2.MaxEventDuration
metric.summary: An approximation of a high percentile of user interaction latency of a page load, in ms. For this metric, we measure the latency of user interactions by Responsiveness.UserInteraction.MaxEventDuration. We record it when a page is moved from the foreground to the background for the first time. This metric is trying to record the INP values which are not able to be recorded in InteractiveTiming.UserInteractionLatency.HighPercentile2.MaxEventDuration for some reasons.

metric.name: InteractiveTiming.WorstUserInteractionLatency.MaxEventDuration
metric.summary: The maximum value of user interaction latency of a page load, in ms. For this metric, we measure the latency of user interactions by Responsiveness.UserInteraction.MaxEventDuration. Each type of interaction has its own budget.

metric.name: InteractiveTimingBeforeSoftNavigation.INPOffset
metric.summary: The one-based offset of the interaction in the list of all interactions in this page load which was the INP before the first soft navigation (https://web.dev/inp).

metric.name: InteractiveTimingBeforeSoftNavigation.INPTime
metric.summary: The time (in milliseconds from navigation starte) of the interaction which was the INP before the first soft navigation (https://web.dev/inp).

metric.name: InteractiveTimingBeforeSoftNavigation.NumInteractions
metric.summary: The number of distinct user interactions before any soft navigation happens. It includes the user interaction that triggers the 1st soft navigtion. See definition of user interaction: https://web.dev/better-responsiveness-metric/#group-events-into-interactions

metric.name: InteractiveTimingBeforeSoftNavigation.UserInteractionLatency.HighPercentile2.MaxEventDuration
metric.summary: An approximation of a high percentile of user interaction latency of a page load before any soft navigation happens, in ms. For this metric, we measure the latency of user interactions by Responsiveness.UserInteraction.MaxEventDuration.

metric.name: IsCrossProcessNavigation
metric.summary: Whether the navigation resulted in the main frame being hosted in a different renderer process than the current document or not.

metric.name: IsExistingBookmark
metric.summary: Marked true if the page was already bookmarked when the navigation committed.

metric.name: IsExistingPartOfTabGroup
metric.summary: Marked true if the page was already part of a tab group when the navigation committed.

metric.name: IsNewBookmark
metric.summary:
Roughly: measures if the page was newly bookmarked. Specifically: this is marked true if the page was not bookmarked when the navigation committed, and is bookmarked at the end of the page lifetime.
Note, this means this boolean is mutually exclusive with IsExistingBookmark.
Also note, this means that a page that is bookmarked, and then sebsequently un-bookmarked is counted as false.

metric.name: IsNTPCustomLink
metric.summary: Marked true if the page has been explicitly added (by the user) to the list of custom links displayed in the NTP. Links added to the NTP by History TopSites don't count for this. Always false on Android, because Android does not have NTP custom links.

metric.name: IsPlacedInTabGroup
metric.summary:
Roughly: measures if the page was added to a tab group. Specifically: this is marked true if the page was not part of a tab group when the navigation committed, and is part of a tab group at the end of the page lifetime.
Note, this means this boolean is mutually exclusive with IsExistingPartOfTabGroup.
Also note, this means that a page that is added, and then sebsequently removed from a tab group is counted as false.

metric.name: IsScopedSearchLikeNavigation
metric.summary: Marked true if the page was eligible for a scoped search when the navigation committed.

metric.name: LayoutInstability.CumulativeShiftScore
metric.summary:
Deprecated as of 04/23. It is still reported, but most users should use PageLoad.LayoutInstability.MaxCumulativeShiftScore.SessionWindow.Gap1000ms.Max5000ms.
Measures the cumulative layout shift (bit.ly/lsm-explainer) that has occurred on the page (including all subframes) during the session. This metric's integral value is 100x the fractional cumulative layout shift score described in the explainer. Stable since M79; previous versions are experimental and subject to fluctuation between releases. Log of major changes: http://bit.ly/chrome-speed-metrics-changelog

metric.name: LayoutInstability.CumulativeShiftScore.BeforeInputOrScroll
metric.summary: Measures the cumulative layout shift (bit.ly/lsm-explainer) that has occurred on the page (including all subframes) during the session, before any user input or scroll (in any frame). This metric's integral value is 100x the fractional cumulative layout shift score described in the explainer.

metric.name: LayoutInstability.CumulativeShiftScore.MainFrame
metric.summary: Measures the cumulative layout shift (bit.ly/lsm-explainer) that has occurred in the main frame during the session. This metric's integral value is 100x the fractional cumulative layout shift score described in the explainer.

metric.name: LayoutInstability.CumulativeShiftScore.MainFrame.BeforeInputOrScroll
metric.summary: Measures the cumulative layout shift (bit.ly/lsm-explainer) that has occurred in the main frame during the session, before any user input or scroll. This metric's integral value is 100x the fractional cumulative layout shift score described in the explainer.

metric.name: LayoutInstability.MaxCumulativeShiftScore.SessionWindow.Gap1000ms.Max5000ms
metric.summary: Measures the maximum cumulative layout shift (bit.ly/cls-explainer) that has occurred in a session window. The gap between two consecutive shifts in a window is not bigger than 1000ms and the maximum window size is 5000ms. This metric's integral value is 100x the fractional cumulative layout shift score described in the explainer.

metric.name: LayoutInstabilityBeforeSoftNavigation.MaxCumulativeShiftScore.MainFrame.SessionWindow.Gap1000ms.Max5000ms
metric.summary: Measures the maximum cumulative layout shift (bit.ly/cls-explainer) that has occurred in a session window before any soft navigation happens. The gap between two consecutive shifts in a window is not bigger than 1000ms and the maximum window size is 5000ms. This metric's integral value is 100x the fractional cumulative layout shift score described in the explainer.

metric.name: MainDocumentSequenceNumber
metric.summary: An id for the main frame's document associated with the current navigation. It doesn't change for same-document navigations and when navigating back to the same document. Unique during the browsing session.

metric.name: MainFrameResource.ConnectDelay
metric.summary: The duration between the start of the connection establishment to the end of the connection establishment to the origin of the main frame request. In ms.

metric.name: MainFrameResource.DNSDelay
metric.summary: The duration between the start of the DNS resolution to the end for the origin of the main frame request. In ms.

metric.name: MainFrameResource.HttpProtocolScheme
metric.summary: HTTP Protocol scheme of the connection using which the main frame response was fetched. Recorded as HttpProtocolScheme enum in tools/metrics/histograms/enum.xml.

metric.name: MainFrameResource.NavigationStartToConnectStart
metric.summary: The duration from the start of the navigation to the start of the connection establishment to the origin of the main frame request. In ms.

metric.name: MainFrameResource.NavigationStartToReceiveHeadersStart
metric.summary: The duration from the start of the navigation to the start of the response headers being received, in ms. The start of the response headers include early hints. This is TTFB in CruX.

metric.name: MainFrameResource.NavigationStartToRequestStart
metric.summary: The duration from the start of the navigation to the start of the main frame request. This includes the time to process any redirects on the main frame request. In ms.

metric.name: MainFrameResource.RedirectCount
metric.summary: The number of main frame redirects that occurred before commit. Unset if there were no redirects.

metric.name: MainFrameResource.RequestHadCookies
metric.summary: A boolean indicating if any mainpage request in the redirect chain or committed request had cookies set on it. Only set when a query result was received from the cookie manager before event recording.

metric.name: MainFrameResource.RequestHasNoStore
metric.summary: A boolean indicating if any mainpage resource has a 'Cache-control: no-store' response header. Recorded only for main frame request.

metric.name: MainFrameResource.RequestStartToReceiveHeadersEnd
metric.summary: The duration from when the request was started to the time when its response headers were received. Recorded only for the main frame request. In ms.

metric.name: MainFrameResource.RequestStartToSendStart
metric.summary: The duration from when the request was started to the time when its request headers were sent. Recorded only for the main frame request. In ms.

metric.name: MainFrameResource.SendStartToReceiveHeadersEnd
metric.summary: The duration from when the request headers were sent to when its response headers were received. Recorded only for the main frame request. In ms.

metric.name: MainFrameResource.SocketReused
metric.summary: True if the socket was reused when establishing connection to the origin of the main frame request.

metric.name: Navigation.InitiatorLocation
metric.summary: page_load_metrics::NavigationHandleUserData::InitiatorLocation enum value. This field is used for identifying the types of prerender trigger.

metric.name: Navigation.PageEndReason3
metric.summary: The |page_load_metrics::PageEndReason| for the main frame navigation of this page load. Replaced Navigation.PageEndReason2 on January 2021 to get rid of END_HIDDEN and use END_APP_BACKGROUND on cases where the metrics are flushed and the app enters the background.

metric.name: Navigation.PageTransition
metric.summary: The |ui::PageTransition| for the main frame navigation of this page load.

metric.name: NavigationEntryOffset
metric.summary: Difference between the last committed navigation entry index before and after the commit of this navigation.

metric.name: Net.CacheBytes2
metric.summary: The total number of body bytes loaded from cache for all resources on the page. This includes the http and memory cache. This is rounded down to the nearest exponential bucket (with a bucket spacing factor of 1.3).

metric.name: Net.DownstreamKbpsEstimate.OnNavigationStart
metric.summary: The downstream throughput (in kilobits per second) for this page load, at the time the navigation for this page load was initiated.

metric.name: Net.EffectiveConnectionType2.OnNavigationStart
metric.summary: The |SystemProfileProto::Network::EffectiveConnectionType| at the time the navigation for this page load was initiated.

metric.name: Net.ErrorCode.OnFailedProvisionalLoad
metric.summary: The |net::Error| code for this page load. Only logged for failed provisional navigations.

metric.name: Net.HttpResponseCode
metric.summary: The HTTP response code (e.g., 200, 404, etc.) associated with the main frame page load.

metric.name: Net.HttpRttEstimate.OnNavigationStart
metric.summary: The HTTP round trip time estimate for this page load, at the time the navigation for this page load was initiated.

metric.name: Net.ImageBytes2
metric.summary: The total number of bytes loaded from the network for images on the page. This is rounded down to the nearest exponential bucket (with a bucket spacing factor of 1.3). On mobile this only records bytes up until the page/app is backgrounded.

metric.name: Net.ImageSubframeBytes2
metric.summary: The total number of bytes loaded from the network for images on the subframes of the page. This is rounded down to the nearest exponential bucket (with a bucket spacing factor of 1.3). On mobile this only records bytes up until the page/app is backgrounded.

metric.name: Net.JavaScriptBytes2
metric.summary: The sum of decoded body lengths of all JavaScript resources that completed loading on the page. This is rounded down to the nearest exponential bucket (with a bucket spacing factor of 1.3). On mobile this only records bytes up until the page/app is backgrounded.

metric.name: Net.JavaScriptMaxBytes2
metric.summary: The max of decoded body lengths of all JavaScript resources that completed loading on the page. This is rounded down to the nearest exponential bucket (with a bucket spacing factor of 1.3). On mobile this only records bytes up until the page/app is backgrounded.

metric.name: Net.MediaBytes2
metric.summary: The total number of bytes loaded from the network for media (video, audio) on the page. This is rounded down to the nearest exponential bucket (with a bucket spacing factor of 1.3). On mobile this only records bytes up until the page/app is backgrounded.

metric.name: Net.NetworkBytes2
metric.summary: The total number of bytes loaded from the network for all resources on the page, including incomplete resources and headers. This is rounded down to the nearest exponential bucket (with a bucket spacing factor of 1.3). On mobile this only records bytes up until the page/app is backgrounded.

metric.name: Net.TransportRttEstimate.OnNavigationStart
metric.summary: The transport round trip time estimate for this page load, at the time the navigation for this page load was initiated.

metric.name: OmniboxUrlCopied
metric.summary: Marked true if the user copied the page's URL from the omnibox at any time for this page load.

metric.name: PageTiming.ForegroundDuration
metric.summary: For page loads that start in the foreground, measures the duration of time in milliseconds until one of the following events occurs: the load of the main resource fails, the page load is stopped, the tab hosting the page is closed, the render process hosting the page goes away, a new navigation which later commits is initiated in the same tab, or the tab hosting the page is backgrounded. This metric has a very long tail, for pages that spend a long period of time in the foreground. As such, use of the mean or tail values are not recommended.

metric.name: PageTiming.NavigationToFailedProvisionalLoad
metric.summary: Measures the time in milliseconds from navigation timing's navigation start to the time the provisional load failed. Only measures provisional loads that failed in the foreground.

metric.name: PageTiming.TotalForegroundDuration
metric.summary: The total amount of time the page spent in the foreground, in milliseconds. Note that this metric adds all foreground durations occurring for the page. For example: if the page starts foregrounded during 1 second, then goes to the background for a while, then is foregrounded again for 2 seconds, and then is unloaded, this metric reports 3 seconds. To see metrics about consecutive foreground sessions, see the PageForegroundSession event instead. This metric is recorded at the end of each page load. As usual with PageLoad metrics, we consider app backgrounding on Android to be the end of page load as well as the app could be evicted by Android anytime after that happens.

metric.name: PageTiming.UserTimingMarkFullyLoaded
metric.summary: When the page UserTiming mark API is called with the standard 'mark_fully_loaded' name to mark that the page is fully loaded. If the mark is called more than once, the last is used.

metric.name: PageTiming.UserTimingMarkFullyVisible
metric.summary: When the page UserTiming mark API is called with the standard 'mark_fully_visible' name to mark that the page is fully visible. If the mark is called more than once, the last is used.

metric.name: PageTiming.UserTimingMarkInteractive
metric.summary: When the page UserTiming mark API is called with the standard 'mark_interactive' name to mark that the page is interactive. If the mark is called more than once, the last is used.

metric.name: PageVisitFinalStatus
metric.summary: Determines the final status for this page load. That is, records whether the page was never foregrounded, was foregrounded but did not reach FCP, or did reach FCP. The metric is recorded at the end of each page load. As usual with PageLoad metrics, we consider app backgrounding on Android to be the end of page load as well as the app could be evicted by Android anytime after that happens.

metric.name: PaintTiming.LargestContentfulPaintBPP
metric.summary: Measures the ratio of image file size (in bits) to displayed pixels for the LCP candidate. Bucketed into roughly exponential intervals to account for the wide range of encountered values (less than 1:100000 to greater than 10000:1). This metric is recorded whenever the LCP candidate is an image, and omitted when it is text.

metric.name: PaintTiming.LargestContentfulPaintImageDiscoveryTime
metric.summary: Measures the time in milliseconds from navigation timing's navigation start to the time when the image LCP element is requested. If the image LCP element is from a subframe, the difference in the navigation start of the main frame and that of the subframe is accounted for. This metric is not recorded when the LCP element is text or video.

metric.name: PaintTiming.LargestContentfulPaintImageIsCrossOrigin
metric.summary: A bool that is set if the LCP element is an image that is cross-origin. This metric is not recorded when the LCP element is not an image, or the LCP element is not in the main frame.

metric.name: PaintTiming.LargestContentfulPaintImageLoadEnd
metric.summary: Measures the time in milliseconds from navigation timing's navigation start to the time when the LCP element(image) finishes loading. For animated images, this is when the image resource finishes loading completely, not the first frame time. See web.dev/optimize-lcp for more details. If the image LCP element is from a subframe, the difference in the navaigation start of the main frame and that of the subframe is accounted for. This metric is not recorded when the LCP element is text or video.

metric.name: PaintTiming.LargestContentfulPaintImageLoadStart
metric.summary: Measures the time in milliseconds from navigation timing's navigation start to the time when the LCP element(image) starts loading. See web.dev/optimize-lcp for more details. If the image LCP element is from a subframe, the difference in the navaigation start of the main frame and that of the subframe is accounted for. This metric is not recorded when the LCP element is text or video.

metric.name: PaintTiming.LargestContentfulPaintRequestPriority
metric.summary: Resource loading priority used for the LCP candidate. This metric is recorded whenever the LCP candidate is an image, and omitted when it is text or video.

metric.name: PaintTiming.LargestContentfulPaintType
metric.summary: Indicates that type of the LargestContentfulPaint candidate. This value is a packed set of flags, defined in third_party/blink/public/common/performance/largest_contentful_paint_type.h

metric.name: PaintTiming.NavigationToFirstContentfulPaint
metric.summary: Measures the time in milliseconds from navigation timing's navigation start to the time when the page first paints content. A contentful paint includes a paint of text, image, SVG, or canvas. Log of major changes: http://bit.ly/chrome-speed-metrics-changelog

metric.name: PaintTiming.NavigationToFirstPaint
metric.summary: Measures the time in milliseconds from navigation timing's navigation start to the time the first paint is performed.

metric.name: PaintTiming.NavigationToLargestContentfulPaint2
metric.summary: Measures the time in milliseconds from navigation timing's navigation start to the time when the page first paints the largest content (text or image) within viewport, across all frames. See http://bit.ly/fcp_plus_plus for more details. Log of major changes: http://bit.ly/chrome-speed-metrics-changelog. Version 2 differs from the first version in that it includes content that has been removed from the page.

metric.name: PaintTiming.NavigationToLargestContentfulPaint2.CrossSiteSubFrame
metric.summary: Similar to PaintTiming.NavigationToLargestContentfulPaint2, this measures the time in milliseconds from the navigation start of the top-level frame to the time when the page first paints the largest content (text or image) in the cross-site subframe which is the top candidate of largest contentful paint. This metric is computed separately from the page-level LCP, it's recorded even in page loads where the page-level LCP is not that cross-site LCP.

metric.name: PaintTiming.NavigationToLargestContentfulPaint2.MainFrame
metric.summary: Measures the time in milliseconds from navigation timing's navigation start to the time when the page first paints the largest content (text or image) within viewport, in the main frame. Includes content that has been removed from the page. See http://bit.ly/fcp_plus_plus for more details.

metric.name: PaintTiming.NavigationToLargestContentfulPaint2AtFirstOnHidden
metric.summary: Measures the time in milliseconds from navigation timing's navigation start to the time when the page first paints the largest content (text or image) within viewport, across all frames. Recorded at the point when a page which has been opened in the foreground is moved to the background for the first time. See http://bit.ly/fcp_plus_plus for more details. Log of major changes: http://bit.ly/chrome-speed-metrics-changelog. This metric is trying to record the LCP values which are not able to be recorded in PaintTiming.NavigationToLargestContentfulPaint2 for some reasons.

metric.name: ParseTiming.NavigationToParseStart
metric.summary: Measures the time in milliseconds from navigation timing's navigation start to the time the parser started, for main frame documents.

metric.name: RefreshRateThrottled
metric.summary: Records whether the refresh rate is throttled (capped to 30Hz) by Power Saver Mode for this Page Load, when the navigation starts. It is possible but very unlikely for this to change mid navigation, for instance due to a change by the user in settings or the battery being recharged above 20%.

metric.name: SiteEngagementScore
metric.summary: Measures the Site Engagement Score for the committed URL rounded to the nearest 10.

metric.name: SiteInstanceRenderProcessAssignment
metric.summary: Records how the main frame's site instance was assigned to a renderer process.

metric.name: SoftNavigationCount
metric.summary:
Number of soft navigations detected by heuristics. See https://bit.ly/soft-navigation
Soft navigations are JS-driven same-document navigations that are using the history API or the new Navigation API, triggered by a user gesture and meaningfully modify the DOM, replacing the previous content with new one.

metric.name: ThirdPartyCookieBlockingEnabledForSite
metric.summary: Records whether third party cookie blocking was enabled for a site. Only recorded for users who enabled the cookie controls ui.

metric.name: WasCached
metric.summary: Set to 1 if the main resource was served from cache.

metric.name: WasDiscarded
metric.summary: Records whether the Page Load is the result of the page having previously been discarded because of performance interventions or critical memory pressure.

event.name: PageLoad.FromGoogleSearch
event.summary: Recorded for page loads that were navigated to via Google Search.

metric.name: GoogleSearchMode
metric.summary: Google Search mode used by the user. This corresponds to the tab (e.g. web result, image results, video results, etc.) the user is on. This information is extracted from the tbm query parameter on the Search URL.

event.name: PageLoad.Internal
event.summary: Debugging metrics for the PageLoad event that do not require the same amount of sampling.

metric.name: PaintTiming.LargestContentfulPaint.ContentType
metric.summary: Records whether the page's largest contentful paint, whose timestamp is measured by PageLoad.PaintTiming.NavigationToLargestContentfulPaint2, comes from text or image. This value is sampled and not on the PageLoad event, so it is not always recorded whenever PageLoad.PaintTiming.NavigationToLargestContentfulPaint2 is recorded.

metric.name: PaintTiming.LargestContentfulPaint.TerminationState
metric.summary: Records the state of the largest contentful paint algorithm at the time in which the browser attempts to record PageLoad.PaintTiming.NavigationToLargestContentfulPaint2. This state depends on whether there was a largest contentful paint candidate or not and the foreground state of the page. This value is sampled and not on the PageLoad event, so it is not always recorded whenever the browser attempts to record PageLoad.PaintTiming.NavigationToLargestContentfulPaint2.

event.name: PageLoad.PervasivePayloadsEnabled
event.summary: Recorded for page loads where the pervasive payloads list is inputted to Chrome.

event.name: PageLoad.ServiceWorkerControlled
event.summary: Recorded for page loads controlled by a service worker.

event.name: PageLoad.SignedExchange
event.summary: Recorded for page loads which main resource is an inner response of a signed exchange.

metric.name: ServedFromGoogleCache
metric.summary: Set to 1 if the main resource was served from Google's cache server.

event.name: PageLoadInitiatorForAdTagging
event.summary: Metrics about the initiator context of a page load (e.g. whether certain condition was true and/or what triggered the page load). Recorded when a page commits. The metrics are specific to ad related conditions or purposes.

metric.name: FromAdClick
metric.summary: Whether the page was opened due to a click on an ad.

metric.name: FromUser
metric.summary: Whether the navigation occurred due to a user action. This includes omnibox navigations as well as renderer initiated navigations with user activation.

event.name: PageWithPassword
event.summary: Metrics about websites that contain password forms and the user's interactions with them. No events are created for pages that don't contain password forms.

metric.name: FormManagerAvailable
metric.summary: Records whether Chrome was sufficiently aware of forms during various stages of saving a credential on the site, or whether saving was aborted due to absence of the corresponding form manager. Values correspond to the enum PasswordManagerMetricsRecorder::FormManagerAvailable.

metric.name: PageLevelUserAction
metric.summary: Records if the user interacts with the password manager in a way that cannot (reliably) be attributed to a specific PasswordFormManager. Values correspond to the enum PasswordManagerMetricsRecorder::PageLevelUserAction.

metric.name: ProvisionalSaveFailure
metric.summary: Records a provisional save failure in case the password manager cannot offer to save a credential. Recorded values correspond to the enum PasswordManagerMetricsRecorder::ProvisionalSaveFailure.

metric.name: UserModifiedPasswordField
metric.summary: Records a 1 for every page on which a user has modified a password text field - regardless of how many password fields a page contains or the user modifies.

event.name: PaintPreviewCapture
event.summary: Metrics related to the performance of capturing Paint Previews.

metric.name: BlinkCaptureTime
metric.summary: The amount of time spent painting in Blink to record a Paint Preview of the main frame.

metric.name: CompressedOnDiskSize
metric.summary: The compressed on-disk size in KB of the captured artifacts. This is rounded down to the nearest exponential bucket (with a bucket ratio of 1.3).

event.name: PartitionedCookiePresentV2
event.summary: Recorded when a page has a partitioned cookie present and the cookie's name is not receive-cookie-deprecation.

metric.name: PartitionedCookiePresentV2
metric.summary: Records '1' if one or more partitioned cookies are present. This metric is only logged for sites that have partitioned cookies.

event.name: PasswordForm
event.summary: Metrics about password forms on websites and the user's interactions with them. A separate event is generated for each password form discovered on a site.

metric.name: Context.FormSignature
metric.summary: Records a low entropy hash of the form signature in order to be able to distinguish two forms on the same site.

metric.name: DynamicFormChanges
metric.summary: Records a bitmask of password form changes after the first time this form is seen by Password Manager. Recorded once per a form when the form is removed from DOM. Bit 0 - changes in number of fields, bit 1 - changes in fields renderer ids, bit 2 - changes in autocomplete attributes, bit 3 - changes in form control types, bit 4 - changes in field names.

metric.name: Fill.FirstFillingResultInRenderer
metric.summary:
Records whether the PasswordAutofillAgent in the renderer manages to fill credentials as instructed by the browser or records a failure reason otherwise. Only the outcome of the first attempt to fill is recorded.
This is replicated as PasswordManager.FirstFillingResultInRenderer in UMA.

metric.name: Fill.FirstWaitForUsernameReason
metric.summary:
This metric records why the browser instructs the renderer not to fill the credentials on page load but to wait for the user to confirm the credential to be filled. Only the value of the first decision to fill is recorded.
This is replicated as PasswordManager.FirstWaitForUsernameReason in UMA.

metric.name: Generation.GeneratedPassword
metric.summary: Records '1' if the user has generated a password on this form.

metric.name: Generation.GeneratedPasswordModified
metric.summary: Records '1' if the user has modified a generated a password on this form.

metric.name: Generation.PopupShown
metric.summary: Records an entry if (and only if) a popup was shown.

metric.name: ManagerFill.Action
metric.summary: Records for each password form (and HTTP auth), whether the password manager has filled/provided credentials. Recorded values correspond to the enum ManagerAutofillEvent.

metric.name: ManagerFill.Assistance
metric.summary:
Records for each successful submission of a password form, the degree to which the user has been assisted by the password manager.
This is replicated as PasswordManager.FillingAssistance in UMA.

metric.name: ManagerFill.AssistanceForSingleUsername
metric.summary:
Records for each successful submission of a single username form, the degree to which the user has been assisted by the password manager.
This is replicated as PasswordManager.FillingAssistanceForSingleUsername in UMA.

metric.name: ParsingDiffFillingAndSaving
metric.summary: Records comparison of username and password fields picked during parsing when the form is filled and when the form is saved. Recorded only if the form is submitted successfully, on corresponding form manager destruction.

metric.name: ReadonlyWhenFilling
metric.summary: Records the readonly status encoded with parsing success after parsing for filling. The |value| is constructed as follows: The least significant bit says whether parsing succeeded (1) or not (0). The rest, shifted by one bit to the right is the FormDataParser::ReadonlyPasswordFields representation of the readonly status.

metric.name: ReadonlyWhenSaving
metric.summary: Records the readonly status encoded with parsing success after parsing for creating pending credentials. The |value| is constructed the same way as for ReadonlyWhenFilling.

metric.name: Saving.Prompt.Interaction
metric.summary: Records how the user interacted with a saving prompt.

metric.name: Saving.Prompt.Shown
metric.summary: Records, for each password form seen by the password manager, whether the user was shown a prompt that asked for permission to save new credentials. In case a store() via the Credential Management API, a virtual password form is created, for which this metric is recorded.

metric.name: Saving.Prompt.Trigger
metric.summary: Records the trigger of each password save bubble that was shown to the user to ask for permission to save new credentials.

metric.name: Saving.ShowedManualFallbackForSaving
metric.summary: Records if a manual fallback for saving option was shown to the user (on Android this is recorded, even though the UI does not exist at the moment). This is a bit mask. 1 means a prompt was shown, 2 means the prompt was due to a generated password, 4 means the prompt was due to an update.

metric.name: Submission.Indicator
metric.summary: Records the event that led the password manager to believe the submission was successful. Metrics is recorded only when the password is saved.

metric.name: Submission.Observed
metric.summary: Records whether a submission of a password form has been observered. The values 0 and 1 correspond to false and true respectively.

metric.name: Submission.SubmissionResult
metric.summary: Records the outcome of a password form submission.

metric.name: Submission.SubmissionResult.GeneratedPassword
metric.summary: Records the outcome of a password form submission for forms on which users generated passwords.

metric.name: Submission.SubmittedFormType2
metric.summary:
Records the presumed type (e.g. signup, login, change password) of a submitted password form.
This is replicated as PasswordManager.SubmittedFormType2 in UMA.

metric.name: Updating.Prompt.Interaction
metric.summary: Records how the user interacted with an updating prompt.

metric.name: Updating.Prompt.Shown
metric.summary: Records, for each password form seen by the password manager, whether the user was shown a prompt that asked for permission to update existing credentials.

metric.name: Updating.Prompt.Trigger
metric.summary: Records the trigger of each password update bubble that was shown to the user to ask for permission to update existing credentials.

metric.name: User.Action.CorrectedUsernameInForm
metric.summary: Counts how many times the user corrected a username in a password form. This is only populated if events happened.

metric.name: User.Action.EditedUsernameInBubble
metric.summary: Counts how many times the user edited a username in a password save/update bubble. This is only populated if events happened.

metric.name: User.Action.SelectedDifferentPasswordInBubble
metric.summary: Counts how many times the user selected a different password in a save/update bubble. This is only populated if events happened.

metric.name: User.Action.TriggeredManualFallbackForSaving
metric.summary: Counts how many times the user triggered the manual fallback for password saving. This is only populated if events happened.

event.name: PasswordManager.FirstCCTPageLoad
event.summary: Logged on Android for the first page that a Chrome Custom Tab loads. Recorded when the primary page changes away from the first loaded one or when the WebContents is destroyed.

metric.name: HasPasswordForm
metric.summary: True if there was a password form detected on the page.

event.name: PasswordManager.LeakWarningDialog
event.summary: Metrics related to leak warnings shown on login and password reset. Leak warning dialogs in Chrome aim to inform users about leaked credentials and advise to change them. We collect type of the dialog that is shown and the response of the user to it.

metric.name: PasswordLeakDetectionDialogDismissalReason
metric.summary: Records the response to the password leak dialog.

metric.name: PasswordLeakDetectionDialogType
metric.summary: Records the type of the password leak dialog.

event.name: PasswordManager.NewlySavedPassword
event.summary: Metrics for newly saved passwords, recorded at the moment of saving.

metric.name: HasEmptyUsername
metric.summary: Whether the password has an empty username.

metric.name: IsPasswordGenerated
metric.summary: Whether the password was generated by Chrome or typed manually by the user.

event.name: PasswordManager.PasswordChangeTriggered
event.summary: Metrics related to password change. The Password Checkup in Chrome Settings aims to help users find their leaked passwords. After getting list of compromised passwords, user can trigger password change.

metric.name: PasswordChangeType
metric.summary: Records what type of password change the user triggers.

event.name: PasswordManager.PasswordFillingIOS
event.summary: Records password filling for IOS. The metric records whether the password filling is successful upon suggestion click.

metric.name: FillingSuccess
metric.summary: A boolean indicating success or failure.
