event.name: PasswordManager.WellKnownChangePasswordResult
event.summary: Metrics related to the usage and support of .well-known/change-password. The .well-known/change-password standard is defined to redirect to the sites change password form. We collect whether a site supports the standard or not. https://wicg.github.io/change-password-url/

metric.name: WellKnownChangePasswordResult
metric.summary: Records if a site supports .well-known/change-password and if a url override was used or not.

event.name: PaymentApp.CheckoutEvents
event.summary: Records checkout events as well as completion status for the invoked payment app when its origin is shown inside the payment handler modal window. This metric uses the specific PAYMENT_APP_ID ukm_source_id which is generated from the app's origin.

metric.name: CompletionStatus
metric.summary: How the Payment Request ended. This metric will only get recorded if a non-autofill payment app gets invoked to handle the request.

metric.name: Events2
metric.summary: The 2.0 version of Events. Bitfield representing the events that occurred during the Payment Request. This metric will only get recorded if a non-autofill payment app gets invoked to handle the request. Values defined in the Event2 enum of components/payments/core/journey_logger.h.

metric.name: CompletionStatus
metric.summary: How the Payment Request ended. Values defined in the CompletionStatus enum of components/payments/core/journey_logger.h.

metric.name: Events2
metric.summary: The 2.0 version of Events. Bitfield representing the events that occurred during the Payment Request. Values defined in the Event2 enum of components/payments/core/journey_logger.h.

metric.name: Category
metric.summary: Tranaction size can be zero, micro, or regular. Values defined in the TransactionSize enum of components/payments/core/journey_logger.h.

metric.name: CompletionStatus
metric.summary: Whether the transaction has been triggered or completed.

event.name: Pepper.Broker
event.summary: Event recorded when a flash instance connects to the broker, resulting in a channel being opened to the broker process.

event.name: PerformanceAPI.LongAnimationFrame
event.summary: This event is associated with the LongAnimationFrame Web Platform API. The metrics are recorded when a long animation frame is detected and reported to the page. For more details see https://crbug.com/1392685.

metric.name: Categorized3PScriptLongAnimationFrameCallbackContributors
metric.summary: A bitfield representation of third party technologies who have contributed at least one event/callback script that blocked the long animation frame for more than 5ms. The bitwise fields are defined by ThirdPartyTechnology enum in third_party/blink_renderer/core/timing/third_party_script_info.h.

metric.name: Categorized3PScriptLongAnimationFrameScriptExecutionContributors
metric.summary: A bitfield representation of third party technologies who have contributed at least one script execution block that blocked the long animation frame for more than 5ms. The bitwise fields are defined by ThirdPartyTechnology enum in third_party/blink_renderer/core/timing/third_party_script_info.h.

metric.name: DidPause
metric.summary: True if there was a pause (sync XHR, dialog) during the long frame.

metric.name: Duration.DelayDefer
metric.summary: A positive or negative duration in milliseconds, representing the time between the desired time for rendering the frame and rendering it in practice. Positive means delayed, negative means deferred.

metric.name: Duration.EffectiveBlocking
metric.summary: The duration in milliseconds, representing the time the LoAF was blocking.

metric.name: Duration.LongScript.JSCompilation
metric.summary: The total duration of long-script JS compilation, in milliseconds.

metric.name: Duration.LongScript.JSExecution
metric.summary: The total duration of long-script JS execution, not including compilation, in milliseconds.

metric.name: Duration.LongScript.JSExecution.EventListeners
metric.summary: The total duration spent in event listeners (e.g. onclick).

metric.name: Duration.LongScript.JSExecution.PromiseHandlers
metric.summary: The total duration spent in promise resolve/reject tasks.

metric.name: Duration.LongScript.JSExecution.ScriptBlocks
metric.summary: The total duration spent in script blocks (script element, import)

metric.name: Duration.LongScript.JSExecution.UserCallbacks
metric.summary: The total duration spent in user callbacks (e.g. requestAnimationFrame).

metric.name: Duration.StyleAndLayout.Forced
metric.summary: The total duration of forced style and layout in long scripts.

metric.name: Duration.StyleAndLayout.RenderPhase
metric.summary: The total duration of the style and layout lifecycle phase, not including forced style and layout.

metric.name: Duration.Total
metric.summary: The duration of the long animation frame in milliseconds. Mirrors the value reported in PerformanceEntry.duration.

event.name: PerformanceAPI.LongTask
event.summary: This event is associated with the LongTask Web Platform API. The metrics are recorded when a long task is detected and reported to the page. For more details see the design doc linked in https://crbug.com/1173527.

metric.name: Duration
metric.summary: The duration of the long task in microseconds. Mirrors the value reported in PerformanceEntry.duration.

metric.name: Duration.V8.Execute
metric.summary: The total time in microseconds that is V8 spent in main-thread V8, including JS-time, but without time spent in callbacks to blink.

metric.name: Duration.V8.GC
metric.summary: The total time in microseconds that V8 spent collecting garbage within the long task.

metric.name: Duration.V8.GC.Full.Atomic
metric.summary: The total time in microseconds that V8 spent in the atomic pause of the full garbage collector within the long task.

metric.name: Duration.V8.GC.Full.Incremental
metric.summary: The total time in microseconds that V8 spent in the incremental pause of the full garbage collector within the long task.

metric.name: Duration.V8.GC.Young
metric.summary: The total time in microseconds that V8 spent in the young garbage collector within the long task.

metric.name: StartTime
metric.summary: The duration in millseconds between the document navigation start and the long task start. Mirrors the value reported in PerformanceEntry.startTime.

event.name: PerformanceAPI.Memory
event.summary: This event is associated with the performance.measureUserAgentSpecificMemory API. The metrics are recorded when the memory measurement is reported to the page. All values represent memory consumption in KB.

metric.name: Dom
metric.summary: The total DOM memory measured in KB.

metric.name: JavaScript
metric.summary: The total JavaScript memory measured in KB.

metric.name: JavaScript.DedicatedWorker
metric.summary: The total JavaScript memory in the DedicatedWorker scope measured in KB.

metric.name: Shared
metric.summary: The total shared memory measured in KB.

event.name: PerformanceAPI.Memory.Legacy
event.summary: This event is associated with the performance.memory API. The metrics are recorded when the memory measurement is reported to the page. All values represent memory consumption in KB.

metric.name: JavaScript
metric.summary: The JavaScript memory (usedJSHeapSize) measured in KB.

event.name: PerformanceManager.PageResourceUsage2
event.summary: Metrics detailing the estimated resources used by pages. The resources of a renderer process are allocated to all pages hosted in that process using the algorithm in performance_manager::ProcessMetricsDecorator. An event is collected for each open page every 2 minutes, which is the same frequency as the PerformanceMonitor.AverageCPU8.* histograms.

metric.name: BackgroundState
metric.summary: Whether the page was in the foreground, background, or mixed during the last 2 minutes.

metric.name: MeasurementAlgorithm
metric.summary: The algorithm used to estimate the resource usage of the page. If this is missing, the Legacy algorithm was used.

metric.name: PrivateFootprintEstimate
metric.summary: The estimated private footprint of the page, in kilobytes.

metric.name: RecentCPUUsage
metric.summary: The estimated CPU usage of the page over the last 2 minutes, with 10,000 as 100% usage of 1 full core. (The same scale as the PerformanceMonitor.AverageCPU8.* histograms.)

metric.name: ResidentSetSizeEstimate
metric.summary: The estimated resident set size of the page, in kilobytes.

metric.name: TotalRecentCPUUsageAllPages
metric.summary: The sum of the estimated CPU usage of all pages over the last 2 minutes, with 10,000 as 100% usage of 1 full core. (The same scale as the PerformanceMonitor.AverageCPU8.* histograms.) RecentCPUUsage can be compared to this to find how the page compares to other open tabs.

event.name: PeriodicBackgroundSyncEventCompleted
event.summary: A website can register to periodically update itself in the background using a service worker. Updates can be done in response to the periodicsync event. Logged when a periodicsync event completes and returns a status. We collect information about retries of this event.

metric.name: MaxAttempts
metric.summary: Integer value of the maximum number of allowed retries.

metric.name: NumAttempts
metric.summary: Integer value of the number of retries / sync events dispatched.

metric.name: Status
metric.summary: The enum value of the completion status, defined in blink::ServiceWorkerStatusCode.

event.name: PeriodicBackgroundSyncRegistered
event.summary: A website can register to periodically update itself in the background using a service worker. Updates can be done in response to the periodicsync event after a periodic Background Sync registration has been created. Logged when a periodic Background Sync registration is created. We collect information about this registration request.

metric.name: IsReregistered
metric.summary: Boolean for whether a registration with this tag has already been created.

metric.name: MinIntervalMs
metric.summary: Integer value of the minimum interval, in milliseconds, of periodicsync events requested by the origin for this registration. This number is exponentially bucketed and uses the UKM GetExponentialBucketMin method with a value of 2.0 for spacing.

event.name: Permission
event.summary: Metrics for a user's permission actions, logged when a permission prompt is resolved or when a permission is revoked. Grouped (microphone+camera) permission prompts are reported separately.

metric.name: Action
metric.summary: An enum of type PermissionAction. One of GRANTED, DENIED, DISMISSED or IGNORED.

metric.name: Gesture
metric.summary: An enum of type PermissionRequestGestureType, whether the prompt was initiated by a user gesture.

metric.name: OsPromptScreen
metric.summary: An enum of type ElementAnchoredBubbleVariant. The screen that prompts the user for the OS screen, if applicable.

metric.name: OsSystemSettingsScreen
metric.summary: An enum of type ElementAnchoredBubbleVariant. The screen that directs the user to the OS Settings, if applicable.

metric.name: PermissionAutoRevocationHistory
metric.summary: An enum of type PermissionAutoRevocationHistory, representing whether the permission has been automatically revoked in the past at least once.

metric.name: PermissionType
metric.summary: An enum of type ContentSettingsType.

metric.name: PredictionsApiResponse.GrantLikelihood
metric.summary: An enum of type PermissionUmaUtil::PredictionGrantLikelihood describing the likelihood returned by the Web Permission Predictions Service, if the service was successfully queried.

metric.name: PredictionsApiResponse.Heldback
metric.summary: A boolean that records whether the response by the permissions prediction service was held back.

metric.name: PriorDismissals
metric.summary: The number of dismissed prompts for the given (origin, permission) pair, capped at 10 dismissals.

metric.name: PriorIgnores
metric.summary: The number of ignored prompts for the given (origin, permission) pair, capped at 10 ignores.

metric.name: PromptDisposition
metric.summary: An enum of type PermissionPromptDisposition. The UI used to display a permission prompt, if any.

metric.name: PromptDispositionReason
metric.summary: An enum of type PermissionPromptDispositionReason. The reason why the particular prompt disposition was used, if any.

metric.name: SatisfiedAdaptiveTriggers
metric.summary: A bitmask consisting of flags defined in enum type AdaptiveTriggers. A bit is set if the corresponding adaptive triggering condition has been satisfied at least once in the past for the user. This metric is not recorded for users who don't have the activation dry run enabled.

metric.name: SiteLevelScreen
metric.summary: An enum of type ElementAnchoredBubbleVariant. The site level screen shown to the user, if any.

metric.name: Source
metric.summary: An enum of type PermissionSourceUI. The UI surface for this action.

metric.name: Stats.AllPrompts.Count
metric.summary: Total number of prompts shown to the user in the last 28 days. The count is bucketed. The bucket minimums being 20, 15, 12, 10, 9, 8, 7, 6, 5, 4 and 0. The correct bucket is the first one that is smaller or equal to the prompt count.

metric.name: Stats.AllPrompts.DenyRate
metric.summary: What percentage of prompts from the last 28 days did the user deny. Aggregated across all origins, all PermissionTypes and all PromptDisposition. Rounded to the nearest 10%.

metric.name: Stats.AllPrompts.DismissRate
metric.summary: What percentage of prompts from the last 28 days did the user dismiss. Aggregated across all origins, all PermissionTypes and all PromptDisposition. Rounded to the nearest 10%.

metric.name: Stats.AllPrompts.GrantRate
metric.summary: What percentage of prompts from the last 28 days did the user grant. Aggregated across all origins, all PermissionTypes and all PromptDisposition. Rounded to the nearest 10%.

metric.name: Stats.AllPrompts.IgnoreRate
metric.summary: What percentage of prompts from the last 28 days did the user ignore. Aggregated across all origins, all PermissionTypes and all PromptDisposition. Rounded to the nearest 10%.

metric.name: Stats.AllPromptsOfType.Count
metric.summary: Total number of prompts shown to the user in the last 28 days for the currently requested permission type. The count is bucketed. The bucket minimums being 20, 15, 12, 10, 9, 8, 7, 6, 5, 4 and 0. The correct bucket is the first one that is smaller or equal to the prompt count.

metric.name: Stats.AllPromptsOfType.DenyRate
metric.summary: What percentage of prompts from the last 28 days did the user deny. Aggregated across all origins, the current requested PermissionType and all PromptDisposition. Rounded to the nearest 10%.

metric.name: Stats.AllPromptsOfType.DismissRate
metric.summary: What percentage of prompts from the last 28 days did the user dismiss. Aggregated across all origins, the current requested PermissionType and all PromptDisposition. Rounded to the nearest 10%.

metric.name: Stats.AllPromptsOfType.GrantRate
metric.summary: What percentage of prompts from the last 28 days did the user grant. Aggregated across all origins, the current requested PermissionType and all PromptDisposition. Rounded to the nearest 10%.

metric.name: Stats.AllPromptsOfType.IgnoreRate
metric.summary: What percentage of prompts from the last 28 days did the user ignore. Aggregated across all origins, the current requested PermissionType and all PromptDisposition. Rounded to the nearest 10%.

metric.name: Stats.LoudPrompts.Count
metric.summary: Total number of loud UI prompts shown to the user in the last 28 days. The count is bucketed. The bucket minimums being 20, 15, 12, 10, 9, 8, 7, 6, 5 ,4 and 0. The correct bucket is the first one that is smaller or equal to the prompt count.

metric.name: Stats.LoudPrompts.DenyRate
metric.summary: What percentage of prompts from the last 28 days did the user deny. Aggregated across all origins, all PermissionTypes and loud UI PromptDispositions only. Rounded to the nearest 10%.

metric.name: Stats.LoudPrompts.DismissRate
metric.summary: What percentage of prompts from the last 28 days did the user dismiss. Aggregated across all origins, all PermissionTypes and loud UI PromptDispositions only. Rounded to the nearest 10%.

metric.name: Stats.LoudPrompts.GrantRate
metric.summary: What percentage of prompts from the last 28 days did the user grant. Aggregated across all origins, all PermissionTypes and loud UI PromptDispositions only. Rounded to the nearest 10%.

metric.name: Stats.LoudPrompts.IgnoreRate
metric.summary: What percentage of prompts from the last 28 days did the user ignore. Aggregated across all origins, all PermissionTypes and loud UI PromptDispositions only. Rounded to the nearest 10%.

metric.name: Stats.LoudPromptsOfType.Count
metric.summary: Total number of loud UI prompts shown to the user in the last 28 days for the currently requested permission type. The count is bucketed. The bucket minimums being 20, 15, 12, 10, 9, 8, 7, 6, 5, 4 and 0. The correct bucket is the first one that is smaller or equal to the prompt count.

metric.name: Stats.LoudPromptsOfType.DenyRate
metric.summary: What percentage of prompts from the last 28 days did the user deny. Aggregated across all origins, currently requested PermissionType and loud UI PromptDispositions only. Rounded to the nearest 10%.

metric.name: Stats.LoudPromptsOfType.DismissRate
metric.summary: What percentage of prompts from the last 28 days did the user dismiss. Aggregated across all origins, currently requested PermissionType and loud UI PromptDispositions only. Rounded to the nearest 10%.

metric.name: Stats.LoudPromptsOfType.GrantRate
metric.summary: What percentage of prompts from the last 28 days did the user grant. Aggregated across all origins, currently requested PermissionType and loud UI PromptDispositions only. Rounded to the nearest 10%.

metric.name: Stats.LoudPromptsOfType.IgnoreRate
metric.summary: What percentage of prompts from the last 28 days did the user ignore. Aggregated across all origins, currently requested PermissionType and loud UI PromptDispositions only. Rounded to the nearest 10%.

metric.name: TimeToDecision
metric.summary: Time (in milliseconds) taken by the user to make a decision on a permission prompt after it was displayed, if at all. Ignoring a prompt doesn't count as a decision.

event.name: Permissions.EmbeddedPromptAction
event.summary: Metrics for a user's permission actions for permission prompts triggered by the user clicking on the Embedded Permission Element, logged when a permission prompt is resolved.

metric.name: Action
metric.summary: An enum of type ElementAnchoredBubbleAction. One of GRANTED, GRANTED_ONCE, OK, DENIED, DISMISSED, DISMISSED_SCRIM, SYSTEM_SETTINGS or IGNORED.

metric.name: PermissionType
metric.summary: Represents the permission type that the prompt is for. An enum of type ContentSettingsType.

metric.name: PreviousScreens
metric.summary: Represents how many screens have already been shown to the user prior to the current one as part of the permission prompt. Also serves as an order number. Should never be bigger than the number of screen variants. It is recorded as int64_t (upper bound 10).

metric.name: ScreenPermissionType
metric.summary: Represents the permission type that current screen is for. This can sometimes differ from PermissionType when the PermissionType is Multiple, as some screens are designed to only be shown for one permission at a time. An enum of type ContentSettingsType.

metric.name: Variant
metric.summary: An enum of type ElementAnchoredBubbleVariant. The screen that the user has currently taken action on.

event.name: PermissionUsage
event.summary: Metrics for usage of capabilities gated behind permission prompts. One event is recorded per permission type for each page load that uses that permission type.

metric.name: PermissionType
metric.summary: An enum of type ContentSettingsType.

event.name: Plugins.FlashInstance
event.summary: Recorded when a Flash object instance is created.

event.name: PlusAddresses.Submission
event.summary: An email was submitted in a form for which the user had previously seen a plus address suggestions. More precisely, this event is recorded whenever a user submits a form that contains a form field for which a plus address suggestion was shown and the form field contains either a plus address or the current profile's GAIA email.

metric.name: CheckoutOrCartPage
metric.summary: Boolean value representing whether commerce heuristics suggest that the main frame URL belongs to a checkout or a cart page.

metric.name: FieldCountBrowserForm
metric.summary: The number of fields in the browser form. This uses exponential bucketing with a spacing of 1.15.

metric.name: FieldCountRendererForm
metric.summary: The number of fields in the renderer form. This uses exponential bucketing with a spacing of 1.15.

metric.name: ManagedProfile
metric.summary: Boolean value representing whether the active profile is managed.

metric.name: NewlyCreatedPlusAddress
metric.summary: Boolean value representing whether the submitted email was newly created for this submission.

metric.name: PasswordFormType
metric.summary: The password manager classification of the form focused by the user when showing the plus address suggestion.

metric.name: PlusAddressCount
metric.summary: The (bucketed) number of plus addresses the user had when shown the plus address suggestion.

metric.name: SubmittedPlusAddress
metric.summary: Boolean value representing whether the submitted email was a plus address.

metric.name: SuggestionContext
metric.summary: The context in which the plus address suggestion (filling or creation) was shown in the Autofill UI.

metric.name: WasShownCreateSuggestion
metric.summary: Boolean value representing whether the user was offered a suggestion to create a new plus address.

event.name: Popup.Closed
event.summary: A popup window was closed.

metric.name: EngagementTime
metric.summary: The time (in ms, rounded down to the nearest power of 2) a popup WebContents is visible / foregrounded, until it is closed. Keyed by the popup opener's URL (not the URL of the popup).

metric.name: NumActivationInteractions
metric.summary: Integer value representing how many mouse down, touch start user, or raw key down interactions the popup had. This is capped at 50.

metric.name: NumGestureScrollBeginInteractions
metric.summary: Integer value representing how many gesture scroll begin user interactions the popup had. This is capped at 50.

metric.name: NumInteractions
metric.summary: Integer value representing how many user interactions the popup had. This is capped at 100. See IsUserInteractionInputType in web_contents_impl for what an interaction is. Currently it is a mouse, scroll begin, touch start, or raw key down event.

metric.name: RedirectCount
metric.summary: The number of redirects taken by the pop-up's initial navigation. This does not include javascript navigations initiated by the opening document for the pop-up.

metric.name: SafeBrowsingStatus
metric.summary: The site's safe browsing status if the pop-up's navigation commits.

metric.name: Trusted
metric.summary: Boolean value representing whether the popup was opened via some trusted event (e.g. context menu, ctrl-click, etc).

metric.name: UserInitiatedClose
metric.summary: Boolean value to represent whether the popup was closed by user gesture.

metric.name: WindowOpenDisposition
metric.summary: The UI window open disposition used when creating the web contents of the pop-up. Set by the browser process when the pop up tracker is created.

event.name: Popup.Page
event.summary: Page level pop-up statistics.

metric.name: Allowed
metric.summary: Whether a user has, explicitly, allowed all popups on the last committed url of a page. The user's site level popup content setting is queried when a page that opened a popup is destroyed. Only emitted when at least one popup has been opened and the last committed page url is valid.

event.name: Portal.Activate
event.summary: A portal was activated. A portal activation enables a seamless transition between different pages. See the portals explainer for more details: https://github.com/WICG/portals/blob/master/README.md.

metric.name: PaintTiming.PortalActivationToFirstPaint
metric.summary: When this page load happened inside a Portal, this metrics records the time in milliseconds between the portal activation and the first paint after activation.

metric.name: PortalActivation
metric.summary: Measures the time between LCP and the portal activation. Uses exponential bucketing with a spacing of 2, with the units in milliseconds.

metric.name: PortalActivationBeforeLCP
metric.summary: Indicates whether the portal activation happened before or after the largest contentful paint.

event.name: PowerUsageScenariosIntervalData
event.summary:
Metrics to measure power consumption in key usage scenarios, as well as the time spent in each scenario. These metrics are reported for a fixed interval of time, the duration of this interval is recorded in the IntervalDurationSeconds metric.
The events aren't tied to a specific URL, a timer independent of any load event runs in Chrome and report these events at regular intervals. All the metrics associated to this event are for the last interval. The URL attached to the event is the one that has been visible for the longest time during the interval, the exact visibility duration for this URL during the interval is reported in the URLVisibilityTimeSeconds metric.
e.g., at T-0 an interval ends and this event is emitted, a new interval then starts. During the interval the users spends 75% of his time watching a fullscreen video on https://foo.com with 10 tabs opened in background. Just before the end of the interval the user closes all the tabs but one. An event will be emitted for https://foo.com at T+X with the following metrics: IntervalDurationSeconds: X in seconds; URLVisibilityTimeSeconds: 0.75 * X in seconds; FullscreenVideoSingleMonitorSeconds: 0.75 * X in seconds; MaxTabCount: 10; TabClosed: 9; ... (not all metrics are listed here because it'd be too long, but in practice all the metrics are reported for each interval).
This is reported on Mac and Windows. See go/chrome_power_use_per_scenario for more details.

metric.name: BatteryDischargeMode
BatteryDischargeRate
metric.summary: Battery discharge rate per minute, with 1/10000 of full charge resolution, example: - Battery capacity = 4000 mAh; - Battery charge at the beginning of the interval: 3900 mAh; - Battery charge at the end of the interval: 3700 mAh; - Discharge proportion: (3900-3700) / 4000 = 0.05 - Reported value: 500.

metric.name: BrowserShuttingDown
metric.summary: Indicates if the browser is shutting down when this event is reported.

metric.name: CPUTimeMs
metric.summary: The CPU time spent executing code, across all Chrome processes in the interval. This can exceed 100% in multi-thread processes running on multi-core systems.

metric.name: DeviceSleptDuringInterval
metric.summary: Whether or not the device has been sleeping at any point during the interval.

metric.name: FullscreenVideoSingleMonitorSeconds
metric.summary: Time spent playing video in fullscreen, single-monitor. In seconds. This includes video for any tabs during the interval.

metric.name: IdleWakeUps
metric.summary: Sum of idle-wakeups for all Chrome processes (Mac-only).

metric.name: IntervalDurationSeconds
metric.summary: The interval duration, in seconds.

metric.name: MaxTabCount
metric.summary: The maximum number of tabs that existed at the same time during the interval.

metric.name: MaxVisibleWindowCount
metric.summary: The maximum number of windows that have been visible at the same time.

metric.name: OriginVisibilityTimeSeconds
metric.summary: The visibility time for the origin of the URL associated with this event, in seconds. This can differ from URLVisibilityTimeSeconds if there's multiple tabs loaded with the same URL that are visible during the interval. E.g. if there's one tab loaded with foo.com/a visible for 1 second during the interval and one loaded with foo.com/b visible for 2 seconds the report will have URLVisibilityTimeSeconds = 2 seconds and OriginVisibilityTimeSeconds = 3 seconds. This can also exceed the interval duration (e.g. 2 visibles tab with loaded the same origin during the entire interval).

metric.name: PackageExits
metric.summary: Sum of package exits from all Chrome processes (Mac-only).

metric.name: PlayingAudioSeconds
metric.summary: The time spent playing audio in at least one tab, in seconds.

metric.name: TabClosed
metric.summary: The number of tabs that have been closed during the interval.

metric.name: TimePlayingVideoInVisibleTab
metric.summary: The time spent playing video in at least one visible tab, in seconds.

metric.name: TimeSinceInteractionWithBrowserSeconds
metric.summary: The time since the last interaction with the browser when this event gets recorded, in seconds.

metric.name: TimeSinceInteractionWithSystemSeconds
metric.summary: The time since the last interaction with the system when this event gets recorded, in seconds.

metric.name: TimeWithOpenWebRTCConnectionSeconds
metric.summary: The time spent with at least one opened WebRTC connection, in seconds.

metric.name: TopLevelNavigationEvents
metric.summary: Number of main frame different-document navigations.

metric.name: UptimeSeconds
metric.summary: Elapsed time since Chrome has started, in seconds.

metric.name: URLVisibilityTimeSeconds
metric.summary: The visibility time for the URL associated with this event, in seconds.

metric.name: UserInteractionCount
metric.summary: Number of user interaction (scroll, click or typing) during the interval.

metric.name: VideoCaptureSeconds
metric.summary: The time spent capturing video in at least one tab, in seconds.

event.name: PrefetchProxy
event.summary: Metrics related to the privacy-preserving prefetch proxy.

metric.name: days_since_last_visit_to_origin
metric.summary: The number of days since the user has last visited any page on this origin, either during a redirect or a committed page. Records -1 if the origin has never been visited before. Metrics for origins that have been visited before are capped at 180 then exponentially bucketed into 10 buckets with values (1,2,3,5,9,15,25,42,70,119). Only set when a query result was received from the history service before event recording.

metric.name: prefetch_attempted_count
metric.summary: The number of pages on the Google Search Result Page that were attempted to be prefetched.

metric.name: prefetch_eligible_count
metric.summary: The number of pages on the Google Search Result page that were eligible to be prefetched.

metric.name: prefetch_successful_count
metric.summary: The number of pages on the Google Search Result Page that were successfully prefetched.

event.name: PrefetchProxy.AfterSRPClick
event.summary: Metrics related to the privacy-preserving prefetch proxy on the page load after the SRP. Recorded only for eligible users on the first mainframe user-initiated page load immediately after a Google Search Result Page (SRP) which Chrome parsed and may have prefetched links.

metric.name: ClickedLinkSRPPosition
metric.summary: When a user clicks on a Google SRP link, this int records which position the clicked link was in. Recorded only when the user navigates to a Google SRP link.

metric.name: PrefetchHeaderLatencyMs
metric.summary: Time between the prefetch request was sent and the time the response headers were received. Unset if the headers have not been received by the time the user navigated to the current page.

metric.name: PrivatePrefetch
metric.summary: This is 1 if the prefetched resource was prefetched via the private prefetch proxy. This is not logged otherwise.

metric.name: ProbeLatencyMs
metric.summary: How long the network probe blocked commit in milliseconds. Recorded only when a probe was required.

metric.name: SameTabAsPrefetchingTab
metric.summary: This is 1 if the page load matching the prefetch was in the same WebContents as the page that triggerd the prefetch. This is not logged otherwise.

metric.name: SRPClickPrefetchStatus
metric.summary: The outcome of the prefetch eligibility check and possible attempt to prefetch the resource. The prefetched url is always the committed URL of the page. See |PrefetchProxyPrefetchStatus| for a mapping of enum values and their meanings.

metric.name: SRPPrefetchEligibleCount
metric.summary: The number of links from the SRP page (the previous page load) that were eligible for prefetch. Always set when this event is recorded.

event.name: PrefetchProxy.PrefetchedResource
event.summary: Each event corresponds to a single resource, either mainframe or subresource, that was prefetched during an Isolated Prerender. Logged one or many times on a Google Search Result Page for eligible users.

metric.name: DataLength
metric.summary: The bucketed number of bytes that transited the network to prefetch this resource. Only set when the resource was prefetched.

metric.name: FetchDurationMS
metric.summary: The number of milliseconds that the prefetch took from start to completion. Only set when the resource was prefetched.

metric.name: ISPFilteringStatus
metric.summary: How ISP filtering interacted with the mainframe resource if it was navigated to. Only set when LinkClicked is true.

metric.name: LinkClicked
metric.summary: Whether the mainframe was navigated to. Always set when the event is recorded, even on subresources which take the value from their corresponding mainframe.

metric.name: LinkPosition
metric.summary: The location in the original navigation prediction of where the mainframe url was. Always set when the event is recorded, even on subresources which take the value from their corresponding mainframe.

metric.name: NavigationStartToFetchStartMS
metric.summary: The number of milliseconds between the start of the navigation and the start of the prefetch request. Only set when the resource was attempted to be prefetched.

metric.name: ResourceType
metric.summary: The type of resource that was prefetched, either mainframe or subresource. Always set when the event is recorded.

metric.name: Status
metric.summary: The outcome of the prefetch eligibility check and possible attempt to prefetch the resource and reuse it. See |PrefetchProxyPrefetchStatus| for a mapping of enum values and their meanings. Always set when the event is recorded.

event.name: Preloading.AnchorInteraction
event.summary: Logged once for each preconnection request sent to the browser in response to the user interacting with an anchor element. Associated with the Source ID of the page triggering the preconnection.

metric.name: AnchorElementPreloaderType
metric.summary: One event for each preload operation that was triggered on the current page.

event.name: Preloading.Attempt
event.summary:
This event is logged for each preloading attempt i.e., for preloading features like Preresolve, Preconnect, Prerender, Prefetch, and others when the user navigates or when the WebContents is being destroyed.
Associated with the Source ID of the page user navigates to. Contains information about the type of attempt, its eligibility, triggering outcome, failure reason and whether the attempt was triggered accurately or not.
Note that this event is sampled based on the PreloadingConfig feature configuration (in addition to the normal UKM sampling mechanism). The sampling rate for each event (which may vary for different preloading features) is logged in the SamplingLikelihood field.

metric.name: AccurateTriggering
metric.summary: Whether the preloading operation was triggered accurately for the predicted URL (regardless of the eligibility, failures, etc.). Set to true if the user navigated to the predicted URL.

metric.name: Eligibility
metric.summary: Eligibility status of preloading operation for the prediction. Values are from the Chrome enum content::PreloadingEligibility.

metric.name: FailureReason
metric.summary: Reason why the preloading operation failed. Values are from the enum specific to the preloading type.

metric.name: HoldbackStatus
metric.summary: The outcome of the holdback check. This is not part of eligibility status to make clarify that this check needs to happen after we are done verifying the eligibility of a preloading attempt. In general, eligibility checks can be reordered, but the holdback check always needs to come after verifying that the preloading attempt was eligible.

metric.name: PrefetchServiceWorkerRegisteredCheck
metric.summary: Records how many times are we checking if a Service Worker is registered for an origin (fast check) or for a certain path of the origin (slow check) to decide if we are proceeding with a prefetch or not. Enum values would be: OriginOnly and Path. OriginOnly value describes the case when there are no Service Workers registered for the origin and we only need to run the fast check. Path value describes the case when there are Service Workers registered for the origin and we need to do a more expensive path check to find out if the prefetch URL falls under a registered Service Worker.

metric.name: PrefetchServiceWorkerRegisteredForURLCheckDuration
metric.summary: Records how long (in microseconds) does the slow check for a Service Worker being registered for a certain path take when deciding if we are proceeding with a prefetch or not. We expect this check to take up to 2 ms. Exponentially bucketed using GetExponentialBucketMin with a value of 1.15.

metric.name: PreloadingPredictor
metric.summary: The type of preloading predictor which made the prediction. Values are from the Chrome enum content::PreloadingPredictor or ChromePreloadingPredictor.

metric.name: PreloadingType
metric.summary: The type of preloading operation used for the preloading attempt. Values are from the Chrome enum content::PreloadingType.

metric.name: ReadyTime
metric.summary: The amount of time the attempt took to reach a state in which it is ready to be used for the next navigation, in milliseconds. Note: currently only Prefetch, Prerender and NoStatePrefetch have a ready state. This field is only meaningful to these three types of preloading.

metric.name: SamplingLikelihood
metric.summary: The sampling rate of this particular event multiplied by 1,000,000 (because we cannot log floating point values in UKM). A value of 1,000,000 means that the event had a 100% chance of being sampled, whereas a value of one means that the event had a one-in-a-millon chance of being sampled. The sampling rate for preloading attempts is set via field trial through the PreloadingConfig feature. The logged value just represents the configuration used for that preloading attempt.

metric.name: SpeculationEagerness
metric.summary: The eagerness of the speculation rule that triggered this preload. This is only set if the Preloading Attempt is triggered by a speculation rule. Values are from the Chrome enum blink::mojom::SpeculationEagerness.

metric.name: TimeToNextNavigation
metric.summary: The amount of time the attempt took to reach the next navigation, in milliseconds. This can happen multiple times, but only the first is recorded.

metric.name: TriggeringOutcome
metric.summary: Specifies whether the preloading attempt triggered and was used for the next navigation. Values are from the Chrome enum content::PreloadingTriggeringOutcome.

event.name: Preloading.Attempt.PreviousPrimaryPage
event.summary:
This is same as Preloading.Attempt but UKM is associated with the primary triggering page (if exists) and logged when the user navigates away or when the WebContents is being destroyed.
Associated with the Source ID of the WebContents primary page where the preloading attempt is triggered (user navigates from). All the metrics data are similar to Preloading.Attempt and please see the comments for more details.
Note that this event is sampled based on the PreloadingConfig feature configuration (in addition to the normal UKM sampling mechanism). The sampling rate for each event (which may vary for different preloading features) is logged in the SamplingLikelihood field.

metric.name: AccurateTriggering
metric.summary:
Whether the preloading operation was triggered accurately (regardless of the eligibility, holdback, failures, etc.).
Set to true if the user navigated to the predicted URL.

metric.name: Eligibility
metric.summary: Eligibility status of preloading operation for the prediction.

metric.name: FailureReason
metric.summary: Reason why the preloading operation failed.

metric.name: HoldbackStatus
metric.summary: The outcome of the holdback check. This is not part of eligibility status to make clarify that this check needs to happen after we are done verifying the eligibility of a preloading attempt. In general, eligibility checks can be reordered, but the holdback check always needs to come after verifying that the preloading attempt was eligible.

metric.name: PrefetchServiceWorkerRegisteredCheck
metric.summary: Records how many times are we checking if a Service Worker is registered for an origin (fast check) or for a certain path of the origin (slow check) to decide if we are proceeding with a prefetch or not. Enum values would be: OriginOnly and Path. OriginOnly value describes the case when there are no Service Workers registered for the origin and we only need to run the fast check. Path value describes the case when there are Service Workers registered for the origin and we need to do a more expensive path check to find out if the prefetch URL falls under a registered Service Worker.

metric.name: PrefetchServiceWorkerRegisteredForURLCheckDuration
metric.summary: Records how long (in microseconds) does the slow check for a Service Worker being registered for a certain path take when deciding if we are proceeding with a prefetch or not. We expect this check to take up to 2 ms. Exponentially bucketed using GetExponentialBucketMin with a value of 1.15.

metric.name: PreloadingPredictor
metric.summary: The type of predictor that made the prediction. Values are from either content::PreloadingPredictor or ChromePreloadingPredictor.

metric.name: PreloadingType
metric.summary: The type of preloading operation used for the preloading attempt.

metric.name: ReadyTime
metric.summary: The amount of time the attempt took to reach a state in which it is ready to be used for the next navigation, in milliseconds. Note: currently only Prefetch, Prerender and NoStatePrefetch have a ready state. This field is only meaningful to these three types of preloading.

metric.name: SamplingLikelihood
metric.summary: The sampling rate of this particular event multiplied by 1,000,000 (because we cannot log floating point values in UKM). A value of 1,000,000 means that the event had a 100% chance of being sampled, whereas a value of one means that the event had a one-in-a-millon chance of being sampled. The sampling rate for preloading attempts is set via field trial through the PreloadingConfig feature. The logged value just represents the configuration used for that preloading attempt.

metric.name: SpeculationEagerness
metric.summary: The eagerness of the speculation rule that triggered this preload. This is only set if the Preloading Attempt is triggered by a speculation rule. Values are from the Chrome enum blink::mojom::SpeculationEagerness.

metric.name: TimeToNextNavigation
metric.summary: The amount of time the attempt took to reach the next navigation, in milliseconds. This can happen multiple times, but only the first is recorded.

metric.name: TriggeringOutcome
metric.summary: Specifies whether the preloading triggered and was used for the next navigation.

event.name: Preloading.NavigationPredictorModelTrainingData
event.summary: Metrics that describe an anchor element, the user's interaction therewith, and whether a prediction of a navigation to the target destination would be accurate. This event is emitted on navigation. The number of events emitted at a time depends on the number of predictions the model makes, subject to sampling. See also NavigationPredictorAnchorElementMetrics and NavigationPredictorUserInteractions.

metric.name: ContainsImage
metric.summary: 1 if the anchor element contains an image.

metric.name: FontSize
metric.summary: The bucketed font size of the anchor element, according to its computed style. 1 corresponds to a pixel font size in [0, 10), 2 corresponds to a fonts size in [10, 18) and 3 is logged when the font size is 18 pixels or larger.

metric.name: HasTextSibling
metric.summary: 1 if the anchor element has an immediate text sibling, 0 otherwise.

metric.name: HoverDwellTimeMs
metric.summary: Dwell time of pointer hovering over the anchor element in milliseconds. Exponentially bucketed using GetExponentialBucketMin with a value of 1.3.

metric.name: IsAccurate
metric.summary: 1 if a navigation to the anchor's destination occurs.

metric.name: IsBold
metric.summary: 1 if the anchor's computed style's font-weight is greater than 500, 0 otherwise.

metric.name: IsInIframe
metric.summary: 1 if the anchor element is in an iframe.

metric.name: IsURLIncrementedByOne
metric.summary: 1 if the anchor element's target URL and the document's source URL only differ by one number, and the target URL increments that number in the source URL by 1.

metric.name: NavigationStartToLinkLoggedMs
metric.summary: The time in ms between navigation start and the moment the anchor was first seen. Exponentially bucketed using GetExponentialBucketMin with a value of 1.3.

metric.name: PathDepth
metric.summary: The number of slashes in the anchor element's href URL path. Truncated at 5.

metric.name: PathLength
metric.summary: The number of characters in the anchor element's href URL path. Buckets of size 10, truncated at 100.

metric.name: PercentClickableArea
metric.summary: The percent of the total document area that this anchor element's clickable area occupies.

metric.name: PercentVerticalDistance
metric.summary: How far down the document the top of the anchor element is, expressed as a ratio with the total document height.

metric.name: PointerHoveringOverCount
metric.summary: How many times the pointer was hovering over the anchor element. Exponentially bucketed using GetExponentialBucketMin a value of 1.3.

metric.name: SameHost
metric.summary: 1 if the anchor element's target URL and the document's source URL are the same host.

metric.name: SamplingAmount
metric.summary: Indicates whether the number of predictions for the associated page was over a limit and needed to be downsampled. All events for a given page logged at the same time have the same SamplingAmount. The value is the exponentially bucketed value of (1,000,000 - (1,000,000 * sampling_rate)). The multiplication by 1,000,000 is because we cannot log floating point values in UKM, and we have larger values indicate more sampling so that a lack of sampling can be unambiguous by having its own bucket. For example, if the number of predictions was under the limit and no sampling was done, then the value would be 0. If the number of predictions was double the limit, the value would be 500,000.

event.name: Preloading.Prediction
event.summary:
This event is logged for each preloading prediction and metrics related to the preloading predictors like OmniboxDirectURLInput, Speculation Rules and others when the navigation is finished or when the WebContents is being destroyed.
Associated with the Source ID of the page user navigates to. Contains information about the type of the predictor, its confidence value and whether the prediction ended up being correct.

metric.name: AccuratePrediction
metric.summary: Whether the user navigated to the predicted URL.

metric.name: Confidence
metric.summary: The confidence percentage of predictor's correct prediction for preloading.

metric.name: PreloadingPredictor
metric.summary: The type of preloading predictor that made the prediction. Values are from content::PreloadingPredictor or ChromePreloadingPredictor.

metric.name: SamplingAmount
metric.summary: Indicates whether the number of preloading predictions for the associated page was over a limit and needed to be downsampled. All events for a given page logged at the same time have the same SamplingAmount. The value is the exponentially bucketed value of (1,000,000 - (1,000,000 * sampling_rate)). The multiplication by 1,000,000 is because we cannot log floating point values in UKM, and we have larger values indicate more sampling so that a lack of sampling can be unambiguous by having its own bucket. For example, if the number of predictions was under the limit and no sampling was done, then the value would be 0. If the number of predictions was double the limit, the value would be 500,000.

metric.name: TimeToNextNavigation
metric.summary: The amount of time the prediction took to reach the next navigation, in milliseconds. This can happen multiple times, but only the first is recorded.

event.name: Preloading.Prediction.PreviousPrimaryPage
event.summary:
This is the same as Preloading.Prediction but UKM is associated with the WebContents primary triggering page (if exists) and logged when the user navigates away or when the WebContents is being destroyed.
Associated with the Source ID of the primary page where the preloading prediction is triggered (user navigates from). All the metrics data are similar to Preloading.Prediction and please see the comments for more details.

metric.name: AccuratePrediction
metric.summary: Whether the user navigated to the predicted URL.

metric.name: Confidence
metric.summary: The confidence percentage of predictor's correct prediction for preloading.

metric.name: PreloadingPredictor
metric.summary: The type of preloading predictor which made the preloading prediction. Values are from content::PreloadingPredictor or ChromePreloadingPredictor.

metric.name: SamplingAmount
metric.summary: Indicates whether the number of preloading predictions for the associated page was over a limit and needed to be downsampled. All events for a given page logged at the same time have the same SamplingAmount. The value is the exponentially bucketed value of (1,000,000 - (1,000,000 * sampling_rate)). The multiplication by 1,000,000 is because we cannot log floating point values in UKM, and we have larger values indicate more sampling so that a lack of sampling can be unambiguous by having its own bucket. For example, if the number of predictions was under the limit and no sampling was done, then the value would be 0. If the number of predictions was double the limit, the value would be 500,000.

metric.name: TimeToNextNavigation
metric.summary: The amount of time the prediction took to reach the next navigation, in milliseconds. This can happen multiple times, but only the first is recorded.

event.name: PrerenderPageLoad
event.summary: Metrics related to Prerender2 (https://docs.google.com/document/d/1P2VKCLpmnNm_cRAjUeE-bqLL0bslL_zKqiNeCzNom_w/edit?usp=sharing).

metric.name: FinalStatus
metric.summary:
A status of a prerendering attempt. Recorded as enum PrerenderHostFinalStatus in //tools/metrics/histograms/enums.xml.
This is replicated as Prerender.Experimental.PrerenderHostFinalStatus.* in UMA.

metric.name: InteractiveTiming.FirstInputDelay4
metric.summary: The duration between the hardware timestamp and the start of event processing on the main thread for the first meaningful input. Recorded on the prerender activation.

metric.name: InteractiveTiming.NumInteractions
metric.summary: The number of distinct user interactions on a prerendered page after it is successfully activated. See definition of user interaction: https://web.dev/better-responsiveness-metric/#group-events-into-interactions

metric.name: InteractiveTiming.UserInteractionLatency.HighPercentile2.MaxEventDuration
metric.summary: An approximation of a high percentile of user interaction latency of a prerendered page after it is successfully activated, in ms. For this metric, we measure the latency of user interactions by Responsiveness.UserInteraction.MaxEventDuration.

metric.name: InteractiveTiming.WorstUserInteractionLatency.MaxEventDuration
metric.summary: The maximum value of user interaction latency of a prerendered page after it is successfully activated, in ms. For this metric, we measure the latency of user interactions by Responsiveness.UserInteraction.MaxEventDuration.

metric.name: LayoutInstability.MaxCumulativeShiftScore.SessionWindow.Gap1000ms.Max5000ms
metric.summary: Measures the maximum cumulative layout shift (bit.ly/cls-explainer) that has occurred on the prerendered page that is successfully activated in a session window. The gap between two consecutive shifts in a window is not bigger than 1000ms and the maximum window size is 5000ms. This metric's integral value is 100x the fractional cumulative layout shift score described in the explainer.

metric.name: MainFrameResource.RequestHasNoStore
metric.summary: A boolean indicating if any prerender activation page load mainpage resource has a 'Cache-control: no-store' response header. Recorded only for main frame request.

metric.name: Navigation.InitiatorLocation
metric.summary: page_load_metrics::NavigationHandleUserData::InitiatorLocation enum value. This field is used for identifying the types of prerender trigger.

metric.name: Navigation.PageTransition
metric.summary: The |ui::PageTransition| for the main frame navigation that this activated prerendering navigation fulfilled.

metric.name: Timing.ActivationToFirstContentfulPaint
metric.summary: The duration in milliseconds from time of activation navigation start to the time when the page first paints content. A contentful paint includes a paint of text, image, SVG, or canvas.

metric.name: Timing.ActivationToLargestContentfulPaint
metric.summary: The duration in milliseconds from time of activation navigation start to the time when the page first paints the largest content (text or image) within viewport, across all frames.

metric.name: Timing.NavigationToActivation
metric.summary: The duration in milliseconds from the start of initial prerendering navigation to the start of activation navigation. This corresponds to the activationStart attribute of PerformanceNavigationTiming.

metric.name: TriggeredPrerender
metric.summary:
A boolean that is set to true if the page triggered prerendering.
Unlike other PrerenderPageLoad metrics, this is recorded not for prerendered page loads, but for page loads that initiated prerender.

metric.name: WasPrerendered
metric.summary: A boolean that is set to true if the page was prerendered and later activated. Cancelled prerender page loads are not logged.

event.name: Presentation.StartResult
event.summary: Records when users start a Presentation and receive a response from the receiver. Android only. The following failures are not recorded because they don't represent an actual error: (1) PresentationErrorType::PRESENTATION_REQUEST_CANCELLED This error is created when users close the dialog without starting a Presentation. (2) PresentationErrorType::NO_PRESENTATION_FOUND This error might happen when a website implementing CastSDK try to join a route every time the page loads.

metric.name: PresentationRequest
metric.summary: Records whether the users' request to start a Presentation with a "cast:" presentation URL succeed.

metric.name: RemotePlayback
metric.summary: Records whether the users' request to start a Presentation through RemotePlayback succeed.

event.name: PWA.Visit
event.summary: PWA usage metrics recorded during a user's interaction with the site.

metric.name: LaunchSource
metric.summary: Launch source of the WebAPK (tap from homescreen, WebShareTarget, deeplink, etc).

metric.name: WebAPKableSiteVisit
metric.summary: Recorded every time a non-installed "WebAPKable" site is visited.

event.name: RedirectHeuristic.CookieAccess2
event.summary: Recorded when a top-level page has its cookies accessed by another page earlier in its redirect chain.

metric.name: AccessAllowed
metric.summary: Whether the cookie access attempt is allowed, or blocked by a third-party cookie block.

metric.name: AccessId
metric.summary: A random integer to identify this cookie access and associate this event with a RedirectHeuristic.CookieAccessThirdParty2 event.

metric.name: DoesFirstPartyPrecedeThirdParty
metric.summary: Whether this top-level site appeared in the tab history (or current navigation) directly before the third party. Used to check for an A-B-A site flow that may narrow the heuristic.

metric.name: HoursSinceLastInteraction
metric.summary: The number of hours before the cookie access that the user last interacted with the tracking site. Clamped to 60 days at most, and rounded down to the nearest exponential bucket (out of 50 buckets). Set to -1 if there has never been a user interaction from the tracking site.

metric.name: IsAdTagged
metric.summary: Whether the cookie access attempt was tagged as an ad cookie, and thus exempt from mitigations including heuristics.

metric.name: IsCurrentInteraction
metric.summary: Whether there was a user interaction on the tracking site when it was loaded in the current redirect chain.

metric.name: MillisecondsSinceRedirect
metric.summary: The number of milliseconds between the redirect to the cookie access. Clamped to 15 minutes at most, and rounded down to the nearest exponential bucket (out of 50 buckets).

metric.name: OpenerHasSameSiteIframe
metric.summary: "True" iff the target page contains an iframe whose URL belongs to the same registrable domain as the tracker page. "Unknown" if the presence of an iframe could not be determined.

metric.name: SitesPassedCount
metric.summary: The number of top-level sites redirected through between the initial tracking page and the final page whose cookies are accessed. Capped at 20 sites.

event.name: RedirectHeuristic.CookieAccessThirdParty2
event.summary: Recorded when a top-level page accesses cookies on another page later in its redirect chain.

metric.name: AccessId
metric.summary: A random integer to identify this cookie access and associate this event with a RedirectHeuristic.CookieAccess2 event.

event.name: RendererSchedulerTask
event.summary: Sampled task from the renderer main thread. Sample rate is controlled dynamically. See blink::scheduler::MainThreadSchedulerImpl::ShouldRecordTaskUkm.

metric.name: FrameStatus
metric.summary: Frame status of the frame associated with the context of this task. See blink::scheduler::FrameStatus for the values of this enum.

metric.name: IsOOPIF
metric.summary: Whether the frame which the task is associated with is an out-of-process iframe. Boolean encoded as an integer (0/1).

metric.name: PageSchedulers
metric.summary: Number of page schedulers in this process. If the value equals one, global per-process tasks can be attributed to the URL.

metric.name: QueueType
metric.summary: Type of the task queue which this task was posted to. See blink::scheduler::MainThreadTaskQueue::TaskType for the values of this enum.

metric.name: RendererAudible
metric.summary: Whether renderer was playing audio when this task was completed. Boolean, encoded as an integer (0/1).

metric.name: RendererBackgrounded
metric.summary: Whether renderer was backgrounded when this task was completed. Boolean, encoded as an integer (0/1).

metric.name: RendererHidden
metric.summary: Whether renderer was hidden when this task was completed. Boolean, encoded as an integer (0/1).

metric.name: SecondsSinceBackgrounded
metric.summary: Seconds since the renderer was backgrounded, recorded when the task was completed. Only set if the renderer is backgrounded.

metric.name: TaskCPUDuration
metric.summary: CPU duration of this task in microseconds.

metric.name: TaskDuration
metric.summary: Duration of this task in microseconds.

metric.name: TaskType
metric.summary: blink::TaskType for the current task.

metric.name: ThreadType
metric.summary: blink::ThreadType for the thread the current task was executed on.

metric.name: UseCase
metric.summary: MainThreadSchedulerImpl's UseCase when this task was completed. See blink::scheduler::UseCase for the values of this enum.

metric.name: Version
metric.summary: The version of this metric, which is used to track the actual set of values present across different builds and channels. Hardcoded into the binary and incremented each time a new field is added or existing is changed. Integer.

event.name: RenderViewContextMenu.Used
event.summary: Metric recorded when a context menu item is selected. This is currently only recorded for Lens context menu items.

metric.name: SelectedMenuItem
metric.summary: The menu item selected by the user on the context menu.

event.name: Responsiveness.UserInteraction
event.summary: Metrics that are associated with user interactions and recorded when users interact with web pages. Currently we are interested in keyboard, tap, click and drag. A user interaction often encompasses multiple input events. For keyboard interactions, we only care about keydown and keyup. For tap, click and drag, we only measure pointerdown, pointerup and click.

metric.name: InteractionType
metric.summary: The type of the user interaction that triggered input event sequence, not the input event type. It can be keyboard, tap, click or drag.

metric.name: MaxEventDuration
metric.summary: The longest input event duration within the user interaction. The unit of duration is ms. For example, if a user pressed a key, we can get a keydown and a keyup. If the duration(keydown) > duration(keyup), the value of this metric will be duration(keydown).

metric.name: TotalEventDuration
metric.summary: The sum of input event durations within the user interaction without double counting the overlap. The unit of duration is ms. For example, if a user pressed a key, we can get a keydown and a keyup. The value of this metric will be duration(keydown) + duration(keyup) - duration(intersection(keydown, keyup)).

event.name: ResponsivenessMeasurement
event.summary: Responsiveness metrics recorded once every 5 seconds.

event.name: SafeBrowsingInterstitial
event.summary: Records that the user encountered a Safe Browsing interstitial and whether they proceeded past the interstitial, bypassing the warning.

metric.name: Bypassed
metric.summary: A boolean signaling that the user bypassed the interstitial; only records true values. This event gets logged right when a user clicks to proceed on the Safe Browsing interstitial UI.

metric.name: Shown
metric.summary: A boolean signaling that the interstitial was shown to the user; only records true values. This event gets logged right when the Safe Browsing interstitial is shown to the user.

event.name: SalientImageAvailability
event.summary: Metrics on the salient image of the page, one record per page load commit and has an annotation calculated on it.

metric.name: ImageAvailability
metric.summary: Records whether salient image was available or not for this page, the source of the salient image and why it was unavailable.

event.name: SamesiteRedirectContextDowngrade
event.summary: A collection of metrics which are recorded for cookies whose inclusion would be changed if the redirect chain was considered in their same-site calculation. This change in inclusion is because the same-site context would "downgrade" from a more permissive context to a less permissive context, one which may not allow a cookie's inclusion.

metric.name: AgePerCookie
metric.summary: Records the age of the cookies, in minutes, for cookies which are being read (on a requset or via JS). The value rounded down to the exponential bucket with factor of 2.0.

metric.name: SamesiteValueReadPerCookie
metric.summary: Records the SameSite value of the cookie for cookies which are being read (on a request or via JS).

metric.name: SamesiteValueWritePerCookie
metric.summary: Records the SameSite value of the cookie for cookies which are being written (on a response or via JS).

event.name: SavedTabGroup.Navigation
event.summary: A collection of metrics which are recorded for navigations inside saved tab groups. The metrics are recorded when a URL is synced from another client and Chrome finishes the navigation to that URL.

metric.name: IsPost
metric.summary: A boolean indicating whether the current navigation is triggered by a POST request.

metric.name: NumSameUrlLoad
metric.summary: Records how many times the same URL, including the current navigation, is loaded in the current tab in the past 2 minutes.

metric.name: NumSameUrlWithoutRefAndQueryParamLoad
metric.summary: Records how many times the same URL, without ref and query parameters, is loaded in the current tab in the past 2 minutes. The current navigation is also included in this count.

metric.name: TabGroupType
metric.summary: Records the type of the tab group the navigation belongs to, such as shared or synced. See |tab_groups::SavedTabGroupTypes| for enum elements.

metric.name: WasRedirected
metric.summary: A boolean indicating whether the current navigation involves any redirection.

metric.name: RequestPerCookie
metric.summary: An enum which records the type of schemeful same-site downgrade applied to a cookie which is being sent on a request along with the secureness of the origin the cookie is attempting to be sent to. This is recorded once per cookie when the cookie is included in the request or if the cookie excluded due to insufficient same-site context only. Cookies that meet the above conditions but without a downgrade warning are not recorded.

metric.name: ResponsePerCookie
metric.summary: An enum which records the type of schemeful same-site downgrade applied to a cookie which is being set by a response along with the secureness of the origin that is attempting to set the cookie. This is recorded once per cookie when the cookie is allowed to be set or if the cookie is ignored due to insufficient same-site context only. Cookies that meet the above conditions but without a downgrade warning are not recorded.

event.name: ScreenBrightness
event.summary: Collects metrics periodically and on screen brightness change.

metric.name: BatteryPercent
metric.summary: Percentage of battery, rounded down to a multiple of 5%, i.e. [0, 5%) is mapped to 0, [5%, 10%) is mapped to 5 etc.

metric.name: Brightness
metric.summary: Screen percentage brightness. between 0 and 100.

metric.name: DayOfWeek
metric.summary: An enum representing the of the week that the data was logged, defined in |ash::power::ml::ScreenBrightnessEvent::Features::ActivityData:: DayOfWeek|.

metric.name: DeviceMode
metric.summary: An enum representing the mode of the device, defined in |ash::power::ml::ScreenBrightnessEvent::Features::EnvData:DeviceMode|

metric.name: HourOfDay
metric.summary: The hour of the day when the data is logged. Hours since midnight in the local time zone.

metric.name: IsAutoclickEnabled
metric.summary: Boolean value to represent whether the auto click is currently enabled.

metric.name: IsBrailleDisplayConnected
metric.summary: Boolean value to represent whether the braille display is currently enabled.

metric.name: IsCaretHighlightEnabled
metric.summary: Boolean value to represent whether the caret highlight is currently enabled.

metric.name: IsCursorHighlightEnabled
metric.summary: Boolean value to represent whether the cursor highlight is currently enabled.

metric.name: IsFocusHighlightEnabled
metric.summary: Boolean value to represent whether the focus highlight is currently enabled.

metric.name: IsHighContrastEnabled
metric.summary: Boolean value to represent whether the high contrast is currently enabled.

metric.name: IsLargeCursorEnabled
metric.summary: Boolean value to represent whether the large cursor is currently enabled.

metric.name: IsMagnifierEnabled
metric.summary: Boolean value to represent whether the full screen magnifier is currently enabled.

metric.name: IsMonoAudioEnabled
metric.summary: Boolean value to represent whether the mono audio is currently enabled.

metric.name: IsSelectToSpeakEnabled
metric.summary: Boolean value to represent whether select to speak is currently enabled.

metric.name: IsSpokenFeedbackEnabled
metric.summary: Boolean value to represent whether the spoken feedback is currently enabled.

metric.name: IsSwitchAccessEnabled
metric.summary: Boolean value to represent whether Switch Access is currently enabled.

metric.name: IsVideoPlaying
metric.summary: Boolean value to represent whether video is currently playing.

metric.name: IsVirtualKeyboardEnabled
metric.summary: Boolean value to represent whether the virtual keyboard is currently enabled.

metric.name: LastActivityTimeSec
metric.summary: Time between the last activity and when the data was logged. A duration timestamp in seconds.

metric.name: NightLightTemperaturePercent
metric.summary: The temperature percentage of night light screen color adjustment when night light is on. Ranges from 0 to 100.

metric.name: NumRecentKeyEvents
metric.summary: The number of keyboard events in the last hour.

metric.name: NumRecentMouseEvents
metric.summary: The number of mouse events in the last hour.

metric.name: NumRecentStylusEvents
metric.summary: The number of stylus events in the last hour.

metric.name: NumRecentTouchEvents
metric.summary: The number of touch events in the last hour.

metric.name: OnBattery
metric.summary: Boolean value to represent whether the device is currently on battery power.

metric.name: PreviousBrightness
metric.summary: The brightness of the screen prior to the time at which the current event was logged.

metric.name: Reason
metric.summary: The reason that the event is logged. Values are enumerated in |ash::power::ml::ScreenBrightnessEvent::Event::Reason|.

metric.name: RecentTimeActiveSec
metric.summary: How long the user has been active. A duration timestamp in seconds.

metric.name: SequenceId
metric.summary: The ID of this event in the current session.

metric.name: TimeSinceLastEventSec
metric.summary: Time in seconds since last time an event was logged.

event.name: Security.SafetyTip
event.summary: Collected when any safety tip heuristic triggers, regardless of whether the Safety Tip UI is shown.

metric.name: SafetyTipInteraction
metric.summary: How the user interacted with the UI, if applicable.

metric.name: SafetyTipStatus
metric.summary: The final reported Safety Tip status.

metric.name: TriggeredKeywordsHeuristics
metric.summary: Whether the sensitive keywords heuristic triggered.

metric.name: TriggeredLookalikeHeuristics
metric.summary: Whether the lookalike keywords heuristic triggered.

metric.name: TriggeredServerSideBlocklist
metric.summary: Whether the server-side blocklist heuristic triggered.

metric.name: UserPreviouslyIgnored
metric.summary: Whether the user had already ignored a safety tip warning for this page in the past, meaning no tip was shown.

event.name: Security.SiteEngagement
event.summary: Site engagement score behavior for a page with a given Security Level.

metric.name: FinalSecurityLevel
metric.summary: The final SecurityLevel of the page before it is closed or another navigation occurs. Based on the enum security_state::SecurityLevel.

metric.name: InitialSecurityLevel
metric.summary: The initial SecurityLevel of the page when the navigation commits. Based on the enum security_state::SecurityLevel.

metric.name: SafetyTipStatus
metric.summary: The Safety Tip status of the page when the navigation commits, after the reputation check finishes.

metric.name: ScoreDelta
metric.summary: The change in Site Engagement score for the page before it is closed or another navigation occurs compared to the score from when the navigation committed. Score is between 0.0 and 100.0; delta is between -100.0 and 100.0. Rounded to the nearest integer.

metric.name: ScoreFinal
metric.summary: The final Site Engagement score for the page before it is closed or another navigation occurs. Score is between 0.0 and 100.0; this metric is rounded down to a multiple of 10 to limit granularity.

event.name: Segmentation.ModelExecution
event.summary: Input and output tensors for executing the ML model identified by optimization targets and version. The input tensors can come from UMA or UKM, with or without aggregation. Check model metadata for more information about each tensor. Since all the ML tensors are floats, they are encoded into int64 and will be decoded later during model training. This metric is recorded after an ML model is executed, and the actual result is collected.

metric.name: ActualResult
metric.summary: UMA or UKM metrics related to the features affected by the ML model, with or without aggregation, after running the features as instructed by the ML model. For example, this can be the 7 day total button clicks after an ML model decides to show it. No encoding is used as this is normally a count or sum of a UMA/UKM metric.

metric.name: ActualResult2
metric.summary: The 2nd UMA or UKM metrics related to the features affected by the ML model, with or without aggregation, after running the features as instructed by the ML model. No encoding is used.

metric.name: ActualResult3
metric.summary: The 3rd UMA or UKM metrics related to the features affected by the ML model, with or without aggregation, after running the features as instructed by the ML model. No encoding is used.

metric.name: ActualResult4
metric.summary: The 4th UMA or UKM metrics related to the features affected by the ML model, with or without aggregation, after running the features as instructed by the ML model. No encoding is used.

metric.name: ActualResult5
metric.summary: The 5th UMA or UKM metrics related to the features affected by the ML model, with or without aggregation, after running the features as instructed by the ML model. No encoding is used.

metric.name: ActualResult6
metric.summary: The 6th UMA or UKM metrics related to the features affected by the ML model, with or without aggregation, after running the features as instructed by the ML model. No encoding is used.

metric.name: Input0
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 0 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input1
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 1 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input10
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 10 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input11
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 11 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input12
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 12 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input13
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 13 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input14
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 14 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input15
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 15 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input16
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 16 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input17
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 17 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input18
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 18 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input19
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 19 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input2
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 2 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input20
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 20 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input21
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 21 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input22
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 22 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input23
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 23 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input24
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 24 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input25
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 25 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input26
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 26 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input27
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 27 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input28
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 28 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input29
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 29 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input3
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 3 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input30
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 30 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input31
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 31 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input32
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 32 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input33
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 33 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input34
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 34 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input35
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 35 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input36
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 36 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input37
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 37 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input38
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 38 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input39
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 39 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input4
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 4 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input40
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 40 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input41
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 41 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input42
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 42 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input43
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 43 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input44
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 44 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input45
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 45 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input46
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 46 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input47
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 47 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input48
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 48 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input49
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 49 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input5
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 5 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input6
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 6 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input7
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 7 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input8
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 8 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: Input9
metric.summary: The input tensor at index 9 represented in IEEE 754 double precision.

metric.name: ModelVersion
metric.summary: The version of the ML model used for execution. May not be a counter int.

metric.name: OptimizationTarget
