metric.summary: The OptimizationTarget associated with the ML model. This is an enum for classifying different ML models. It represents what an model is trying to optimize.

metric.name: OutputDelaySec
metric.summary: The time from last segment selection to when the UKM is recorded, in seconds.

metric.name: PredictionResult
metric.summary: Predicted result from executing the ML model. This is normally a score for determining whether to show a particular feature. The value is a floating number between 0 to 1 and encoded into IEEE 754 double precision here. This is being deprecated. Use PredictionResult1 instead. Will add deprecated flag once all the code are start using PredictionResult1.

metric.name: PredictionResult1
metric.summary: The 1st output when inferencing the ML model with input data. The value is a floating number between 0 to 1 and encoded into IEEE 754 double precision here.

metric.name: PredictionResult10
metric.summary: The 10th output when inferencing the ML model with input data. The value is a floating number between 0 to 1 and encoded into IEEE 754 double precision here.

metric.name: PredictionResult2
metric.summary: The 2nd output when inferencing the ML model with input data. The value is a floating number between 0 to 1 and encoded into IEEE 754 double precision here.

metric.name: PredictionResult3
metric.summary: The 3rd output when inferencing the ML model with input data. The value is a floating number between 0 to 1 and encoded into IEEE 754 double precision here.

metric.name: PredictionResult4
metric.summary: The 4th output when inferencing the ML model with input data. The value is a floating number between 0 to 1 and encoded into IEEE 754 double precision here.

metric.name: PredictionResult5
metric.summary: The 5th output when inferencing the ML model with input data. The value is a floating number between 0 to 1 and encoded into IEEE 754 double precision here.

metric.name: PredictionResult6
metric.summary: The 6th output when inferencing the ML model with input data. The value is a floating number between 0 to 1 and encoded into IEEE 754 double precision here.

metric.name: PredictionResult7
metric.summary: The 7th output when inferencing the ML model with input data. The value is a floating number between 0 to 1 and encoded into IEEE 754 double precision here.

metric.name: PredictionResult8
metric.summary: The 8th output when inferencing the ML model with input data. The value is a floating number between 0 to 1 and encoded into IEEE 754 double precision here.

metric.name: PredictionResult9
metric.summary: The 9th output when inferencing the ML model with input data. The value is a floating number between 0 to 1 and encoded into IEEE 754 double precision here.

metric.name: SelectionResult
metric.summary: The OptimizationTarget of the last selected segment before UKM is recorded. Some features might have several ML models with them. This metric records which model is currently selected for the feature.

event.name: ServiceWorker.MainResourceLoadCompleted
event.summary: Events taken when the main resource is loaded. This only occurs when the static routing API is used.

metric.name: ActualRouterSourceType
metric.summary: The actual router source type for Static Routing API. This indicates which source type was actually used to get the resource. This will be recorded only when the router has matched the incoming resource.

metric.name: MatchedFirstRouterSourceType
metric.summary: The result of the router evaluation of the Static Routing API. This indicates which source type is specified to get the resource. This will be recorded only when the router has matched the incoming resource.

metric.name: RouterEvaluationTime
metric.summary: Time spent for the SW static routing API router evaluation to handle a request. Recorded when the service worker has associated routing info, and the browser or renderer is processing requests. Duration in units of micro seconds.

metric.name: RouterRuleCount
metric.summary: The sum of registered router rules with the Static Routing API in main resource. Recorded per navigation when the service worker, which has associated routing info, is activated. This is rounded down to the nearest exponential bucket (with a bucket ratio on 1.15).

metric.name: WorkerStatusOnEvaluation
metric.summary: ServiceWorker running status at the time when router evaluation is conducted.

event.name: ServiceWorker.OnLoad
event.summary: Events taken at the end of the loading pages controlled by the service workers. It means that the metrics is recorded only if the loading is successfully completed.

metric.name: AudioFallback
metric.summary: True if there are network fallback sub-resources of audios in the page. (boolean value. true: 1 or false: 0)

metric.name: AudioHandled
metric.summary: True if there are service workers handling sub-resource loads of audios in the page. (boolean value. true: 1 or false: 0)

metric.name: CSSStyleSheetFallback
metric.summary: True if there are network fallback sub-resources of CSSStyleSheet in the page. (boolean value. true: 1 or false: 0)

metric.name: CSSStyleSheetHandled
metric.summary: True if there are service workers handling sub-resource loads of CSSStyleSheet in the page. (boolean value. true: 1 or false: 0)

metric.name: DictionaryFallback
metric.summary: True if there are network fallback sub-resources of Dictionary in the page. (boolean value. true: 1 or false: 0)

metric.name: DictionaryHandled
metric.summary: True if there are service workers handling sub-resource loads of Dictionary in the page. (boolean value. true: 1 or false: 0)

metric.name: FontFallback
metric.summary: True if there are network fallback sub-resources of CSSStyleSheet in the page. (boolean value. true: 1 or false: 0)

metric.name: FontHandled
metric.summary: True if there are service workers handling sub-resource loads of fonts in the page. (boolean value. true: 1 or false: 0)

metric.name: ImageFallback
metric.summary: True if there are network fallback sub-resources of images in the page. (boolean value. true: 1 or false: 0)

metric.name: ImageHandled
metric.summary: True if there are service workers handling sub-resource loads of images in the page. (boolean value. true: 1 or false: 0)

metric.name: LinkPrefetchFallback
metric.summary: True if there are network fallback sub-resources of link prefetches in the page. (boolean value. true: 1 or false: 0)

metric.name: LinkPrefetchHandled
metric.summary: True if there are service workers handling sub-resource loads of link prefetches in the page. (boolean value. true: 1 or false: 0)

metric.name: MainAndSubResourceLoadLocation
metric.summary: Enum value to represent how main / sub resource are loaded.

metric.name: ManifestFallback
metric.summary: True if there are network fallback sub-resources of manifests in the page. (boolean value. true: 1 or false: 0)

metric.name: ManifestHandled
metric.summary: True if there are service workers handling sub-resource loads of manifests in the page. (boolean value. true: 1 or false: 0)

metric.name: MatchedCacheRouterSourceCount
metric.summary: Total number of sub-resources which were matched to `RouterSourceEnum.cache` in ServiceWorker Static Routing API, from navigation start until onload event. This is rounded down to the nearest exponential bucket (with a bucket ratio on 1.15).

metric.name: MatchedFetchEventRouterSourceCount
metric.summary: Total number of sub-resources which were matched to `RouterSourceEnum.fetch-event` in ServiceWorker Static Routing API, from navigation start until onload event. This is rounded down to the nearest exponential bucket (with a bucket ratio on 1.15).

metric.name: MatchedNetworkRouterSourceCount
metric.summary: Total number of sub-resources which were matched to `RouterSourceEnum.network` in ServiceWorker Static Routing API, from navigation start until onload event. This is rounded down to the nearest exponential bucket (with a bucket ratio on 1.15).

metric.name: MatchedRaceNetworkAndFetchRouterSourceCount
metric.summary: Total number of sub-resources which were matched to the `RouterSourceEnum.race-network-and-fetch-handler` in ServiceWorker Static Routing API, from navigation start till onload event. This is rounded down to the nearest exponential bucket (with a bucket ratio on 1.15).

metric.name: ScriptFallback
metric.summary: True if there are network fallback sub-resources of CSSStyleSheet in the page. (boolean value. true: 1 or false: 0)

metric.name: ScriptHandled
metric.summary: True if there are service workers handling sub-resource loads of scripts in the page. (boolean value. true: 1 or false: 0)

metric.name: SpeculationRulesFallback
metric.summary: True if there are network fallback sub-resources of speculation rules in the page. (boolean value. true: 1 or false: 0)

metric.name: SpeculationRulesHandled
metric.summary: True if there are service workers handling sub-resource loads of speculation rules in the page. (boolean value. true: 1 or false: 0)

metric.name: SubResourceFallbackRatio
metric.summary: An integer ratio (0 - 100) to represent the ratio of network fallbacks from the total subresource load.

metric.name: SVGDocumentFallback
metric.summary: True if there are network fallback sub-resources of SVG documents in the page. (boolean value. true: 1 or false: 0)

metric.name: SVGDocumentHandled
metric.summary: True if there are service workers handling sub-resource loads of SVG documents in the page. (boolean value. true: 1 or false: 0)

metric.name: TextTrackFallback
metric.summary: True if there are network fallback sub-resources of text tracks in the page. (boolean value. true: 1 or false: 0)

metric.name: TextTrackHandled
metric.summary: True if there are service workers handling sub-resource loads of text tracks in the page. (boolean value. true: 1 or false: 0)

metric.name: TotalSubResourceFallback
metric.summary: The number of network fallbacks during sub resource load. This is rounded down to the nearest exponential bucket (with a bucket ratio on 1.15).

metric.name: TotalSubResourceLoad
metric.summary: The number of all sub resource load of the page controlled by the service worker. This is rounded down to the nearest exponential bucket (with a bucket ratio on 1.15).

metric.name: VideoFallback
metric.summary: True if there are network fallback sub-resources of videos in the page. (boolean value. true: 1 or false: 0)

metric.name: VideoHandled
metric.summary: True if there are service workers handling sub-resource loads of videos in the page. (boolean value. true: 1 or false: 0)

metric.name: XSLStyleSheetFallback
metric.summary: True if there are network fallback sub-resources of XSLStyleSheet in the page. (boolean value. true: 1 or false: 0)

metric.name: XSLStyleSheetHandled
metric.summary: True if there are service workers handling sub-resource loads of XSLStyleSheet in the page. (boolean value. true: 1 or false: 0)

event.name: SharedHighlights.LinkGenerated
event.summary: Event representing the user action of selecting a piece of text on a page and selecting the option to create a link which would contain the necessary text fragment information to link back to this specific part of the page.

metric.name: Error
metric.summary: Record the type of error which prevented a successful link generation. Only recorded if the link creation failed.

metric.name: Success
metric.summary: Whether the generation of a link with text fragments was successful or not.

event.name: SharedHighlights.LinkGenerated.Requested
event.summary: Event representing the user action of selecting a piece of text on a page and selecting the option to create a link which would contain the necessary text fragment information to link back to this specific part of the page.

metric.name: Error
metric.summary: Record the type of error which prevented offering link to text to user. Only recorded if the link was not offered to the user.

metric.name: Success
metric.summary: Whether the link with text fragments was successful offered to the user or not.

event.name: SharedHighlights.LinkOpened
event.summary: Event representing the browser action of highlighting (incl. scroll-to) text fragments that were part of the URL. This happens only once during page load.

metric.name: Source
metric.summary: Record the type of source for the current navigation, which has text fragments. At the moment, this maps to identifying whether the source is a known search engine or not.

metric.name: Success
metric.summary: Whether the highlighting of a set of text fragments was a complete success or not.

event.name: Sharing.ClickToCall
event.summary: Collected when a user finishes a Click to Call journey.

metric.name: EntryPoint
metric.summary: Specifies the entry point of the user journey (i.e. left click, right click, text selection).

metric.name: HasApps
metric.summary: Boolean value indicating whether any (>0) apps are available that the call could be sent to.

metric.name: HasDevices
metric.summary: Boolean value indicating whether any (>0) devices are available that the call could be sent to. These belong to the same account and are running Android.

metric.name: Selection
metric.summary: Whether the user selected a device, an app or nothing (i.e. dismissal) in the user interface. For some entry points this might always be the same value (e.g. right clicks always select devices).

event.name: Shopping.AddToCartDetection
event.summary: Metrics related to DOM-based AddToCart button detection in ChromeCart.

metric.name: HeuristicsExecutionTime
metric.summary: Logged when DOM-based AddToCart button detection heuristic is executed and records the execution time. This is expressed in microseconds.

event.name: Shopping.CartExtraction
event.summary: Metrics related to performance of cart content extraction in ChromeCart.

metric.name: ExtractionElapsedTime
metric.summary: Logged when cart content extraction is executed for ChromeCart and records the elapsed time within the JavaScript, so script parsing/compilation time is excluded. Shopping.CartExtraction.ExtractionExecutionTime is measured in native, and is the end-to-end elapsed time. This is expressed in milliseconds.

metric.name: ExtractionExecutionTime
metric.summary: Logged when cart content extraction is executed for ChromeCart and records the elapsed time between the start and the end of the extraction JavaScript request execution. This includes script parsing/compilation time and sleeping time. This is expressed in milliseconds.

metric.name: ExtractionLongestTaskTime
metric.summary: Logged when cart content extraction is executed for ChromeCart and records the time span of the longest task blocking the main thread of the renderer process. This is expressed in milliseconds.

metric.name: ExtractionTimedOut
metric.summary: Logged when cart content extraction is executed for ChromeCart and records whether the cart content extraction has timed out.

metric.name: ExtractionTotalTasksTime
metric.summary: Logged when cart content extraction is executed for ChromeCart and records the time spent on execution, excluding sleeping time. This is expressed in milliseconds.

event.name: Shopping.ChromeCart
event.summary: Metrics related to Chrome cart module in Chrome NTP.

metric.name: VisitCart
metric.summary: Always true. Recorded when users have visited cart page from Chrome cart module.

event.name: Shopping.FormSubmitted
event.summary: Recorded when user submits a form.

metric.name: IsTransaction
metric.summary: Whether the form submission is a shopping transaction. This has 50% chance randomized.

event.name: Shopping.MerchantTrust.DataAvailable
event.summary: Recorded when user finishes a navigation while the merchant trust data is available.

metric.name: DataAvailable
metric.summary: A boolean signaling that data is available. Only records true values.

event.name: Shopping.MerchantTrust.MessageClicked
event.summary: Recorded when a merchant trust message is clicked by the user.

metric.name: HasOccurred
metric.summary: A boolean signaling that message is clicked. Only records true values.

event.name: Shopping.MerchantTrust.MessageSeen
event.summary: Recorded when a merchant trust message is displayed to the user.

metric.name: HasOccurred
metric.summary: A boolean signaling that message is displayed. Only records true values.

event.name: Shopping.MerchantTrust.RowClicked
event.summary: Recorded when a store info row in trusted surface is clicked by the user.

metric.name: HasOccurred
metric.summary: A boolean signaling that store info row is clicked. Only records true values.

event.name: Shopping.MerchantTrust.RowSeen
event.summary: Recorded when a store info row in trusted surface is displayed to the user.

metric.name: HasOccurred
metric.summary: A boolean signaling that store info row is displayed. Only records true values.

event.name: Shopping.PDPStateWithLocalInfo
event.summary: Records if and how a page has been determined to be a product details page. It is recorded per eligible navigation (e.g. limited to main frame navigation, http/https check, etc), and is recorded after the page has finished loading.

metric.name: PDPState
metric.summary: The state of if and how the page has been determined to be a product details page.

event.name: Shopping.ShoppingAction
event.summary: Recorded when an user takes action on shopping features in Chrome in the current page.

metric.name: DiscountCopied
metric.summary: Whether the user has clicked in the discount bubble to copy the discount code.

metric.name: DiscountOpened
metric.summary: Whether the user has clicked and expanded the discount bubble in omnibox.

metric.name: PriceInsightsOpened
metric.summary: Whether the user has clicked the page action entry point to request to show the price insights.

metric.name: PriceTracked
metric.summary: Whether the user has tracked the product on the current page using any of the on-page entry points.

event.name: Shopping.ShoppingInformation
event.summary: Recorded when an user navigates to a shopping-related page and reports shopping-related information about this page.

metric.name: HasDiscount
metric.summary: Whether a discount from Chrome is available for this page.

metric.name: HasPriceInsights
metric.summary: Whether price insights is available for this page.

metric.name: IsPriceTrackable
metric.summary: Whether the page is price trackable.

metric.name: IsShoppingContent
metric.summary: Whether the page is classified as shopping content.

metric.name: PromotedFeature
metric.summary: Which, if any, contextual shopping feature is promoted in expanded state or in-product highlight.

event.name: Shopping.WillSendRequest
event.summary: Recorded when an XHR is about to be sent.

metric.name: IsAddToCart
metric.summary: Whether the XHR is to add a product to a shopping cart. This has 50% chance randomized.

event.name: Site.Install
event.summary: Records various metrics about whether a particular site has been installed as a PWA or not.

metric.name: IsFullyInstalled
metric.summary: Measured if the site matches the scope of any installed PWAs present in the profile.

metric.name: IsPartiallyInstalled
metric.summary: Measured if the app is partially installed via sync. This means that the user has installed the app on another device, and it exists in their sync profile, but it is not 'fully' installed on this device. This is expected to be a strong signal that the user will want to install the app fully on this device, as they already have it installed elsewhere.

event.name: Site.Manifest
event.summary: Records various signals regarding the fields present inside a manifest. If the DisplayMode is undefined, HasStartUrl is kEmpty, and all the other fields are false, then that denotes a site that has no manifest. For all other use-cases, the existence of a manifest is confirmed.

metric.name: DisplayMode
metric.summary: Records the display mode populated inside the manifest as per the manifest specifications. If it does not exist, this is measured as Undefined.

metric.name: HasBackgroundColor
metric.summary: Records whether the manifest has the background_color field populated.

metric.name: HasIconsAny
metric.summary: Records whether the manifest has icons of purpose ANY.

metric.name: HasIconsMaskable
metric.summary: Records whether the manifest has maskable icons.

metric.name: HasName
metric.summary: Records whether the manifest has the name field populated.

metric.name: HasScreenshots
metric.summary: Records whether the manifest has valid screenshots specified.

metric.name: HasStartUrl
metric.summary: Records whether the manifest has a valid URL in the start_url field.

metric.name: HasThemeColor
metric.summary: Records whether the manifest has the theme_color field populated.

event.name: Site.Quality
event.summary: Records various signals about a site that boost its chances of being installed as a web app, like valid fetch handlers (boosting offline capabilities), service worker data usage and whether the site has favicons.

metric.name: CacheStorageSize
metric.summary: Measures the service worker cache storage size as determined by the Quota system for a site in bytes.

metric.name: HasFavicons
metric.summary: Records whether the site has non-default favicons or not. Non-default favicons are those which are either explicitly set in the client side, or are icons obtained from the manifest. The default favicon set by chrome, that follows document_url/favicon.ico is not counted as part of this metric.

metric.name: HasFetchHandler
metric.summary: Records if the service worker exists and has a valid fetch handler.

metric.name: ServiceWorkerScriptSize
metric.summary: Measured the size of the service worker script in bytes. If the service worker does not exist, then a script size of 0 is measured.

event.name: SiteFamiliarityHeuristicResult
event.summary: Recorded when a toplevel page navigates. This records which user-page-familiarity-heuristics would trigger for a given site, if any.

metric.name: AnyHeuristicsMatch
metric.summary: Whether any familiarity heuristics would trigger on the site.

metric.name: OnHighConfidenceAllowlist
metric.summary: Whether the site is on the safe browsing high confidence allowlist.

metric.name: SiteEngagementScore
metric.summary: Site engagement score in the range [0, 100], rounded down to a multiple of 10 to limit granularity.

metric.name: SiteFamiliarityHistoryHeuristic
metric.summary: The most stringent navigation-history related familiarity heuristic that would trigger on the site. If no navigation-history heuristic matches then the value NO_HEURISTIC_MATCH is recorded.

event.name: SiteInstance
event.summary: Records how likely a tab can share processes with other tabs.

metric.name: NewProcessUsedForNavigationWhenSameSiteProcessExists
metric.summary: Records whether a new process is used for navigation when there are other same site process(es). Recorded for the outer-most main frame navigation when navigation is committed (cross-origin redirects are not recorded).

event.name: SmartCharging
event.summary: Records user activities that are related to charging such as battery percentage, time, device type, etc.

metric.name: BatteryPercentage
metric.summary: Percentage of the battery.

metric.name: BatteryPercentageBeforeLastCharge
metric.summary: The percentage of the battery at the beginning of the last charge.

metric.name: BatteryPercentageOfLastCharge
metric.summary: The percentage of the battery that the last charge reached.

metric.name: DayOfMonth
metric.summary: Event's day of the month (e.g. 1-31) in local time zone.

metric.name: DayOfWeek
metric.summary: Event's day of the week in local time zone. Sunday is 0.

metric.name: DeviceMode
metric.summary: Mode of the device (e.g. closed lid, tablet).

metric.name: DeviceType
metric.summary: Type of the device (e.g. laptop, tablet).

metric.name: DurationOfLastCharge
metric.summary: Duratioin of the last time the device was charged in minutes.

metric.name: DurationRecentAudioPlaying
metric.summary: Duration of audio playing in the last 30 minutes (bucketized and in minutes).

metric.name: DurationRecentVideoPlaying
metric.summary: Duration of video playing in the last 30 minutes (bucketized and in minutes).

metric.name: EventId
metric.summary: A unique number that represent order of an event.

metric.name: HaltFromLastCharge
metric.summary: Whether there's any shutdown/suspend actions between the last charge and current event.

metric.name: IsCharging
metric.summary: Whether the device is being charged or not.

metric.name: Month
metric.summary: Event's month (e.g. Jan-Dec) in local time zone. January is 1.

metric.name: NumRecentKeyEvents
metric.summary: Number of key events in past 30 minutes.

metric.name: NumRecentMouseEvents
metric.summary: Number of mouse events in past 30 minutes.

metric.name: NumRecentStylusEvents
metric.summary: Number of stylus events in past 30 minutes.

metric.name: NumRecentTouchEvents
metric.summary: Number of touch events in past 30 minutes.

metric.name: Reason
metric.summary: Reason for the logging of an event.

metric.name: ScreenBrightnessPercent
metric.summary: Brightness of the screen in percent.

metric.name: TimeOfTheDay
metric.summary: Time of the event in minutes since midnight in the local time zone.

metric.name: TimeSinceLastCharge
metric.summary: Time since the last time user unplugged the charger in minutes.

metric.name: TimezoneDifferenceFromLastCharge
metric.summary: Timezone difference from the last charge. It is equal to current_timezone - timezone_from_last_charge. The valid range of time zone value will be -12 (UTC -12:00) to +14 (UTC +14:00).

metric.name: Voltage
metric.summary: Charge voltage in mV.

event.name: SMSReceiver
event.summary: Records performance metrics for SMSReceiver API.

metric.name: IsCrossOriginFrame
metric.summary: Flag indicating whether the report comes from a cross-origin frame or not.

metric.name: Outcome
metric.summary: Records the result of a call to the SmsReceiver API.

metric.name: SmsParsingStatus
metric.summary: Records the status of parsing an incoming SMS when using the WebOTP API. It records one sample per incoming SMS.

metric.name: TimeSmsReceiveMs
metric.summary: Records the duration from when the API is called to when an SMS has been successfully received.

metric.name: TimeSuccessMs
metric.summary: Records the duration from when the API is called to when the user presses "Allow" to pass the incoming SMS to the site and proceed with the SMS verification flow.

metric.name: TimeUserCancelMs
metric.summary: Records the duration from when the API is called to when the user presses "Deny" to terminate the SMS verification flow.

event.name: SoftNavigation
event.summary: Records soft navigation metrics including LCP, CLS, INP. Soft navigation is DOM changes that are perceived to be navigation, but there is no page load. Soft navigation metrics like LCP are equivalent to their counterpart of the PageLoad event.

metric.name: InteractiveTiming.INPOffset
metric.summary: One-based offset in the list of all user interactions of the interaction which was the INP (https://web.dev/inp).

metric.name: InteractiveTiming.INPTime
metric.summary: The time (in milliseconds from navigation start) of the interaction which was the INP (https://web.dev/inp).

metric.name: InteractiveTiming.NumInteractions
metric.summary: The number of distinct user interactions from the time when the soft navigation happens to the time when the next soft navigation happens or the page load end. See definition of user interaction: https://web.dev/better-responsiveness-metric/#group-events-into-interactions

metric.name: InteractiveTiming.UserInteractionLatency.HighPercentile2.MaxEventDuration
metric.summary: An approximation of a high percentile of user interaction latency from the time when the soft navigation happens to the time when the next soft navigation happens or the page load end, in ms. For this metric, we measure the latency of user interactions by Responsiveness.UserInteraction.MaxEventDuration.

metric.name: LayoutInstability.MaxCumulativeShiftScore.SessionWindow.Gap1000ms.Max5000ms
metric.summary: Measures the maximum cumulative layout shift (bit.ly/cls-explainer) that has occurred from the time when the soft navigation happens to the time when the next soft navigation happens or the page load ends in a session window. The gap between two consecutive shifts in a window is not bigger than 1000ms and the maximum window size is 5000ms. This metric's integral value is 100x the fractional cumulative layout shift score described in the explainer.

metric.name: NavigationId
metric.summary: Records the navigation id of a soft navigation. It is used to differentiate soft navigations.

metric.name: PaintTiming.LargestContentfulPaint
metric.summary: Measures the LargestContentfulPaint in milliseconds from the start time of the soft navigation to the LCP time.

metric.name: PaintTiming.LargestContentfulPaintBPP
metric.summary: Measures the ratio of image file size (in bits) to displayed pixels for the LCP candidate. Bucketed into roughly exponential intervals to account for the wide range of encountered values (less than 1:100000 to greater than 10000:1). This metric is recorded whenever the LCP candidate is an image, and omitted when it is text.

metric.name: PaintTiming.LargestContentfulPaintImageDiscoveryTime
metric.summary: Measures the time in milliseconds from soft navigation's start time to the time when the image LCP element is requested. This metric is not recorded when the LCP element is text or video.

metric.name: PaintTiming.LargestContentfulPaintImageLoadEnd
metric.summary: Measures the time in milliseconds from soft navigation's start time to the time when the image LCP element finishes loading. For animated images, this is when the image resource finishes loading completely, not the first frame time. See web.dev/optimize-lcp for more details. This metric is not recorded when the LCP element is text or video.

metric.name: PaintTiming.LargestContentfulPaintImageLoadStart
metric.summary: Measures the time in milliseconds from soft navigation's start time to the time when the image LCP element starts loading. See web.dev/optimize-lcp for more details. This metric is not recorded when the LCP element is text or video.

metric.name: PaintTiming.LargestContentfulPaintRequestPriority
metric.summary: Resource loading priority used for the LCP candidate. This metric is recorded whenever the LCP candidate is an image, and omitted when it is text or video.

metric.name: PaintTiming.LargestContentfulPaintType
metric.summary: Indicates that type of the LargestContentfulPaint candidate. This value is a packed set of flags, defined in third_party/blink/public/common/performance/largest_contentful_paint_type.h

metric.name: StartTime
metric.summary: Records the start time of a soft navigation. The start time is a time delta in milliseconds relative to the last hard navigation time. It is used to analyze the frequency of soft navigations.

event.name: SubresourceFilter
event.summary: Recorded for page loads where subresource filtering policy was evaluated.

metric.name: ActivationDecision
metric.summary: Enum that records the policy decision to activate subresource filtering for a page load. 0 = Unknown, 1 = Activated, 2 = Disabled, 3 = Deprecated value, 4 = URL whitelisted, 5 = Activation conditions not met. 'Activated' indicates that subresource filtering was activated. All other reasons indicate that subresource filtering was not activated.

metric.name: DryRun
metric.summary: Records '1' if subresource filtering was activated in dry run mode. In dry run mode, subresource filtering policy is evaluated against resources loaded on the page, but subresource filtering is not actually applied.

metric.name: EnforcementRedirectPosition
metric.summary: An enum denoting where in the redirect chain there was a URL that matched one of the subresource_filter activation lists, and wasn't marked as warning. See subresource_filter::RedirectPosition for values.

event.name: SubresourceLoad.ZstdContentEncoding
event.summary: Event recorded when a response is received that is content-encoded with zstd and is a subresource load.

metric.name: UsedZstd
metric.summary: Set to 1 if the subresource served zstd-compressed content.

event.name: SubresourceRedirect.PublicSrcVideoCompression
event.summary: Records coverage information for src videos. Recorded once for every src video that is fetched from network.

metric.name: FullContentLength
metric.summary: The full content length of the video retrieved from the first range request for the resource. This full content length represents the total bytes when the entire video is played. The bytes are rounded down to the exponential bucket with factor of 1.3.

metric.name: SubresourceRedirectResult
metric.summary: Enumerates the different results possible for subresource redirection, such as redirectable or different reasons of ineligibility.

event.name: Tab.Screenshot
event.summary: User took one or more screenshots of a page.

metric.name: HasOccurred
metric.summary: A boolean signaling that the event has occurred; only records true values.

event.name: TabManager.Background.FirstAlertFired
event.summary: Collects the duration in MS from when the tab is backgrounded to when a JavaScript alert is fired. Only recorded when the tab is in the background.

metric.name: IsMainFrame
metric.summary: Indicates whether the alert is fired from main frame.

metric.name: TimeFromBackgrounded
metric.summary: Duration in MS from when the tab is backgrounded to when alert is fired.

event.name: TabManager.Background.FirstAudioStarts
event.summary: Collects the duration in MS from when the tab is backgrounded to when audio starts to play. Only recorded when the tab is in the background.

metric.name: IsMainFrame
metric.summary: Indicates whether the audio stream belongs to the main frame.

metric.name: TimeFromBackgrounded
metric.summary: Duration in MS from when the tab is backgrounded to when audio starts to play.

event.name: TabManager.Background.FirstFaviconUpdated
event.summary: Collects the duration in MS from when the tab is backgrounded to when the favicon is updated. Only recorded when the tab is in the background.

metric.name: TimeFromBackgrounded
metric.summary: Duration in MS from when the tab is backgrounded to when the favicon is updated.

metric.name: IsMainFrame
metric.summary: Indicates whether the notification creation is from the main frame.

metric.name: TimeFromBackgrounded
metric.summary: Duration in MS from when the tab is backgrounded to when a non-persistent notification is created.

event.name: TabManager.Background.FirstTitleUpdated
event.summary: Collects the duration in MS from when the tab is backgrounded to when the title is updated. Only recorded when the tab is in the background.

metric.name: TimeFromBackgrounded
metric.summary: Duration in MS from when the tab is backgrounded to when the title is updated.

event.name: TabManager.Background.ForegroundedOrClosed
event.summary: Collects the duration in MS from when the tab is backgrounded to when it is brought to foreground or closed.

metric.name: IsDiscarded
metric.summary: Boolean value indicating whether or not the tab is discarded since it was backgrounded.

metric.name: IsForegrounded
metric.summary: Boolean value indicating whether or not it's triggered because the tab is brought to foreground again.

metric.name: LabelId
metric.summary: An int64 random number generated when TabManager.TabMetric is logged. The same number is also logged here, so that feature label can be paired.

metric.name: TimeFromBackgrounded
metric.summary: Duration in MS from when the tab is backgrounded to when it is brought to foreground or closed.

event.name: TabManager.Experimental.SessionRestore.TabSwitchLoadStopped
event.summary: Collects data when a tab is switched to from another tab and then finishes loading in the foreground during session restore The event is only recorded when a tab is switched to from another tab within the same tabstrip. As a result, the initial foreground tab is not included in this event since it was not switched to from another tab. The event is not recorded when the session overlaps with background tab opening.

metric.name: SequenceId
metric.summary: The ID of this event's sequence in current session restore session.

metric.name: SessionRestoreSessionId
metric.summary: The ID of this session restore session.

metric.name: SessionRestoreTabCount
metric.summary: Number of tabs that are restored.

metric.name: SystemTabCount
metric.summary: Number of all tabs of the system, which includes all browser windows.

metric.name: TabSwitchLoadTime
metric.summary: Tab switch load time in MS. It is defined as the time between when the user switches to a background tab, and the time when that tab finishes loading in the foreground. If the user switches away before the tab finishes loading, a metric will not be recorded unless the user switches back, in which case the tab switch load time is measured from that point in time.

event.name: TabManager.LifecycleStateChange
event.summary: Collects information about when and why a lifecycle state change was attempted, and information about the outcome of the attempt.

metric.name: FailureGlobalDisallowlist
metric.summary: Boolean indicating that the tab was explicitly opted out of the intervention via the global disallowlist.

metric.name: FailureHeuristicAudio
metric.summary: Boolean indicating that the intervention was disallowed because the origin has historically been observed emitting audio while in the background.

metric.name: FailureHeuristicFavicon
metric.summary: Boolean indicating that the intervention was disallowed because the origin has historically been observed updating its favicon while in the background.

metric.name: FailureHeuristicInsufficientObservation
metric.summary: Boolean indicating that the site is still being observed locally in order to determine which features are in use. In the meantime, it is protected from this intervention.

metric.name: FailureHeuristicNotifications
metric.summary: Boolean indicating that the intervention was disallowed because the origin has historically been observed making use of notifications while in the background.

metric.name: FailureHeuristicTitle
metric.summary: Boolean indicating that the intervention was disallowed because the origin has historically been observed updating its tab title while in the background.

metric.name: FailureLifecyclesEnterprisePolicyOptOut
metric.summary: Boolean indicating that the browser was explicitly opted out of the intervention via enterprise policy.

metric.name: FailureLivePictureInPicture
metric.summary: Boolean indicating that the intervention was disallowed because the tab is displaying content in picture-in-picture.

metric.name: FailureLiveStateCapturing
metric.summary: Boolean indicating that the intervention was disallowed because the tab is currently capturing user media (making use of webcam or microphone, for example).

metric.name: FailureLiveStateDesktopCapture
metric.summary: Boolean indicating that the intervention was disallowed because the tab is currently capturing a window or screen.

metric.name: FailureLiveStateDevToolsOpen
metric.summary: Boolean indicating that the intervention was disallowed because the tab is currently using DevTools.

metric.name: FailureLiveStateExtensionDisallowed
metric.summary: Boolean indicating that the intervention was disallowed by an extension.

metric.name: FailureLiveStateFormEntry
metric.summary: Boolean indicating that the intervention was disallowed because the tab currently contains text form entry.

metric.name: FailureLiveStateHasNotificationsPermission
metric.summary: Boolean indicating that the intervention was disallowed because the tab has the permission to use notifications.

metric.name: FailureLiveStateIsPDF
metric.summary: Boolean indicating that the intervention was disallowed because the tab is currently hosting a PDF.

metric.name: FailureLiveStateMirroring
metric.summary: Boolean indicating that the intervention was disallowed because the tab is currently being mirrored (casting its contents, for example).

metric.name: FailureLiveStatePlayingAudio
metric.summary: Boolean indicating that the intervention was disallowed because the tab is currently playing audio.

metric.name: FailureLiveStateSharingBrowsingInstance
metric.summary: Boolean indicating that the intervention was disallowed because the tab is sharing its BrowsingInstance with at least one other tab.

metric.name: FailureLiveStateUsingBluetooth
metric.summary: Boolean indicating that the intervention was disallowed because the tab is currently connected to at least one bluetooth device.

metric.name: FailureLiveStateUsingIndexedDBLock
metric.summary: Boolean indicating that the intervention was disallowed because the tab is currently holding at least one IndexedDB lock.

metric.name: FailureLiveStateUsingWebLock
metric.summary: Boolean indicating that the intervention was disallowed because the tab is currently holding at least one WebLock.

metric.name: FailureLiveStateUsingWebSockets
metric.summary: Boolean indicating that the intervention was disallowed because the tab is currently using WebSockets.

metric.name: FailureLiveStateUsingWebUSB
metric.summary: Boolean indicating that the intervention was disallowed because the tab is currently using WebUSB.

metric.name: FailureLiveStateVisible
metric.summary: Boolean indicating that the intervention was disallowed because the tab is currently visible.

metric.name: FailureLiveWebApp
metric.summary: Boolean indicating that the intervention was disallowed because the tab is a web application window.

metric.name: FailureOriginTrialOptOut
metric.summary: Boolean indicating that the tab explicitly opted out of the intervention via origin trial.

metric.name: FailureOriginTrialUnknown
metric.summary: Boolean indicating that the tab did not report its origin trial opt-in/opt-out yet.

metric.name: LifecycleStateChangeReason
metric.summary: The reason the lifecycle state change was attempted. This corresponds to the LifecycleStateChangeReason enumeration in lifecycle_unit_state.mojom.

metric.name: NewLifecycleState
metric.summary: The lifecycle state the tab will be in after the state change. Corresponds to a value from the LifecycleUnitState enum defined in lifecycle_unit_state.mojom.

metric.name: OldLifecycleState
metric.summary: The lifecycle state the tab was in prior to the state change. Corresponds to a value from the LifecycleUnitState enum defined in lifecycle_unit_state.mojom.

metric.name: Outcome
metric.summary: Whether or not the state change was successful. The various Failure and Success booleans encode the actual reasons for this outcome. Note that multiple success and failure reasons may be encoded for a single decision. The presence of any failure bit indicates a negative outcome. If only success bits are present the outcome is positive.

metric.name: SuccessGlobalAllowlist
metric.summary: Boolean indicating that the tab was explicitly opted in to the intervention via the global allowlist.

metric.name: SuccessHeuristic
metric.summary: Boolean indicating that the tab was deemed safe for the intervention via local observations and heuristics.

metric.name: SuccessOriginTrialOptIn
metric.summary: Boolean indicating that the tab explicitly opted in to the intervention via origin trial.

metric.name: TimeSinceNavigationMs
metric.summary: The time elapsed (in milliseconds) since the top-level navigation was committed that loaded this tab's content.

metric.name: TimeSinceVisibilityStateChangeMs
metric.summary: The amount of time that has passed (in milliseconds) since the last visibility state change.

metric.name: TransitionForced
metric.summary: This is set if the transition to the new lifecycle state was forced by the browser because the content took too long to respond to the lifecycle event.

metric.name: VisibilityState
metric.summary: The current visibility state. This is an integer value corresponding to the enumeration in content/public/browser/visibility.h.

event.name: TabManager.SessionRestore.ForegroundTab.ExpectedTaskQueueingDurationInfo
event.summary: Collects the expected queueing duration of tasks and metadata for the foreground tab during session restore. The event is not recorded when the session overlaps with background tab opening. The event is not recorded when there is only one restored tab, in which case, there is no background restored tab and we are not interested at that. In this way, we cap the volume while keeping all interesting data.

metric.name: ExpectedTaskQueueingDuration
metric.summary: Duration in MS for expected task queueing duration. The metric reflects the responsiveness of a tab. A lower value means the tab will respond to inputs faster. This metric is equal to RendererScheduler.ExpectedTaskQueueingDuration. It is emitted once for all tasks in each 1000-ms window.

metric.name: SequenceId
metric.summary: The ID of this event's sequence in current session restore session.

metric.name: SessionRestoreSessionId
metric.summary: The ID of this session restore session.

metric.name: SessionRestoreTabCount
metric.summary: Number of tabs that are restored.

metric.name: SystemTabCount
metric.summary: Number of all tabs of the system, which includes all browser windows.

event.name: TabManager.TabLifetime
event.summary: Collected when a tab is closed, at most once per source.

metric.name: TimeSinceNavigation
metric.summary: The time when the tab was closed, expressed as the amount of time in MS that has elapsed since the main frame navigation providing the content of the tab.

event.name: TabManager.WindowMetrics
event.summary: Collects information about a browser or app window to associate with the TabManager.TabMetrics entries for tabs in that window.

metric.name: IsActive
metric.summary: Whether the window is the active (frontmost) window.

metric.name: ShowState
metric.summary: Enumeration of the window show state, such as fullscreen or minimized. Values are enumerated in metrics::WindowMetricsEvent::ShowState.

metric.name: TabCount
metric.summary: Number of tabs in the tab strip. Rounded down to the nearest exponential bucket (with a bucket spacing factor of 1.5). Will be 1 for windows with only one top-level WebContents, such as app windows.

metric.name: Type
metric.summary: Enumeration for the type of the window. Values are enumerated in metrics::WindowMetricsEvent::Type.

metric.name: WindowId
metric.summary: Session ID of the browser or app window this entry represents, unique for the browsing session.

event.name: TabRevisitTracker.TabStateChange
event.summary: Recorded when a tab changes state to/from "active", "background", and "closed".

metric.name: NewState
metric.summary: The state the tab is in after the transition.

metric.name: NumTabsOnSameUrl
metric.summary: The count of tabs with the same main frame URL as this one in the current browser session. Used to identify instances of duplicate tabs. Bucketed in 20 buckets (0 to 20) + an overflow bucket.

metric.name: NumTotalRevisits
metric.summary: The count of times this tab was revisited since it was created. Bucketed in 20 buckets (0 to 20) + an overflow bucket.

metric.name: PreviousState
metric.summary: The state the tab was in before the transition.

metric.name: SameTabGroupAsActiveTab
metric.summary: Whether the tab is in the same tab group as the current active tab.

metric.name: SameWindowAsActiveTab
metric.summary: Whether the tab is in the same browser window as the current active tab.

metric.name: TimeInPreviousState
metric.summary: The time spent by the tab in `PreviousState`, before transitioning to `NewState`, in seconds.

metric.name: TotalTimeActive
metric.summary: The total time the tab has spent as the active tab since it was created, in seconds.

event.name: TabStripOrganization
event.summary: Collects information about UKMSourceIDs group affiliation in a tab strip when group organization actions are taken up to 30 SourceIDs.

metric.name: IsActiveTab
metric.summary: Whether this source is the active tab for the trigger, only recorded for 1 tab within all TabStripOrganization events recorded with the same TabStripEventID.

metric.name: MinutesSinceLastActive
metric.summary: Number of minutes since the tab was last active. exponentially bucketed using GetExponentialBucketMin with a spacing of 1.3

metric.name: SuggestedTabStripOrganizationReason
metric.summary: The trigger reason for when this tab strip event was taken.

metric.name: TabGroupID
metric.summary: The lower 64 bits of the tab group id corresponding to the TabStripEventID.

metric.name: TabStripEventID
metric.summary: An ID corresponding to the event that will enable joining all active tabs for the window and the event together.

event.name: ThirdPartyCookies.BreakageIndicator.HTTPError
event.summary: Recorded when there is an HTTP error that could be associated with breakage from 3PC blocking.

metric.name: TPCBlocked
metric.summary: Whether the site actually had any blocked 3P cookies.

metric.name: TPCBlockedInSettings
metric.summary: Whether the user had third party cookies blocked when the event occurred. This metric considers the global third party cookie setting as well as per-top-level-site content settings for opting out of third party cookie blocking, but not e.g. the Storage Access state of individual iframes.

event.name: ThirdPartyCookies.BreakageIndicator.UncaughtJSError
event.summary: Recorded when there is a JS error that could be associated with breakage from 3PC blocking.

metric.name: HasOccurred
metric.summary: A boolean signaling that the event has occurred; only records true values.

event.name: ThirdPartyCookies.BreakageIndicator.UserReload
event.summary: Recorded when there is a user reload event that could be associated with breakage from 3PC blocking.

metric.name: TPCBlocked
metric.summary: Whether the site actually had any blocked 3P cookies.

metric.name: TPCBlockedInSettings
metric.summary: Whether the user had third party cookies blocked when the event occurred. This metric considers the global third party cookie setting as well as per-top-level-site content settings for opting out of third party cookie blocking, but not e.g. the Storage Access state of individual iframes.

event.name: ThirdPartyCookies.CookieControlsActivated
event.summary: Recorded each time User Bypass is activated to bypass 3PC blocking and re-enable 3PC for the page. Only applicable if the user is blocking 3PC by default.

metric.name: FedCmInitiated
metric.summary: Whether the page attempted to call the FedCM API on the current page load, prior to the user re-enabling 3PC.

metric.name: PageRefreshCount
metric.summary: The number of times the user refreshed the current page (without navigating elsewhere) before re-enabling 3PC. An integer clamped to the range 0 to 10.

metric.name: RepeatedActivation
metric.summary: Whether the user has previously re-enabled 3PC on this page via the in-context controls.

metric.name: SiteEngagementLevel
metric.summary: The site engagement level of the page when the user re-enabled 3PC.

metric.name: StorageAccessAPIRequested
metric.summary: Whether the page had requested access to storage via the Storage Access API on the current page load, prior to the user re-enabling 3PC

metric.name: ThirdPartySiteDataAccessType
metric.summary: The state of site data access (e.g. blocked, allowed, etc) for the the current page load prior to the user re-enabling 3PC.

event.name: ThirdPartyCookies.TopLevelDeprecationTrial
event.summary: Recorded when there is a change in the status of the TopLevelTpcd deprecation trial.

metric.name: Enabled
metric.summary: True if the trial was enabled. False if the trial was disabled.

metric.name: MatchSubdomains
metric.summary: True iff the token used to enable the trial is/was valid for subdomains of the origin store in the token.

event.name: TopToolbar.Share
event.summary: User pressed the 'Share' in the top toolbar, which shares the current URL.

metric.name: HasOccurred
metric.summary: A boolean signaling that the event has occurred; only records true values.

event.name: TouchToFill.Shown
event.summary: Recorded when a user takes an action on the Touch To Fill sheet.

metric.name: UserAction
metric.summary: The action a user took when interacting with the Touch To Fill sheet.

event.name: TouchToFill.SubmissionReadiness
event.summary: Recorded when the Touch To Fill sheet is shown to the user.

metric.name: SubmissionReadiness
metric.summary: A given form's state (e.g. there are empty fields in the form, or there are only two fields and they both are filled by Chrome) that indicates whether the form is ready for submission. Recorded when a Touch To Fill sheet is shown for that form.

event.name: TouchToFill.TimeToSuccessfulLogin
event.summary: Recorded when a successful login is detected after filling by the Touch To Fill.

metric.name: TimeToSuccessfulLogin
metric.summary: The time in milliseconds between filling by the Touch To Fill and a successful submission (To ensure filling and submitting correspond to each other, the usernames should coincide and the timestamps should be within one minute. A successful login after a failed submission or manual typing is not reported). Used to compare the times when automated submission is enabled and disabled. Thus, we measure the time saved for a user. Recorded when the password manager detects that the login was successful.

event.name: Tpcd.Mitigations.Dt.FirstParty.Deployment2
event.summary: Recorded on a page load cadance for every third party cookie accessed.

metric.name: Deployed
metric.summary: Indicates when an origin deployed its 1P (AKA First Party, top-level) DT Token and thus was granted cookie access.

event.name: Translate
event.summary: Metrics related to a Translate event. These metrics are described in TranslateEventProto.

event.name: TranslatePageLoad
event.summary: Summary of the user's interaction with Translate from the beginning of the page load to when the event is recorded. This event is recorded when a page load is completed and every time that Chrome is backgrounded during the course of the page load. This means we can record multiple events for a single page load, but it guarantees that we will still record data in instances where the user backgrounds then kills Chrome.

metric.name: FinalSourceLanguage
metric.summary: The source language at the time the event is recorded. In most cases this will match the initial source language, but the user can change the source language manually if they want. Note that the semantic_type attribute is included in order to remain consistent with the previous Translate UKM proto.

metric.name: FinalState
metric.summary: At the time this event is recorded, the state of Translate. This includes whether the page is translated or not and if no UI is shown, just the omnibox icon is shown, or the full translate UI is shown.

metric.name: FinalTargetLanguage
metric.summary: The target language at the time the event is recorded. In most cases this will match the initial target language, but the user can change the target language manually if they want. Note that the semantic_type attribute is included in order to remain consistent with the previous Translate UKM proto.

metric.name: FirstTranslateError
metric.summary: The first error to occur within Translate for this page load.

metric.name: FirstUIInteraction
metric.summary: The user's first interaction with the Translate UI.

metric.name: HTMLContentLanguage
metric.summary: The HTML language content attribute specified by the document. Note that the semantic_type attribute is included in order to remain consistent with the previous Translate UKM proto.

metric.name: HTMLDocumentLanguage
metric.summary: The HTML language attribute specified by the document. Note that the semantic_type attribute is included in order to remain consistent with the previous Translate UKM proto.

metric.name: InitialSourceLanguage
metric.summary: The initial source language that Translate determines for the page. Note that the semantic_type attribute is included in order to remain consistent with the previous Translate UKM proto.

metric.name: InitialSourceLanguageInContentLanguages
metric.summary: Whether or not the initial source language is in the user's never translate language list. By default the UI language, languages the user has translated to, and languages added to the content languages are on the never tranlsate list. However, users can add or remove languages manually as well.

metric.name: InitialState
metric.summary: At the beginning of the page load, the state of Translate. This includes whether the page is translated or not and if no UI is shown, just the omnibox icon is shown, or the full translate UI is shown.

metric.name: InitialTargetLanguage
metric.summary: The initial target language that Translate thinks it should translate to. Note that the semantic_type attribute is included in order to remain consistent with the previous Translate UKM proto.

metric.name: MaxTimeToTranslate
metric.summary: Across all translations during this page load, the maximum amount of time (in milliseconds) it took to translate the page.

metric.name: ModelDetectedLanguage
metric.summary: The language that was estimated by the language model based on the page's content. Note that the semantic_type attribute is included in order to remain consistent with the previous Translate UKM proto.

metric.name: ModelDetectionReliabilityScore
metric.summary: The score, or confidence, of the language detection model's prediction about the language of the page content. The score is between 0 and 100, higher means more confident.

metric.name: NumReversions
metric.summary: The number of times a translation was reverted.

metric.name: NumTargetLanguageChanges
metric.summary: The number of times that the target language was changed by the user over the course of this page load.

metric.name: NumTranslateErrors
metric.summary: The number of errors to occur within Translate for this page load.

metric.name: NumTranslations
metric.summary: The number of times the page was translated.

metric.name: NumUIInteractions
metric.summary: The number of times that the user interacts with the Translate UI.

metric.name: RankerDecision
metric.summary: Decision of the Ranker whether to show the UI or not.

metric.name: RankerVersion
metric.summary: Version of Ranker used for this page load.

metric.name: SequenceNumber
metric.summary: In case multiple events are logged for one page load, we track the sequence in the order each event is logged.

metric.name: TotalTimeNotTranslated
metric.summary: The amount of time (in seconds) this page was in the foreground and not translated.

metric.name: TotalTimeTranslated
metric.summary: The amount of time (in seconds) this page was in the foreground and translated.

metric.name: TriggerDecision
metric.summary: The highest priority trigger that determined the initial state of Translate for this page load.

metric.name: WasContentEmpty
metric.summary: Whether the page content used to detect the page language was empty or not.

event.name: TrustedWebActivity.Open
event.summary: Collected when a page is opened in a Trusted Web Activity.

metric.name: HasOccurred
metric.summary: A boolean signaling that the event has occurred (typically only records true values).

event.name: Unload
event.summary: Metrics associated with a unloading in preparation for a navigation.

metric.name: BeforeUnloadDuration
metric.summary: Measures the time in milliseconds of running the beforeunload handlers for a page. If the beforeunloads are dispatched from the browser, this reports the longest running one since these handlers will be run in parallel when the page has frames in different processes. If the navigation is renderer initiated, it will be the duration of running beforeunload handlers in the navigating frame and its local descendants in the renderer plus any latency caused by dispatching beforeunloads to remote frames from the browser.

metric.name: BeforeUnloadQueueingDuration
metric.summary: Measures the time in milliseconds from when the browser sends the request to run beforeunload to the renderer to when the renderer begins dispatching the handlers. For browser-initiated navigations, we report the queueing duration of the beforeunloads that took the longest since they are dispatched in parallel to remote and local frames. For renderer- initiated navigations this metric will only be filled in if we dispatched beforeunloads from the browser to remote frames which we had to wait on.

metric.name: BeforeUnloadToCommit.CrossProcess
metric.summary: Measures the time in milliseconds from when the browser sends the request to dispatch before unload handlers (for browser-intitated navigations) or from when the renderer started running before unload handlers (for renderer-initiated navigations) to when the commit navigation was sent to the renderer process. For cross-process navigations this represents the total amount of time the navigation is blocked since it was initiated.

metric.name: BeforeUnloadToCommit.SameProcess
metric.summary: Measures the time in milliseconds from when the browser sends the request to dispatch before unload handlers (for browser-intitated navigations) or from when the renderer started running before unload handlers (for renderer-initiated navigations) to when the navigation was committed. For same-process navigations this represents the total amount of time the navigation is blocked since it was initiated.

metric.name: UnloadDuration
metric.summary: Measures the time in milliseconds to run the unload handlers in the old document where the navigation was committed. Only records for same-process navigations since there are no unload handlers to be run in the new process during a cross-process navigation.

metric.name: UnloadQueueingDuration
metric.summary: Measures the time in milliseconds from when the browser sends the commit navigation request to the renderer to when the renderer dispatches unload events. Only records for same-process navigations since unload handlers are run in a different process for cross-process navigations.

event.name: UserActivity
event.summary: Collects user activity events after staying idle for a short period of time.

metric.name: BatteryPercent
metric.summary: Percentage of battery, bucketed to 5 percents, i.e. [0, 5%) is mapped to 0, [5%, 10%) is mapped to 5 etc.

metric.name: DeviceManagement
metric.summary: An enum representing whether the device is managed, defined in |ash::power::ml::UserActivityEvent::Features::ManagementType|.

metric.name: DeviceMode
metric.summary: An enum representing the mode of the device, defined in |ash::power::ml::UserActivityEvent::Features::DeviceMode|

metric.name: DeviceType
metric.summary: An enum representing the type of the device, defined in |ash::power::ml::UserActivityEvent::Features::DeviceType|

metric.name: EventLogDuration
metric.summary: Time taken in seconds from when we start logging the features to the moment when the event type is known and we finish logging the complete event.

metric.name: EventReason
metric.summary: An enum representing the reason of the event, defined in |ash::power::ml::UserActivityEvent::Event::Reason|.

metric.name: EventType
metric.summary: An enum representing the type of the event, defined in |ash::power::ml::UserActivityEvent::Event::Type|.

metric.name: KeyEventsInLastHour
metric.summary: The number of key events reported as user activity in the last hour. This is bucketized so that it is recorded precisely when it is in [0, 99], rounded to nearest 100 when it is in [100, 999] and rounded to nearest 1000 when it is in [1000, 9999]. It is capped at 10000.

metric.name: LastActivityDay
metric.summary: An enum representing the last activity day of the week, defined in |ash::power::ml::UserActivityEvent::Features::DayOfWeek|.

metric.name: LastActivityTime
metric.summary: Last activity time as hours since midnight in the local time zone.

metric.name: LastUserActivityTime
metric.summary: Last user activity time as hours since midnight in the local time zone.

metric.name: ModelApplied
metric.summary: A boolean value whether model prediction is applied in the screen-dim decision. False means powerd dims the screen regardless of the model prediction.

metric.name: ModelDecisionThreshold
metric.summary: A threhold in the range of [0, 100]. Higher threshold makes screen-dim less likely.

metric.name: ModelInactivityScore
metric.summary: Probability that user will remain inactive as predicted by the model. It is in the range of [0, 100]. If score is less than the decision threshold, dim will be deferred.

metric.name: ModelResponse
metric.summary: Whether the model recommends the screen dim should be deferred (NO_DIM) or allowed (DIM), or encountered an error (MODEL_ERROR).

metric.name: MouseEventsInLastHour
metric.summary: The number of mouse events reported as user activity in the last hour. This is bucketized so that it is recorded precisely when it is in [0, 99], rounded to nearest 100 when it is in [100, 999] and rounded to nearest 1000 when it is in [1000, 9999]. It is capped at 10000.

metric.name: OnBattery
metric.summary: Boolean value to represent whether the device is currently on battery power.

metric.name: PreviousNegativeActionsCount
metric.summary: Number of times user reactivated, not including the current event.

metric.name: PreviousPositiveActionsCount
metric.summary: Number of times user has remained idle, not including the current event.

metric.name: RecentTimeActive
metric.summary: Duration of activity in seconds up to last activity.

metric.name: RecentVideoPlayingTime
metric.summary: Recent video playing time in seconds.

metric.name: ScreenDimDelay
metric.summary: Delay after which the screen will be dimmed in seconds.

metric.name: ScreenDimmedInitially
metric.summary: Whether screen was dimmed at the start of the event.

metric.name: ScreenDimOccurred
metric.summary: Whether screen dim occurred due to inactivity timeout.

metric.name: ScreenDimToOffDelay
metric.summary: Delay after which the screen will be turned off in seconds, as measured from screen is dimmed. If dim is disabled, it is the delay from the start of inactivity.

metric.name: ScreenLockedInitially
metric.summary: Whether screen was locked at the start of the event.

metric.name: ScreenLockOccurred
metric.summary: Whether screen lock occurred.

metric.name: ScreenOffInitially
metric.summary: Whether screen was off at the start of the event.

metric.name: ScreenOffOccurred
metric.summary: Whether screen off occurred due to inactivity timeout.

metric.name: SequenceId
metric.summary: The ID of this event in the current session.

metric.name: TimeSinceLastKey
metric.summary: Time from last key event in seconds.

metric.name: TimeSinceLastMouse
metric.summary: Time from last mouse event in seconds.

metric.name: TimeSinceLastTouch
metric.summary: Time from last touch event in seconds.

metric.name: TimeSinceLastVideoEnded
metric.summary: Time from the end of the last video playing in seconds.

metric.name: TouchEventsInLastHour
metric.summary: The number of touch events reported as user activity in the last hour. This is bucketized so that it is recorded precisely when it is in [0, 99], rounded to nearest 100 when it is in [100, 999] and rounded to nearest 1000 when it is in [1000, 9999]. It is capped at 10000.

event.name: UserActivityId
event.summary: Records an activity that happened while a page was open.

metric.name: ActivityId
metric.summary: The ID of the activity that happened while a page was open.

metric.name: ContentType
metric.summary: Enumeration for the MIME type of the page. Values are enumerated in metrics::TabMetricsEvent::ContentType.

metric.name: HasFormEntry
metric.summary: Boolean value indicating whether the page has any user-input text.

metric.name: IsActive
metric.summary: Boolean value representing whether this tab is selected in its containing browser.

metric.name: IsBrowserFocused
metric.summary: Boolean value representing whether the containing browser is in focus.

metric.name: IsBrowserVisible
metric.summary: Boolean value representing whether the containing browser is visible.

metric.name: IsTopmostBrowser
metric.summary: Boolean value representing whether the containing browser is the topmost one on the screen.

metric.name: SiteEngagementScore
metric.summary: Site engagement score in the range [0, 100], rounded down to a multiple of 10 to limit granularity.

event.name: UserPerceivedPageVisit
event.summary: Metrics related to a page visit as a user perceives it, regardless of how the page was visited. More metrics may be associated with a particular page visit, these can be found in specific UKM events with the same UKM source ID. Currently the methods of visiting a page that will populate this event are: 'normal' page load (more metrics in the PageLoad event), Back-Forward Cache Restore (more metrics in the HistoryNavigation event), and AMP documents (more metrics in the AmpPageLoad event)

metric.name: NotCountedForCoreWebVitals
metric.summary: Determines if this page visit event should be counted towards calculations involving Core Web Vitals. If true, this page visit event should not be counted. If false or NULL, the page visit event should be considered. Currently this is determined by checking the mime-type of the page visit event.

metric.name: PageVisitFinalStatus
metric.summary: Determines the final status for this page visit. That is, records whether the page was never foregrounded, was foregrounded but did not reach FCP, or did reach FCP. The metric is recorded at the end of each page visit. App backgrounding on Android to is considered to be the end of page visit as well, as the app could be evicted by Android anytime after that happens.

metric.name: UserInitiated
metric.summary: True if this page visit was initiated by user input. For renderer navigations, only true if the code can trace back to the original input event - there are cases where this known not to be possible.

event.name: V8.Wasm.ModuleCompiled
event.summary: Records information on baseline compilation of WebAssembly modules and its resource consumption.

metric.name: Async
metric.summary: The compilation was running asynchronously.

metric.name: Cached
metric.summary: The compilation was re-using already cached native module code instead of JIT compiling it.

metric.name: CodeSize
metric.summary: The size of the native module code in bytes after baseline compilation finished.

metric.name: Deserialized
metric.summary: The compilation was deserializing the native module code from the disk cache instead of JIT compiling it.

metric.name: Lazy
metric.summary: The lazy compilation flag was enabled and therefore not all functions might have been compiled.

metric.name: LiftoffBailoutCount
metric.summary: The number of functions that Liftoff could not compile and that therefore needed to be compiled by TurboFan.

metric.name: Streamed
metric.summary: The wire bytes were streamed while compiling.

metric.name: Success
metric.summary: The module was successfully compiled.

metric.name: WallClockDuration
metric.summary: The total time the compilation took in microseconds.

event.name: V8.Wasm.ModuleDecoded
event.summary: Records information on WebAssembly modules included in websites.

metric.name: FunctionCount
metric.summary: Number of declared functions in this module.

metric.name: ModuleSize
metric.summary: The size of the wire bytes of the module.

metric.name: Streamed
metric.summary: The wire bytes were streamed while decoding.

metric.name: Success
metric.summary: The module was successfully decoded.

metric.name: WallClockDuration
metric.summary: The total time the decoding took in microseconds.

event.name: V8.Wasm.ModuleInstantiated
event.summary: Records information on instantiation of WebAssembly modules and its resource consumption.

metric.name: ImportedFunctionCount
metric.summary: The number of functions imported by the module.

metric.name: Success
metric.summary: The module was successfully instantiated.

metric.name: WallClockDuration
metric.summary: The total time the instantiation took in microseconds.

event.name: V8.Wasm.ModuleTieredUp
event.summary: Records information on top-tier compilation of WebAssembly modules and its resource consumption.

metric.name: CodeSize
metric.summary: The size of the native module code in bytes after top-tier compilation finished.

metric.name: Lazy
metric.summary: The lazy compilation flag was enabled and therefore not all functions might have been compiled.

metric.name: WallClockDuration
metric.summary: The total time the compilation took in microseconds.

event.name: V8CompileHints.Version5
event.skip_proto_reason: Too many metrics causing scalability issue b/275678348
event.summary: Data about which JavaScript functions are compiled. The collected data is obfuscated to not reveal script URLs. The data is split into separate int64s.

event.name: VideoConferencingEvent
event.summary: Records videoconferencing usage on web apps. Only records for apps which have captured camera, microphone, or screen at least once while open.

metric.name: CameraCaptureDuration
metric.summary: The total time duration (bucketed) the VC app spent capturing camera. Duration in units of ms.

metric.name: DidCaptureCamera
metric.summary: App captured camera.

metric.name: DidCaptureMicrophone
metric.summary: App captured microphone.

metric.name: DidCaptureScreen
metric.summary: App captured screen.

metric.name: MicrophoneCaptureDuration
metric.summary: The total time duration (bucketed) the VC app spent capturing microphone. Duration in units of ms.

metric.name: ScreenCaptureDuration
metric.summary: The total time duration (bucketed) the VC app spent capturing the screen. Duration in units of ms.

metric.name: TotalDuration
metric.summary: Duration (bucketed) for which the VC app was open. Duration in units of ms.

event.name: VirtualKeyboard.Open
event.summary: Collects the used input types with the Virtual Keyboard.

metric.name: TextInputType
metric.summary: Type of a input field which the user is trying to use the Virtual Keyboard on.

event.name: Wallet.BoardingPassDetect
event.summary: Records boarding pass barcode detection results.

metric.name: Detected
metric.summary: Whether a boarding pass barocode is detected.

event.name: WebAPK.Install
event.summary: WebAPK usage metrics recorded on Install.

metric.name: DisplayMode
metric.summary: Records the display mode of the added WebAPK.

metric.name: Distributor
metric.summary: Distribution source of the WebAPK (browser, device policy, etc).

metric.name: Install
metric.summary: Recorded when this WebAPK is installed.

metric.name: InstallSource
metric.summary: From where the user initiates the install: app menu, install API, etc.

event.name: WebAPK.SessionEnd
event.summary: WebAPK usage metrics recorded when the WebAPK is backgrounded.

metric.name: AppVersion
metric.summary: WebAPK app version that the user has installed.

metric.name: Distributor
metric.summary: Distribution source of the WebAPK (browser, device policy, etc).

metric.name: SessionDuration
metric.summary: How long the user spent inside the WebAPK, in milliseconds. This will use exponential bucketing (ie, count in 10s of milliseconds at first, then 100s, then seconds, 10s of seconds, minutes, 10 minutes, hours, 10 hours, days, weeks, years.)

event.name: WebAPK.Uninstall
event.summary: WebAPK usage metrics recorded on Uninstall.

metric.name: AppVersion
metric.summary: WebAPK app version that the user has installed.

metric.name: Distributor
metric.summary: Distribution source of the WebAPK (browser, device policy, etc).

metric.name: InstalledDuration
metric.summary: How long between Install and Uninstall of a particular WebAPK, in seconds. This will use exponential bucketing (ie, count in seconds at first, then 10s of seconds, minutes, 10 minutes, hours, 10 hours, days, weeks, years.)

metric.name: LifetimeLaunches
metric.summary: Records how many times the WebAPK was launched between install and uninstall (in coarse buckets).

metric.name: Uninstall
metric.summary: Recorded when this WebAPK is uninstalled.

event.name: WebAPK.Visit
event.summary: WebAPK usage metrics recorded during a user's interaction with the site.

metric.name: AppVersion
metric.summary: WebAPK app version that the user has installed.

metric.name: Distributor
metric.summary: Distribution source of the WebAPK (browser, device policy, etc).

metric.name: Launch
metric.summary: Recorded every time this WebAPK is launched.

metric.name: LaunchSource
metric.summary: Launch source of the WebAPK.

event.name: Webapp.AddToHomeScreen
event.summary: A2HS usage metrics recorded when users started the add to home screen flow on Android.

metric.name: DisplayMode
metric.summary: Records the display mode of the added Webapp.

metric.name: SameAsManifestStartUrl
metric.summary: Record whether `start_url` for the Shortcuts is the same as the current page url.

metric.name: ShortcutReason
metric.summary: Record an StatusCode for why this site is not WebAPK compatible.

event.name: WebApp.DailyInteraction
event.summary: Features of a web app recorded at most once per day if a user interacted with the web app. Recorded for installed and/or promotable (installable) web apps unless noted otherwise on the metric. Keyed by start_url of the app. Note: Changed in Feb 2023 to only record when App sync is enabled.

metric.name: BackgroundDuration
metric.summary: Records the total duration (seconds, bucketed) that the app was open in the background in the last calendar day. Summed during the day and emitted if the date changes. Recorded only for installed web apps that are currently open in a web app window, and all preinstalled web apps. In m128 a bug is fixed where on resuming from sleep background time did not resume counting.

metric.name: CapturesLinks
metric.summary: Whether the user has enabled link capturing to open in-scope links in the app window. Recorded only for installed web apps that are currently open in a web app window, and all preinstalled web apps.

metric.name: DisplayMode
metric.summary: Effective display mode of the web app (may be set by a manifest and manifest may be overridden by the user).

metric.name: ForegroundDuration
metric.summary: Records the total duration (seconds, bucketed) that the app was open in the foreground in the last calendar day. Summed during the day and emitted if the date changes. Recorded only for installed web apps that are currently open in a web app window, and all preinstalled web apps. As of m128, this time is cut short if Chrome determines that it is not active anymore (see the DesktopSessionDurationTracker). This is to keep the cumulative sum of this value comparable with Session.TotalDuration.

metric.name: Installed
metric.summary: Whether the web app is installed.

metric.name: InstallSource
metric.summary: Install source of the web app (omnibox, menu item, policy, etc).

metric.name: NumSessions
metric.summary: Records the total number of times the app was focused in the foreground in the last calendar day. Recorded only for installed web apps that are currently open in a web app window, and all preinstalled web apps.

metric.name: Promotable
metric.summary: Whether the web app is currently considered 'promotable' (passes Chrome checks to be considered an installable web app).

metric.name: Used
